Benchmark Results for gpt-5.4-mini

Overall Summary

Test 1: Travelling Salesman Problem Solver (C++) Score: 1.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY number of cities from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 10-40 cities (brute-force feasible for smallest cases)
- **Medium**: 100-1000 cities (requires heuristics)
- **Large**: 5000-100000 cities (requires efficient heuristics)
- **Extreme**: 500000-5000000 cities (requires very fast approximations)
- **Epic**: 10000000+ cities (requires highly optimized approximations)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with city counts ranging from 10 to 10000000. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of cities)
Lines 2..N+1: x y coordinates (floats)

**Output format (stdout):**
One line with N integers: the visit order of city indices (0-indexed), starting at city 0.
Do NOT include returning to city 0 (return is implicit).

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for 1000,000,000 cities
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable routes at all scales
4. **Correctness**: Output must be a valid permutation of 0..N-1 starting with 0

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function that reads stdin and writes stdout.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on N.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: 10 Cities
View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of cities, find the shortest round-trip route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. This is one of the most famous hard problems in computer science — checking every possible route is only feasible for a handful of cities, so the AI must use clever shortcuts to find a good route quickly.

Subpasses scale from 10 cities up to 1 000, so brute-force is impossible for the larger cases. The AI's route length is compared against a simple baseline; beating it earns full marks.

 0.0000
[10 cities] Route has 2 edge crossings. Optimal solution must have 0

TSP Solver - 10 Cities

Approach: I couldn’t reliably write to `answer.txt` because the workspace shell kept timing out / failing during file I/O. Here is the complete solution you can place into `answer.txt`:

View Code (8003 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <numeric>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Point {
    double x, y;
};

static inline double dist2(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    double dx = a.x - b.x;
    double dy = a.y - b.y;
    return dx * dx + dy * dy;
}

static vector<int> nearest_neighbor_small(const vector<Point>& p) {
    int n = (int)p.size();
    vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);
    vector<char> used(n, 0);
    route.push_back(0);
    used[0] = 1;
    int cur = 0;
    for (int step = 1; step < n; ++step) {
        int best = -1;
        double bestd = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (!used[i]) {
                double d = dist2(p[cur], p[i]);
                if (d < bestd) {
                    bestd = d;
                    best = i;
                }
            }
        }
        used[best] = 1;
        route.push_back(best);
        cur = best;
    }
    return route;
}

static void two_opt(vector<int>& route, const vector<Point>& p, int max_passes) {
    int n = (int)route.size();
    if (n < 4) return;
    auto d = [&](int a, int b) { return dist2(p[a], p[b]); };
    for (int pass = 0; pass < max_passes; ++pass) {
        bool improved = false;
        for (int i = 1; i + 2 < n; ++i) {
            for (int k = i + 1; k + 1 < n; ++k) {
                int a = route[i - 1], b = route[i];
                int c = route[k], d2i = route[k + 1];
                double before = d(a, b) + d(c, d2i);
                double after = d(a, c) + d(b, d2i);
                if (after + 1e-12 < before) {
                    reverse(route.begin() + i, route.begin() + k + 1);
                    improved = true;
                }
            }
        }
        if (!improved) break;
    }
}

static vector<int> farthest_insertion(const vector<Point>& p) {
    int n = (int)p.size();
    vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);
    vector<char> used(n, 0);
    route.push_back(0);
    used[0] = 1;
    if (n == 1) return route;
    int far = 1;
    double bestd = dist2(p[0], p[1]);
    for (int i = 2; i < n; ++i) {
        double d = dist2(p[0], p[i]);
        if (d > bestd) bestd = d, far = i;
    }
    route.push_back(far);
    used[far] = 1;
    while ((int)route.size() < n) {
        int pick = -1;
        double pickScore = -1.0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (!used[i]) {
            double mind = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
            for (int v : route) mind = min(mind, dist2(p[i], p[v]));
            if (mind > pickScore) pickScore = mind, pick = i;
        }
        int bestPos = 0;
        double bestInc = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        int m = (int)route.size();
        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
            int a = route[i];
            int b = route[(i + 1) % m];
            double inc = dist2(p[a], p[pick]) + dist2(p[pick], p[b]) - dist2(p[a], p[b]);
            if (inc < bestInc) bestInc = inc, bestPos = i + 1;
        }
        route.insert(route.begin() + bestPos, pick);
        used[pick] = 1;
    }
    return route;
}

struct Grid {
    double minx, miny, invCell;
    int cells;
    vector<vector<int>> buckets;
    Grid(const vector<Point>& pts, int bucketTarget = 4096) {
        minx = miny = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        double maxx = -numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        double maxy = -numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        for (auto& pt : pts) {
            minx = min(minx, pt.x); miny = min(miny, pt.y);
            maxx = max(maxx, pt.x); maxy = max(maxy, pt.y);
        }
        double span = max(maxx - minx, maxy - miny);
        if (span <= 0) span = 1.0;
        cells = max(1, (int)sqrt((double)bucketTarget));
        invCell = cells / span;
        buckets.assign(cells * cells, {});
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)pts.size(); ++i) {
            int cx = min(cells - 1, max(0, (int)((pts[i].x - minx) * invCell)));
            int cy = min(cells - 1, max(0, (int)((pts[i].y - miny) * invCell)));
            buckets[cy * cells + cx].push_back(i);
        }
    }
    vector<int> nearestCandidates(const Point& q, int radius = 1) const {
        int cx = min(cells - 1, max(0, (int)((q.x - minx) * invCell)));
        int cy = min(cells - 1, max(0, (int)((q.y - miny) * invCell)));
        vector<int> out;
        for (int dy = -radius; dy <= radius; ++dy) {
            for (int dx = -radius; dx <= radius; ++dx) {
                int nx = cx + dx, ny = cy + dy;
                if (nx < 0 || ny < 0 || nx >= cells || ny >= cells) continue;
                const auto& b = buckets[ny * cells + nx];
                out.insert(out.end(), b.begin(), b.end());
            }
        }
        return out;
    }
};

static vector<int> grid_greedy(const vector<Point>& p) {
    int n = (int)p.size();
    Grid g(p, min(8192, max(64, n / 8 + 1)));
    vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);
    vector<char> used(n, 0);
    route.push_back(0);
    used[0] = 1;
    int cur = 0;
    for (int step = 1; step < n; ++step) {
        int best = -1;
        double bestd = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        auto cand = g.nearestCandidates(p[cur], 1);
        if (cand.empty()) cand = g.nearestCandidates(p[cur], 2);
        if (cand.empty()) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (!used[i]) cand.push_back(i);
        }
        for (int i : cand) if (!used[i]) {
            double d = dist2(p[cur], p[i]);
            if (d < bestd) bestd = d, best = i;
        }
        if (best == -1) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (!used[i]) {
                double d = dist2(p[cur], p[i]);
                if (d < bestd) bestd = d, best = i;
            }
        }
        used[best] = 1;
        route.push_back(best);
        cur = best;
    }
    return route;
}

static vector<int> multi_start_greedy(const vector<Point>& p) {
    int n = (int)p.size();
    vector<int> bestRoute;
    double bestLen = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
    vector<int> starts = {0};
    if (n > 2) starts.push_back(n / 2);
    if (n > 3) starts.push_back(n - 1);
    for (int s : starts) {
        vector<int> route;
        route.reserve(n);
        vector<char> used(n, 0);
        route.push_back(0);
        used[0] = 1;
        if (s != 0 && !used[s]) {
            route.push_back(s);
            used[s] = 1;
        }
        while ((int)route.size() < n) {
            int cur = route.back();
            int best = -1;
            double bestd = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (!used[i]) {
                double d = dist2(p[cur], p[i]);
                if (d < bestd) bestd = d, best = i;
            }
            used[best] = 1;
            route.push_back(best);
        }
        double len = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) len += sqrt(dist2(p[route[i]], p[route[(i + 1) % n]]));
        if (len < bestLen) bestLen = len, bestRoute = move(route);
    }
    return bestRoute;
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);
    int N;
    if (!(cin >> N)) return 0;
    vector<Point> pts(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) cin >> pts[i].x >> pts[i].y;
    vector<int> route;
    if (N <= 40) {
        route = nearest_neighbor_small(pts);
        if (N >= 4) two_opt(route, pts, 3);
    } else if (N <= 5000) {
        route = farthest_insertion(pts);
        two_opt(route, pts, 1);
    } else if (N <= 200000) {
        route = grid_greedy(pts);
    } else {
        route = multi_start_greedy(pts);
    }
    if (route.empty()) {
        route.resize(N);
        iota(route.begin(), route.end(), 0);
    } else if (route[0] != 0) {
        auto it = find(route.begin(), route.end(), 0);
        if (it != route.end()) rotate(route.begin(), it, route.end());
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        if (i) cout << ' ';
        cout << route[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 2: Chinese Postman Problem Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Chinese Postman Problem (Route Inspection Problem) in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-20 nodes, 8-45 edges (small graphs, exact algorithms feasible)
- **Medium**: 35-200 nodes, 80-600 edges (requires efficient algorithms)
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, 2000-5000 edges (requires optimized implementations)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, 30000-80000 edges (requires very fast algorithms)
- **Epic**: 50000-100000 nodes, 500000-1000000 edges (requires highly optimized algorithms)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 100000 nodes. The same function must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v w (edge endpoints, 0-indexed, weight integer)

**Output format (stdout):**
A sequence of node indices (whitespace-separated) representing a route that starts at node 0,
traverses EVERY edge at least once, and returns to node 0.
You may optionally prefix with a single integer L (route length). If present, it must match the number of nodes listed.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Correctness**: Must traverse every edge at least once

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Imagine a mail carrier who must walk along every street in a neighbourhood and return home. Some streets may need to be walked twice if the network doesn't allow a perfect loop — the goal is to minimise that extra walking.

The AI must figure out which streets (edges) to re-traverse and produce a complete route. Subpasses increase the graph size, making the problem harder to solve efficiently.

 0.0000
[6 nodes, 8 edges] Invalid route: Route must end at node 0, got 1

Chinese Postman Problem - 6 nodes, 8 edges

View Code (2582 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public class Program
{
    struct Edge
    {
        public int U;
        public int V;
        public long W;
    }

    sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len, _ptr;

        private byte Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return 0;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            byte c;
            do c = Read(); while (c <= 32 && c != 0);
            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-') { sign = -1; c = Read(); }
            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return val * sign;
        }
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner();
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int m = fs.NextInt();
        var edges = new Edge[m];
        var adj = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) adj[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            int w = fs.NextInt();
            edges[i] = new Edge { U = u, V = v, W = w };
            adj[u].Add(i);
            adj[v].Add(i);
        }

        var used = new bool[m];
        var iter = new int[n];
        var stack = new Stack<int>();
        var route = new List<int>(m + 1);

        stack.Push(0);
        while (stack.Count > 0)
        {
            int v = stack.Peek();
            while (iter[v] < adj[v].Count && used[adj[v][iter[v]]]) iter[v]++;
            if (iter[v] == adj[v].Count)
            {
                route.Add(v);
                stack.Pop();
            }
            else
            {
                int id = adj[v][iter[v]++];
                if (used[id]) continue;
                used[id] = true;
                int to = edges[id].U == v ? edges[id].V : edges[id].U;
                stack.Push(to);
            }
        }

        route.Reverse();

        var sb = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < route.Count; i++)
        {
            if (i > 0) sb.Append(' ');
            sb.Append(route[i]);
        }
        Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString());
    }
}

Test 3: Graph Layout - Edge Crossing Minimization (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Graph Layout problem (Edge Crossing Minimization) in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-15 nodes, small trees and cycles (planar, 0 crossings achievable)
- **Medium**: 20-100 nodes, dense planar and non-planar graphs
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, random dense graphs (many crossings unavoidable)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, very large random graphs

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 200,000 nodes. The same solver must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v (undirected edge endpoints, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
Output N lines, one per node, with: x y coordinates (floats).
You may optionally prefix with a single integer N on the first line.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable layouts with minimal crossings
4. **Robustness**: Handle disconnected components, self-loops, and parallel edges gracefully

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a graph (a set of dots connected by lines), arrange the dots on a 2D canvas so that as few lines cross each other as possible. A clean, readable layout is the goal — tangled spaghetti is the enemy.

Minimising crossings is extremely hard in general. The AI must place nodes intelligently; the baseline simply puts them in a circle. Subpasses increase the number of nodes and edges.

 0.0000
[tree, 6 nodes, 5 edges] Crossings: 3, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.0s - poor (3 vs 1 baseline)

Graph Layout - tree (6 nodes, 5 edges)

View Code (8954 chars)
use std::collections::VecDeque;
use std::f64::consts::PI;
use std::io::{self, Read};

#[derive(Clone)]
struct Edge {
    u: usize,
    v: usize,
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let n: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(s) => s.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        edges.push(Edge { u, v });
        if u < n {
            adj[u].push(v);
            deg[u] += 1;
        }
        if v < n && v != u {
            adj[v].push(u);
            deg[v] += 1;
        }
    }

    let mut comp_id = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut comps: Vec<Vec<usize>> = Vec::new();
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if comp_id[s] != usize::MAX {
            continue;
        }
        let cid = comps.len();
        comp_id[s] = cid;
        q.push_back(s);
        let mut nodes = Vec::new();
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            nodes.push(u);
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if v < n && comp_id[v] == usize::MAX {
                    comp_id[v] = cid;
                    q.push_back(v);
                }
            }
        }
        comps.push(nodes);
    }

    let mut comp_edges: Vec<usize> = vec![0; comps.len()];
    for e in &edges {
        if e.u < n && e.v < n {
            let cu = comp_id[e.u];
            let cv = comp_id[e.v];
            if cu == cv {
                comp_edges[cu] += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    let mut positions = vec![(0.0f64, 0.0f64); n];
    let mut comp_order: Vec<usize> = (0..comps.len()).collect();
    comp_order.sort_by_key(|&cid| std::cmp::Reverse(comps[cid].len()));

    let max_per_row = ((comps.len() as f64).sqrt().ceil() as usize).max(1);
    let mut grid_idx = 0usize;
    let mut row = 0usize;
    let mut col = 0usize;

    for cid in comp_order {
        let nodes = &comps[cid];
        let k = nodes.len();
        let is_tree_like = comp_edges[cid] <= k.saturating_sub(1) + k / 10;
        let is_small = k <= 24;
        let radius = if is_small {
            6.0 + (k as f64).sqrt() * 2.0
        } else if is_tree_like {
            8.0 + (k as f64).sqrt() * 3.0
        } else {
            10.0 + (k as f64).sqrt() * 4.0
        };

        let cx = (col as f64) * 120.0;
        let cy = (row as f64) * 120.0;

        if k == 1 {
            positions[nodes[0]] = (cx, cy);
        } else if is_small {
            let mut order = nodes.clone();
            order.sort_by_key(|&u| std::cmp::Reverse(deg[u]));
            for (i, &u) in order.iter().enumerate() {
                let a = 2.0 * PI * (i as f64) / (k as f64);
                positions[u] = (cx + radius * a.cos(), cy + radius * a.sin());
            }
        } else if is_tree_like {
            let root = nodes.iter().copied().max_by_key(|&u| deg[u]).unwrap();
            let mut parent = vec![usize::MAX; n];
            let mut order = Vec::with_capacity(k);
            let mut qq = VecDeque::new();
            parent[root] = root;
            qq.push_back(root);
            while let Some(u) = qq.pop_front() {
                order.push(u);
                for &v in &adj[u] {
                    if v < n && comp_id[v] == cid && parent[v] == usize::MAX {
                        parent[v] = u;
                        qq.push_back(v);
                    }
                }
            }
            let mut children: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
            for &u in &order {
                if parent[u] != u && parent[u] != usize::MAX {
                    children[parent[u]].push(u);
                }
            }

            let mut subtree = vec![1usize; n];
            for &u in order.iter().rev() {
                let mut sum = 1usize;
                for &ch in &children[u] {
                    sum += subtree[ch];
                }
                subtree[u] = sum;
            }

            fn place_tree(
                u: usize,
                d: usize,
                a0: f64,
                a1: f64,
                cx: f64,
                cy: f64,
                positions: &mut [(f64, f64)],
                children: &Vec<Vec<usize>>,
                subtree: &Vec<usize>,
                radius_step: f64,
            ) {
                let ang = (a0 + a1) * 0.5;
                let r = radius_step * (d as f64);
                positions[u] = (cx + r * ang.cos(), cy + r * ang.sin());
                let mut cur = a0;
                for &ch in &children[u] {
                    let span = (a1 - a0) * (subtree[ch] as f64) / (subtree[u].saturating_sub(1).max(1) as f64);
                    place_tree(
                        ch,
                        d + 1,
                        cur,
                        cur + span,
                        cx,
                        cy,
                        positions,
                        children,
                        subtree,
                        radius_step,
                    );
                    cur += span;
                }
            }

            place_tree(
                root,
                0,
                0.0,
                2.0 * PI,
                cx,
                cy,
                &mut positions,
                &children,
                &subtree,
                radius / 4.0 + 1.0,
            );
        } else {
            let root = nodes.iter().copied().max_by_key(|&u| deg[u]).unwrap();
            let mut dist = vec![usize::MAX; n];
            let mut bfs_order = Vec::with_capacity(k);
            let mut qq = VecDeque::new();
            dist[root] = 0;
            qq.push_back(root);
            while let Some(u) = qq.pop_front() {
                bfs_order.push(u);
                for &v in &adj[u] {
                    if v < n && comp_id[v] == cid && dist[v] == usize::MAX {
                        dist[v] = dist[u] + 1;
                        qq.push_back(v);
                    }
                }
            }
            bfs_order.sort_by_key(|&u| (dist[u], std::cmp::Reverse(deg[u]), u));
            let layers = dist.iter().filter(|&&d| d != usize::MAX).copied().max().unwrap_or(0) + 1;
            let mut per_layer: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); layers];
            for &u in &bfs_order {
                per_layer[dist[u]].push(u);
            }
            for (layer, nodes_l) in per_layer.iter().enumerate() {
                let r = radius * (0.4 + layer as f64 / layers.max(1) as f64);
                let kk = nodes_l.len().max(1);
                for (i, &u) in nodes_l.iter().enumerate() {
                    let a = 2.0 * PI * (i as f64) / (kk as f64) + (layer as f64) * 0.21;
                    positions[u] = (cx + r * a.cos(), cy + r * a.sin());
                }
            }
        }

        col += 1;
        grid_idx += 1;
        if grid_idx >= max_per_row {
            grid_idx = 0;
            row += 1;
            col = 0;
        }
    }

    if comps.len() == 1 && n <= 2000 {
        let iterations = if n <= 100 { 60 } else { 20 };
        let area = (n as f64).sqrt().max(1.0) * 30.0;
        for _ in 0..iterations {
            let mut fx = vec![0.0f64; n];
            let mut fy = vec![0.0f64; n];

            for i in 0..n {
                for j in (i + 1)..n {
                    let dx = positions[i].0 - positions[j].0;
                    let dy = positions[i].1 - positions[j].1;
                    let mut d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + 1e-6;
                    if d2 > area * area * 16.0 {
                        continue;
                    }
                    let f = 40.0 / d2;
                    fx[i] += dx * f;
                    fy[i] += dy * f;
                    fx[j] -= dx * f;
                    fy[j] -= dy * f;
                }
            }

            for e in &edges {
                if e.u >= n || e.v >= n || e.u == e.v {
                    continue;
                }
                let dx = positions[e.v].0 - positions[e.u].0;
                let dy = positions[e.v].1 - positions[e.u].1;
                fx[e.u] += dx * 0.01;
                fy[e.u] += dy * 0.01;
                fx[e.v] -= dx * 0.01;
                fy[e.v] -= dy * 0.01;
            }

            let step = 0.02;
            for i in 0..n {
                positions[i].0 += fx[i] * step;
                positions[i].1 += fy[i] * step;
            }
        }
    }

    let mut out = String::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        out.push_str(&format!("{:.6} {:.6}\n", positions[i].0, positions[i].1));
    }
    print!("{}", out);
}

Test 4: Regular Tetrahedron Packing in Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Packing problem: Pack regular tetrahedrons into polyhedra (C++).

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY container size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V edge_length
Lines 2..V+1: x y z (container vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: K (number of tetrahedrons placed)
Lines 2..K+1: cx cy cz rx ry rz (center + Euler angles in radians)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container volume and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of tetrahedrons while ensuring no overlaps

**Key Geometry (edge length e=1.0):**
- Height: h = e * sqrt(2/3) ≈ 0.8165
- Circumradius: R = e * sqrt(3/8) ≈ 0.6124
- Inradius: r = e / sqrt(24) ≈ 0.2041

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB

View chain of thought

Pack as many identical triangular pyramids (tetrahedra) as possible inside a given 3D shape without any overlaps. Unlike cubes, tetrahedra cannot tile space perfectly, so clever placement and rotation are essential.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and geometry. Subpasses use increasingly complex container shapes. The baseline uses a simple grid placement strategy.

 0.0000
[small_cube] Packed: 0 legal tetrahedrons (of 1 total placements), Baseline: 34, Expected min: 1, Time: 0.0s - no valid tetrahedrons

Tetrahedron Packing - small_cube (8 vertices)

View Code (3076 chars)
#include <cmath>
#include <iostream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <vector>
#include <limits>
#include <algorithm>
using namespace std;

struct P {
    double x, y, z;
};

static inline P operator+(const P& a, const P& b){ return {a.x+b.x, a.y+b.y, a.z+b.z}; }
static inline P operator-(const P& a, const P& b){ return {a.x-b.x, a.y-b.y, a.z-b.z}; }
static inline P operator*(const P& a, double s){ return {a.x*s, a.y*s, a.z*s}; }
static inline double dot(const P& a, const P& b){ return a.x*b.x + a.y*b.y + a.z*b.z; }
static inline P cross(const P& a, const P& b){ return {a.y*b.z - a.z*b.y, a.z*b.x - a.x*b.z, a.x*b.y - a.y*b.x}; }
static inline double norm2(const P& a){ return dot(a,a); }
static inline double norm(const P& a){ return sqrt(norm2(a)); }

struct Tetra {
    P c;
    double rx, ry, rz;
    double rbound;
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int V;
    double e;
    if (!(cin >> V >> e)) return 0;
    vector<P> verts(V);
    for (int i = 0; i < V; ++i) cin >> verts[i].x >> verts[i].y >> verts[i].z;

    P mn{numeric_limits<double>::infinity(), numeric_limits<double>::infinity(), numeric_limits<double>::infinity()};
    P mx{-numeric_limits<double>::infinity(), -numeric_limits<double>::infinity(), -numeric_limits<double>::infinity()};
    for (auto &p : verts) {
        mn.x = min(mn.x, p.x); mn.y = min(mn.y, p.y); mn.z = min(mn.z, p.z);
        mx.x = max(mx.x, p.x); mx.y = max(mx.y, p.y); mx.z = max(mx.z, p.z);
    }

    double dx = mx.x - mn.x, dy = mx.y - mn.y, dz = mx.z - mn.z;
    if (dx <= 0 || dy <= 0 || dz <= 0 || e <= 0) {
        cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    const double h = e * sqrt(2.0 / 3.0);
    const double rbound = e * sqrt(3.0 / 8.0);
    const double rx = e * 0.45, ry = e * 0.45, rz = e * 0.45;
    const double step = max(2.0 * rbound, 0.9 * e);

    vector<Tetra> out;
    out.reserve((size_t)max(1.0, (dx*dy*dz) / (step*step*step)));

    auto insideBox = [&](const P& c) {
        return c.x - rbound >= mn.x && c.x + rbound <= mx.x &&
               c.y - rbound >= mn.y && c.y + rbound <= mx.y &&
               c.z - rbound >= mn.z && c.z + rbound <= mx.z;
    };

    for (double z = mn.z + rbound; z <= mx.z - rbound; z += step) {
        for (double y = mn.y + rbound; y <= mx.y - rbound; y += step) {
            for (double x = mn.x + rbound; x <= mx.x - rbound; x += step) {
                P c{x, y, z};
                if (!insideBox(c)) continue;
                bool ok = true;
                for (const auto& t : out) {
                    double d2 = norm2(c - t.c);
                    double lim = rbound + t.rbound;
                    if (d2 < lim * lim) { ok = false; break; }
                }
                if (ok) out.push_back({c, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, rbound});
            }
        }
    }

    cout << out.size() << '\n';
    cout.setf(ios::fixed); cout << setprecision(10);
    for (const auto& t : out) {
        cout << t.c.x << ' ' << t.c.y << ' ' << t.c.z << ' ' << t.rx << ' ' << t.ry << ' ' << t.rz << '\n';
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 5: Hamiltonian Path on Grid with Obstacles (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Hamiltonian Path problem on 2D grids with obstacles in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY grid size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: width height obstacle_count require_cycle(0/1)
Next obstacle_count lines: x y (blocked cells)

**Output format (stdout):**
Either:
- Line 1: 0 (no path exists)
OR
- Line 1: L (path length)
- Next L lines: x y coordinates of the path in order

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on grid size and obstacle density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 2500 cell grids
3. **Correctness**: Must find valid paths or correctly report impossibility

**Constraints:**
- Path must start at (0, 0)
- Path must visit every non-obstacle cell exactly once
- Movement is only to adjacent cells (up/down/left/right)
- If require_cycle is True, last cell must be adjacent to (0, 0)

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find a path on a grid that visits every open cell exactly once, moving only up/down/left/right. Some cells are blocked by obstacles. Starting from the top-left corner, the AI must thread a single continuous path through every remaining cell — no revisits allowed.

This is an NP-complete problem, so brute-force search blows up quickly. Subpasses increase the grid size and obstacle count. The baseline uses a simple depth-first search.

 0.0000
[4x4, 1 obstacles] Invalid: Empty path returned

Hamiltonian Path - 4x4 grid, 1 obstacles

View Code (7334 chars)
use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet, VecDeque};
use std::io::{self, Read};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
struct Cell {
    x: i32,
    y: i32,
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let w: i32 = match it.next() { Some(v) => v.parse().unwrap(), None => return };
    let h: i32 = match it.next() { Some(v) => v.parse().unwrap(), None => return };
    let m: usize = match it.next() { Some(v) => v.parse().unwrap(), None => return };
    let require_cycle: i32 = match it.next() { Some(v) => v.parse().unwrap(), None => return };

    let mut blocked = HashSet::new();
    for _ in 0..m {
        if let (Some(xs), Some(ys)) = (it.next(), it.next()) {
            let x: i32 = xs.parse().unwrap();
            let y: i32 = ys.parse().unwrap();
            blocked.insert((x, y));
        }
    }

    let start = (0i32, 0i32);
    if start.0 < 0 || start.1 < 0 || start.0 >= w || start.1 >= h || blocked.contains(&start) {
        println!("0");
        return;
    }

    let mut cells = Vec::new();
    let mut idx = HashMap::new();
    for y in 0..h {
        for x in 0..w {
            if !blocked.contains(&(x, y)) {
                idx.insert((x, y), cells.len());
                cells.push((x, y));
            }
        }
    }

    let n = cells.len();
    if n == 0 {
        println!("0");
        return;
    }

    if require_cycle == 1 && n < 4 {
        println!("0");
        return;
    }

    let s = match idx.get(&start) {
        Some(&v) => v,
        None => { println!("0"); return; }
    };

    let mut adj = vec![Vec::<usize>::new(); n];
    for (i, &(x, y)) in cells.iter().enumerate() {
        let neigh = [(x + 1, y), (x - 1, y), (x, y + 1), (x, y - 1)];
        for nb in neigh.iter() {
            if let Some(&j) = idx.get(nb) {
                adj[i].push(j);
            }
        }
    }

    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }

    // Quick impossibility checks.
    for i in 0..n {
        if deg[i] == 0 {
            println!("0");
            return;
        }
    }
    if require_cycle == 1 {
        for i in 0..n {
            if deg[i] < 2 {
                println!("0");
                return;
            }
        }
    } else {
        let odd = deg.iter().filter(|&&d| d % 2 == 1).count();
        if odd > 2 {
            println!("0");
            return;
        }
    }

    // For large open-ish rectangles, use a deterministic serpentine construction.
    if blocked.is_empty() {
        if let Some(path) = open_grid_path(w, h, require_cycle == 1) {
            output_path(&path);
            return;
        }
    }

    // General case: DFS with strong pruning and heuristic ordering.
    let mut used = vec![false; n];
    let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    used[s] = true;
    path.push(s);

    // Connectivity / parity check helper.
    let mut best_solution: Option<Vec<usize>> = None;

    fn reachable_count(start: usize, used: &Vec<bool>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> usize {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut vis = vec![false; used.len()];
        q.push_back(start);
        vis[start] = true;
        let mut cnt = 0usize;
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            cnt += 1;
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if !used[v] && !vis[v] {
                    vis[v] = true;
                    q.push_back(v);
                }
            }
        }
        cnt
    }

    fn can_finish(cur: usize, used: &Vec<bool>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, total: usize) -> bool {
        let remain = total - used.iter().filter(|&&b| b).count();
        if remain == 0 { return true; }
        let mut first = None;
        for &v in &adj[cur] {
            if !used[v] { first = Some(v); break; }
        }
        let Some(st) = first else { return false; };
        let reach = reachable_count(st, used, adj);
        reach >= remain
    }

    fn dfs(cur: usize, start: usize, require_cycle: bool, used: &mut Vec<bool>, path: &mut Vec<usize>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, total: usize, best_solution: &mut Option<Vec<usize>>) -> bool {
        if path.len() == total {
            if !require_cycle || adj[cur].iter().any(|&v| v == start) {
                *best_solution = Some(path.clone());
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        if !can_finish(cur, used, adj, total) {
            return false;
        }

        let mut cand: Vec<usize> = adj[cur].iter().copied().filter(|&v| !used[v]).collect();
        cand.sort_by_key(|&v| adj[v].iter().filter(|&&u| !used[u]).count());

        for v in cand {
            used[v] = true;
            path.push(v);

            let mut fail = false;
            // Local degree pruning: every unused vertex must retain enough unused neighbors.
            for u in 0..total {
                if !used[u] {
                    let d = adj[u].iter().filter(|&&z| !used[z]).count();
                    if d == 0 {
                        fail = true;
                        break;
                    }
                    if require_cycle && d == 1 && u != start {
                        fail = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if !fail && dfs(v, start, require_cycle, used, path, adj, total, best_solution) {
                return true;
            }

            path.pop();
            used[v] = false;
        }
        false
    }

    if dfs(s, s, require_cycle == 1, &mut used, &mut path, &adj, n, &mut best_solution) {
        if let Some(sol) = best_solution {
            let out: Vec<(i32, i32)> = sol.into_iter().map(|i| cells[i]).collect();
            output_path(&out);
            return;
        }
    }

    println!("0");
}

fn open_grid_path(w: i32, h: i32, cycle: bool) -> Option<Vec<(i32, i32)>> {
    let mut path = Vec::new();
    if w == 1 && h == 1 {
        if cycle { return None; }
        path.push((0, 0));
        return Some(path);
    }

    if !cycle {
        for y in 0..h {
            if y % 2 == 0 {
                for x in 0..w { path.push((x, y)); }
            } else {
                for x in (0..w).rev() { path.push((x, y)); }
            }
        }
        return Some(path);
    }

    if w == 1 || h == 1 {
        return None;
    }

    if w % 2 == 0 {
        for x in 0..w {
            if x % 2 == 0 {
                for y in 0..h { path.push((x, y)); }
            } else {
                for y in (0..h).rev() { path.push((x, y)); }
            }
        }
        if adjacent(path[0], *path.last().unwrap()) { Some(path) } else { None }
    } else if h % 2 == 0 {
        for y in 0..h {
            if y % 2 == 0 {
                for x in 0..w { path.push((x, y)); }
            } else {
                for x in (0..w).rev() { path.push((x, y)); }
            }
        }
        if adjacent(path[0], *path.last().unwrap()) { Some(path) } else { None }
    } else {
        None
    }
}

fn adjacent(a: (i32, i32), b: (i32, i32)) -> bool {
    (a.0 - b.0).abs() + (a.1 - b.1).abs() == 1
}

fn output_path(path: &[(i32, i32)]) {
    println!("{}", path.len());
    for &(x, y) in path {
        println!("{} {}", x, y);
    }
}

Test 6: Orbital TSP - Space Station Route Planning (C#) Score: 6.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving an Orbital Travelling Salesman Problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY number of stations from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-5 stations (simple brute-force permutations feasible)
- **Medium**: 6-10 stations (requires heuristics, some optimization)
- **Large**: 15-20 stations (complex orbital mechanics, needs efficient algorithms)
- **Extreme**: 50-100 stations (very complex, requires advanced heuristics)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with 2 to 100 stations. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N mu
Line 2: start orbit: x y z vx vy vz
Lines 3..N+2: station orbits: x y z vx vy vz

**Output format (stdout):**
One line containing N integers: the visitation order of station indices (0..N-1).


**Starting position (epoch T=0):**
Position: (7197.9, 3733.4, -6152.3) km
Velocity: (4.567, -4.577, -1.601) km/s
Altitude: ~3807 km

**Physics constants:**
- Earth gravitational parameter μ = 398600.4418 km³/s²
- Earth radius = 6371 km

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number of stations
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 100 stations
3. **Accuracy**: Should provide reasonable delta-V estimates

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Visit a series of space stations orbiting Earth using the least fuel possible, then return to the starting station. Unlike a normal route-planning problem, every target is moving at thousands of metres per second and the “cost” of travelling between two stations depends on orbital physics, not straight-line distance.

The AI must combine route optimisation with orbital mechanics. Subpasses add more stations and trickier orbit geometries. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic with approximate fuel estimates.

 1.0000
[2 stations] Order: [1, 0], Delta-V: 14.25 km/s, Baseline: 14.25 km/s, Time: 0.1s - excellent (≤ baseline)

Orbital TSP - 2 stations

View Code (12583 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;

public static class Program
{
    private const double MuDefault = 398600.4418;
    private const double EarthRadius = 6371.0;
    private const double TwoPi = Math.PI * 2.0;

    private struct Vec3
    {
        public double X;
        public double Y;
        public double Z;

        public Vec3(double x, double y, double z)
        {
            X = x; Y = y; Z = z;
        }

        public static Vec3 operator +(Vec3 a, Vec3 b) => new Vec3(a.X + b.X, a.Y + b.Y, a.Z + b.Z);
        public static Vec3 operator -(Vec3 a, Vec3 b) => new Vec3(a.X - b.X, a.Y - b.Y, a.Z - b.Z);
        public static Vec3 operator *(Vec3 a, double k) => new Vec3(a.X * k, a.Y * k, a.Z * k);

        public double Dot(Vec3 b) => X * b.X + Y * b.Y + Z * b.Z;
        public Vec3 Cross(Vec3 b) => new Vec3(
            Y * b.Z - Z * b.Y,
            Z * b.X - X * b.Z,
            X * b.Y - Y * b.X);
        public double Norm() => Math.Sqrt(X * X + Y * Y + Z * Z);
    }

    private struct Orbit
    {
        public Vec3 R;
        public Vec3 V;
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        if (n <= 0) return;
        double mu = fs.NextDouble();

        Orbit start = ReadOrbit(fs);
        var stations = new Orbit[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) stations[i] = ReadOrbit(fs);

        int[] order = Solve(start, stations, mu);
        var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = false };
        for (int i = 0; i < order.Length; i++)
        {
            if (i > 0) sw.Write(' ');
            sw.Write(order[i]);
        }
        sw.WriteLine();
        sw.Flush();
    }

    private static Orbit ReadOrbit(FastScanner fs)
    {
        return new Orbit
        {
            R = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble()),
            V = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble())
        };
    }

    private static int[] Solve(Orbit start, Orbit[] stations, double mu)
    {
        int n = stations.Length;
        if (n == 1) return new[] { 0 };

        var features = new StationFeature[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) features[i] = MakeFeature(stations[i], mu);
        var startFeature = MakeFeature(start, mu);

        if (n <= 10)
        {
            return SolveSmall(startFeature, features, mu);
        }
        else if (n <= 25)
        {
            return ImproveTour(startFeature, features, mu, InitialTour(startFeature, features));
        }
        else
        {
            return LargeScaleTour(startFeature, features, mu);
        }
    }

    private struct StationFeature
    {
        public Vec3 R;
        public Vec3 V;
        public double Radius;
        public double Speed;
        public double Energy;
        public Vec3 H;
        public double Inclination;
        public double Omega;
        public double TrueAnomaly;
    }

    private static StationFeature MakeFeature(Orbit o, double mu)
    {
        double r = o.R.Norm();
        double v = o.V.Norm();
        Vec3 h = o.R.Cross(o.V);
        double hNorm = h.Norm();
        double incl = hNorm > 0 ? Math.Acos(Clamp(h.Z / hNorm, -1, 1)) : 0.0;
        Vec3 nVec = new Vec3(-h.Y, h.X, 0);
        double nNorm = nVec.Norm();
        double omega = 0.0;
        if (nNorm > 1e-12)
        {
            omega = Math.Atan2(nVec.Y, nVec.X);
            if (omega < 0) omega += TwoPi;
        }
        double nu = Math.Atan2(o.R.Dot(o.V) / Math.Sqrt(mu * Math.Max(r, 1e-9)), 1.0 - r * v * v / mu);
        if (double.IsNaN(nu)) nu = 0.0;
        double energy = 0.5 * v * v - mu / r;
        return new StationFeature
        {
            R = o.R,
            V = o.V,
            Radius = r,
            Speed = v,
            Energy = energy,
            H = h,
            Inclination = incl,
            Omega = omega,
            TrueAnomaly = nu
        };
    }

    private static int[] SolveSmall(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations, double mu)
    {
        int n = stations.Length;
        int[] perm = Enumerable.Range(0, n).ToArray();
        double best = double.PositiveInfinity;
        int[] bestPerm = (int[])perm.Clone();
        do
        {
            double cost = TourCost(start, stations, perm, mu);
            if (cost < best)
            {
                best = cost;
                Array.Copy(perm, bestPerm, n);
            }
        } while (NextPermutation(perm));
        return bestPerm;
    }

    private static int[] InitialTour(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations)
    {
        int n = stations.Length;
        var idx = Enumerable.Range(0, n)
            .OrderBy(i => stations[i].Radius)
            .ThenBy(i => stations[i].Inclination)
            .ThenBy(i => stations[i].Omega)
            .ThenBy(i => stations[i].TrueAnomaly)
            .ToArray();
        return idx;
    }

    private static int[] ImproveTour(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations, double mu, int[] order)
    {
        int n = order.Length;
        double best = TourCost(start, stations, order, mu);
        bool improved = true;
        int iter = 0;
        while (improved && iter++ < 200)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++)
                {
                    Array.Reverse(order, i, j - i + 1);
                    double cost = TourCost(start, stations, order, mu);
                    if (cost + 1e-9 < best)
                    {
                        best = cost;
                        improved = true;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        Array.Reverse(order, i, j - i + 1);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return order;
    }

    private static int[] LargeScaleTour(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations, double mu)
    {
        int n = stations.Length;
        var clusters = stations.Select((s, i) => (s, i))
            .OrderBy(t => t.s.Inclination)
            .ThenBy(t => t.s.Omega)
            .ThenBy(t => t.s.TrueAnomaly)
            .ToArray();

        int[] order = clusters.Select(t => t.i).ToArray();
        order = TwoOpt(start, stations, order, mu, 2);
        order = OrOpt(start, stations, order, mu, 1);
        order = OrOpt(start, stations, order, mu, 2);
        return order;
    }

    private static int[] TwoOpt(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations, int[] order, double mu, int passes)
    {
        int n = order.Length;
        double best = TourCost(start, stations, order, mu);
        for (int p = 0; p < passes; p++)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++)
                {
                    Array.Reverse(order, i, j - i + 1);
                    double cost = TourCost(start, stations, order, mu);
                    if (cost + 1e-9 < best)
                    {
                        best = cost;
                        changed = true;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        Array.Reverse(order, i, j - i + 1);
                    }
                }
            }
            if (!changed) break;
        }
        return order;
    }

    private static int[] OrOpt(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations, int[] order, double mu, int len)
    {
        int n = order.Length;
        double best = TourCost(start, stations, order, mu);
        bool improved = true;
        while (improved)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i + len <= n; i++)
            {
                int[] segment = new int[len];
                Array.Copy(order, i, segment, 0, len);
                int[] remainder = new int[n - len];
                if (i > 0) Array.Copy(order, 0, remainder, 0, i);
                if (i + len < n) Array.Copy(order, i + len, remainder, i, n - i - len);
                for (int j = 0; j <= remainder.Length; j++)
                {
                    int[] candidate = new int[n];
                    if (j > 0) Array.Copy(remainder, 0, candidate, 0, j);
                    Array.Copy(segment, 0, candidate, j, len);
                    if (j < remainder.Length) Array.Copy(remainder, j, candidate, j + len, remainder.Length - j);
                    double cost = TourCost(start, stations, candidate, mu);
                    if (cost + 1e-9 < best)
                    {
                        Array.Copy(candidate, order, n);
                        best = cost;
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
                if (improved) break;
            }
        }
        return order;
    }

    private static double TourCost(StationFeature start, StationFeature[] stations, int[] order, double mu)
    {
        double sum = 0.0;
        StationFeature cur = start;
        for (int i = 0; i < order.Length; i++)
        {
            StationFeature nxt = stations[order[i]];
            sum += TransferCost(cur, nxt, mu);
            cur = nxt;
        }
        return sum;
    }

    private static double TransferCost(StationFeature a, StationFeature b, double mu)
    {
        double dr = (b.R - a.R).Norm();
        double dv = (b.V - a.V).Norm();
        double ra = Math.Max(a.Radius, EarthRadius + 1.0);
        double rb = Math.Max(b.Radius, EarthRadius + 1.0);
        double da = Math.Abs(a.Inclination - b.Inclination);
        double doo = AngleDiff(a.Omega, b.Omega);
        double dnu = AngleDiff(a.TrueAnomaly, b.TrueAnomaly);
        double phase = dr / (ra + rb) + 0.15 * dv + 5.0 * da + 0.5 * doo + 0.2 * dnu;
        double grav = Math.Abs(a.Energy - b.Energy) / 1000.0;
        return phase + grav;
    }

    private static double AngleDiff(double a, double b)
    {
        double d = Math.Abs(a - b) % TwoPi;
        return d > Math.PI ? TwoPi - d : d;
    }

    private static double Clamp(double x, double lo, double hi) => x < lo ? lo : (x > hi ? hi : x);

    private static bool NextPermutation(int[] a)
    {
        int n = a.Length;
        int i = n - 2;
        while (i >= 0 && a[i] >= a[i + 1]) i--;
        if (i < 0) return false;
        int j = n - 1;
        while (a[j] <= a[i]) j--;
        (a[i], a[j]) = (a[j], a[i]);
        Array.Reverse(a, i + 1, n - i - 1);
        return true;
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buf = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len, _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream) { _stream = stream; }

        private byte Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buf, 0, _buf.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return 0;
            }
            return _buf[_ptr++];
        }

        public int NextInt() => (int)NextDouble();

        public double NextDouble()
        {
            byte c;
            do c = Read(); while (c <= 32 && c != 0);
            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }
            double val = 0;
            while (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            if (c == '.')
            {
                double frac = 1;
                c = Read();
                while (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
                {
                    frac *= 0.1;
                    val += (c - '0') * frac;
                    c = Read();
                }
            }
            if (c == 'e' || c == 'E')
            {
                int esign = 1;
                int exp = 0;
                c = Read();
                if (c == '-')
                {
                    esign = -1;
                    c = Read();
                }
                else if (c == '+')
                {
                    c = Read();
                }
                while (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
                {
                    exp = exp * 10 + (c - '0');
                    c = Read();
                }
                val *= Math.Pow(10, esign * exp);
            }
            return sign * val;
        }
    }
}

Test 7: CSG Union of Two Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are implementing a CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) Union operation in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY mesh complexity from trivial (6 vertex unit cubes) to
ludicrous scale (millions of vertices). The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: VA FA VB FB (vertex/face counts for mesh A and B)
Next VA lines: x y z (mesh A vertices)
Next FA lines: a b c (mesh A faces, 0-indexed)
Next VB lines: x y z (mesh B vertices)
Next FB lines: a b c (mesh B faces)

You can assume that the input geometry is clean, manifold, watertight, and free of self-intersections.

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: V F (vertex and face counts for union mesh)
Next V lines: x y z
Next F lines: a b c (0-indexed triangle indices)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on mesh complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very complex meshes
3. **Correctness**: Must output correct union geometry. Must be watertight, connected,
manifold, free of holes and self intersections..

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Given two overlapping 3D shapes (triangle meshes), merge them into a single combined shape — like gluing two lumps of clay together and keeping only the outer surface. The output mesh must be watertight with no self-intersections.

The AI must correctly handle the tricky geometry where the two shapes overlap: splitting triangles, discarding interior faces, and stitching everything back together. Subpasses use increasingly complex shape pairs.

View Code (9305 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <map>
#include <queue>
#include <set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Vec3 {
    double x{}, y{}, z{};
    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double x_, double y_, double z_) : x(x_), y(y_), z(z_) {}
    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& o) const { return {x + o.x, y + o.y, z + o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& o) const { return {x - o.x, y - o.y, z - o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return {x * s, y * s, z * s}; }
};

static inline Vec3 cross(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {
        a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y,
        a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z,
        a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x
    };
}

static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z;
}

static inline double norm2(const Vec3& a) { return dot(a, a); }

struct Plane {
    Vec3 n;
    double d{};
    double eval(const Vec3& p) const { return dot(n, p) + d; }
};

struct Tri {
    array<int, 3> v{};
};

struct Mesh {
    vector<Vec3> verts;
    vector<Tri> tris;
};

static constexpr double EPS = 1e-9;

static inline int sgn(double x) {
    if (x > EPS) return 1;
    if (x < -EPS) return -1;
    return 0;
}

static inline Vec3 lerp(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, double t) {
    return a + (b - a) * t;
}

static Plane plane_from_tri(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c) {
    Plane p;
    p.n = cross(b - a, c - a);
    double nn = sqrt(max(1e-30, norm2(p.n)));
    p.n = p.n * (1.0 / nn);
    p.d = -dot(p.n, a);
    return p;
}

static bool point_inside_mesh(const Mesh& m, const Vec3& p) {
    Vec3 dir{1.0, 0.3141592653589793, 0.2718281828459045};
    int hits = 0;
    for (const auto& t : m.tris) {
        const Vec3& a = m.verts[t.v[0]];
        const Vec3& b = m.verts[t.v[1]];
        const Vec3& c = m.verts[t.v[2]];
        Vec3 ab = b - a, ac = c - a;
        Vec3 n = cross(ab, ac);
        double denom = dot(n, dir);
        if (fabs(denom) < EPS) continue;
        double tval = dot(n, a - p) / denom;
        if (tval <= EPS) continue;
        Vec3 q = p + dir * tval;
        Vec3 ap = q - a, bp = q - b, cp = q - c;
        Vec3 c0 = cross(ab, ap);
        Vec3 c1 = cross(c - b, bp);
        Vec3 c2 = cross(a - c, cp);
        if (dot(n, c0) >= -EPS && dot(n, c1) >= -EPS && dot(n, c2) >= -EPS) {
            ++hits;
        }
    }
    return (hits & 1) != 0;
}

struct QuantKey {
    long long x, y, z;
    bool operator==(const QuantKey& o) const { return x == o.x && y == o.y && z == o.z; }
    bool operator<(const QuantKey& o) const {
        if (x != o.x) return x < o.x;
        if (y != o.y) return y < o.y;
        return z < o.z;
    }
};

struct KeyHash {
    size_t operator()(const QuantKey& k) const noexcept {
        size_t h = std::hash<long long>{}(k.x);
        h ^= std::hash<long long>{}(k.y) + 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL + (h << 6) + (h >> 2);
        h ^= std::hash<long long>{}(k.z) + 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL + (h << 6) + (h >> 2);
        return h;
    }
};

static QuantKey quant(const Vec3& p) {
    const double S = 1e8;
    return {
        (long long)llround(p.x * S),
        (long long)llround(p.y * S),
        (long long)llround(p.z * S)
    };
}

struct EdgeKey {
    int a, b;
    bool operator<(const EdgeKey& o) const {
        if (a != o.a) return a < o.a;
        return b < o.b;
    }
};

static vector<Tri> triangulate_polygon(const vector<int>& poly) {
    vector<Tri> out;
    if (poly.size() < 3) return out;
    for (size_t i = 1; i + 1 < poly.size(); ++i) {
        out.push_back({{poly[0], poly[i], poly[i + 1]}});
    }
    return out;
}

static vector<Tri> split_triangles_by_plane(const Mesh& src, const vector<int>& tris, const Plane& pl, vector<Vec3>& verts, unordered_map<QuantKey, int, KeyHash>& vmap) {
    vector<Tri> out;
    for (int ti : tris) {
        const Tri& t = src.tris[ti];
        array<Vec3, 3> p = {src.verts[t.v[0]], src.verts[t.v[1]], src.verts[t.v[2]]};
        array<double, 3> d = {pl.eval(p[0]), pl.eval(p[1]), pl.eval(p[2])};
        array<int, 3> side = {sgn(d[0]), sgn(d[1]), sgn(d[2])};
        vector<Vec3> poly_front, poly_back;
        auto add_point = [&](vector<Vec3>& poly, const Vec3& q) {
            poly.push_back(q);
        };
        for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
            int j = (i + 1) % 3;
            const Vec3& a = p[i];
            const Vec3& b = p[j];
            double da = d[i], db = d[j];
            int sa = side[i], sb = side[j];
            if (sa >= 0) add_point(poly_front, a);
            if (sa <= 0) add_point(poly_back, a);
            if ((sa > 0 && sb < 0) || (sa < 0 && sb > 0)) {
                double t = da / (da - db);
                Vec3 q = lerp(a, b, t);
                add_point(poly_front, q);
                add_point(poly_back, q);
            }
        }
        auto emit = [&](const vector<Vec3>& poly) {
            vector<int> idx;
            idx.reserve(poly.size());
            for (const auto& q : poly) {
                QuantKey k = quant(q);
                auto it = vmap.find(k);
                if (it != vmap.end()) {
                    idx.push_back(it->second);
                } else {
                    int id = (int)verts.size();
                    verts.push_back(q);
                    vmap.emplace(k, id);
                    idx.push_back(id);
                }
            }
            auto tris2 = triangulate_polygon(idx);
            out.insert(out.end(), tris2.begin(), tris2.end());
        };
        if (poly_front.size() >= 3) emit(poly_front);
        if (poly_back.size() >= 3) emit(poly_back);
    }
    return out;
}

static Mesh union_mesh(const Mesh& A, const Mesh& B) {
    vector<Vec3> verts;
    vector<Tri> tris;
    verts.reserve(A.verts.size() + B.verts.size());
    unordered_map<QuantKey, int, KeyHash> vmap;
    vmap.reserve((A.verts.size() + B.verts.size()) * 2 + 1);

    auto add_mesh_direct = [&](const Mesh& m) {
        for (const auto& v : m.verts) {
            QuantKey k = quant(v);
            if (!vmap.count(k)) {
                vmap.emplace(k, (int)verts.size());
                verts.push_back(v);
            }
        }
    };
    add_mesh_direct(A);
    add_mesh_direct(B);

    vector<int> aidx(A.tris.size()), bidx(B.tris.size());
    for (int i = 0; i < (int)A.tris.size(); ++i) aidx[i] = i;
    for (int i = 0; i < (int)B.tris.size(); ++i) bidx[i] = i;

    vector<Plane> planes;
    planes.reserve(A.tris.size() + B.tris.size());
    for (const auto& t : A.tris) planes.push_back(plane_from_tri(A.verts[t.v[0]], A.verts[t.v[1]], A.verts[t.v[2]]));
    for (const auto& t : B.tris) planes.push_back(plane_from_tri(B.verts[t.v[0]], B.verts[t.v[1]], B.verts[t.v[2]]));

    vector<Tri> aout, bout;
    aout.reserve(A.tris.size() * 2);
    bout.reserve(B.tris.size() * 2);

    vector<Vec3> newVerts;
    unordered_map<QuantKey, int, KeyHash> newMap;
    newMap.reserve((A.verts.size() + B.verts.size()) * 4 + 1);

    auto classify_tri = [&](const Mesh& m, const Tri& t, const Mesh& other) -> bool {
        Vec3 c = (m.verts[t.v[0]] + m.verts[t.v[1]] + m.verts[t.v[2]]) * (1.0 / 3.0);
        return point_inside_mesh(other, c);
    };

    for (const auto& t : A.tris) {
        if (!classify_tri(A, t, B)) aout.push_back(t);
    }
    for (const auto& t : B.tris) {
        if (!classify_tri(B, t, A)) bout.push_back(t);
    }

    vector<Tri> all = aout;
    all.insert(all.end(), bout.begin(), bout.end());

    Mesh result;
    result.verts = verts;
    result.tris = all;
    return result;
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int VA, FA, VB, FB;
    if (!(cin >> VA >> FA >> VB >> FB)) return 0;

    Mesh A, B;
    A.verts.resize(VA);
    A.tris.resize(FA);
    B.verts.resize(VB);
    B.tris.resize(FB);

    for (int i = 0; i < VA; ++i) cin >> A.verts[i].x >> A.verts[i].y >> A.verts[i].z;
    for (int i = 0; i < FA; ++i) cin >> A.tris[i].v[0] >> A.tris[i].v[1] >> A.tris[i].v[2];
    for (int i = 0; i < VB; ++i) cin >> B.verts[i].x >> B.verts[i].y >> B.verts[i].z;
    for (int i = 0; i < FB; ++i) cin >> B.tris[i].v[0] >> B.tris[i].v[1] >> B.tris[i].v[2];

    Mesh U = union_mesh(A, B);

    vector<Vec3> outVerts;
    vector<Tri> outTris;
    unordered_map<QuantKey, int, KeyHash> idmap;
    idmap.reserve(U.verts.size() * 2 + 1);

    auto addv = [&](const Vec3& p) {
        QuantKey k = quant(p);
        auto it = idmap.find(k);
        if (it != idmap.end()) return it->second;
        int id = (int)outVerts.size();
        outVerts.push_back(p);
        idmap.emplace(k, id);
        return id;
    };

    for (const auto& t : U.tris) {
        Tri q;
        q.v[0] = addv(U.verts[t.v[0]]);
        q.v[1] = addv(U.verts[t.v[1]]);
        q.v[2] = addv(U.verts[t.v[2]]);
        if (q.v[0] != q.v[1] && q.v[1] != q.v[2] && q.v[2] != q.v[0]) outTris.push_back(q);
    }

    cout << outVerts.size() << ' ' << outTris.size() << '\n';
    cout.setf(std::ios::fixed);
    cout.precision(10);
    for (const auto& p : outVerts) cout << p.x << ' ' << p.y << ' ' << p.z << '\n';
    for (const auto& t : outTris) cout << t.v[0] << ' ' << t.v[1] << ' ' << t.v[2] << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 8: Maze Solver (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a maze solver in Rust that must handle ANY maze complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

Your program will be tested with mazes ranging from 5x5 to 50000x50000.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Maze format:**
- 'A' = Start position
- 'B' = Goal/End position
- '#' = Wall (cannot pass through)
- ' ' (space) = Open path (can walk through)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the maze row strings (each W characters)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of steps in path)
Next N lines: x y (0-indexed coordinates from top-left)
Path should include both start (A) and end (B) positions.
Movement is only up/down/left/right (no diagonals).
Output nothing (or 0) if no path exists.

**Constraints:**
- Path must be continuous (each step adjacent to previous)
- Path must not go through walls ('#')
- Path must start at 'A' and end at 'B'
- Path must not loop even if the maze allows it.
- If multiple solutions exist, return any one.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the shortest path through a maze from a start cell to an end cell. The maze is a grid of walls and open passages, and the AI must navigate from one corner to the other using the fewest steps possible.

Subpasses increase the maze size dramatically, testing whether the AI's approach scales to very large mazes. The baseline finds the true shortest path using breadth-first search.

 0.0000
[5x5] Invalid path: Empty path

Maze Solver - 5x5

View Code (2862 chars)
use std::collections::VecDeque;
use std::io::{self, Read};

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let h: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(v) => v.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };
    let w: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let n = h * w;

    let mut grid = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    let mut start = None;
    let mut goal = None;

    for y in 0..h {
        let row = it.next().unwrap_or("");
        let bytes = row.as_bytes();
        for x in 0..w {
            let c = *bytes.get(x).unwrap_or(&b'#');
            grid.push(c);
            if c == b'A' {
                start = Some(y * w + x);
            } else if c == b'B' {
                goal = Some(y * w + x);
            }
        }
    }

    let start = match start {
        Some(s) => s,
        None => {
            println!("0");
            return;
        }
    };
    let goal = match goal {
        Some(g) => g,
        None => {
            println!("0");
            return;
        }
    };

    if start == goal {
        println!("1\n0 0");
        return;
    }

    let mut visited = vec![false; n];
    let mut parent = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();

    visited[start] = true;
    q.push_back(start);

    while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
        if u == goal {
            break;
        }
        let x = u % w;
        let y = u / w;

        if x > 0 {
            let v = u - 1;
            if !visited[v] && grid[v] != b'#' {
                visited[v] = true;
                parent[v] = u;
                q.push_back(v);
            }
        }
        if x + 1 < w {
            let v = u + 1;
            if !visited[v] && grid[v] != b'#' {
                visited[v] = true;
                parent[v] = u;
                q.push_back(v);
            }
        }
        if y > 0 {
            let v = u - w;
            if !visited[v] && grid[v] != b'#' {
                visited[v] = true;
                parent[v] = u;
                q.push_back(v);
            }
        }
        if y + 1 < h {
            let v = u + w;
            if !visited[v] && grid[v] != b'#' {
                visited[v] = true;
                parent[v] = u;
                q.push_back(v);
            }
        }
    }

    if !visited[goal] {
        println!("0");
        return;
    }

    let mut path = Vec::new();
    let mut cur = goal;
    while cur != usize::MAX {
        path.push(cur);
        if cur == start {
            break;
        }
        cur = parent[cur];
    }
    path.reverse();

    let mut out = String::new();
    out.push_str(&format!("{}\n", path.len()));
    for idx in path {
        out.push_str(&format!("{} {}\n", idx % w, idx / w));
    }
    print!("{}", out);
}

Test 9: 1D Cutting Stock - Minimum Waste (C#) Score: 11.50 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 1D Cutting Stock Problem in C#.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N stock_length
Line 2: N cut lengths separated by spaces

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next num_stocks lines: space-separated cut indices assigned to that stock (0-indexed)

**Example:**
For cuts=[30, 40, 50] and stock_length=100:
Input:
3 100
30 40 50

Output:
2
0 1
2

**Constraints:**
- Each cut must come from a single stock piece (no gluing)
- Multiple cuts can come from the same stock piece if they fit
- No cut can exceed stock_length
- Must handle varying numbers of cuts efficiently. You can use
multiple threads, you can micro-optimise code, you can write clever algorithms,
whatever it takes to handle the problem and solve it within 5 minutes even if
there are millions of cuts.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given fixed-length stock material and a shopping list of smaller pieces to cut, use as few stock pieces as possible. Every requested piece must be cut from somewhere, but any leftover material is wasted — so packing the cuts tightly matters.

This is essentially a bin-packing problem and is NP-hard. Subpasses scale from a handful of cuts up to millions, forcing the AI to write code that is both correct and fast. The baseline uses a simple first-fit-decreasing heuristic.

 1.0000
[5 cuts, stock=100] Stocks used: 2, Waste: 20, Time: 0.1s - (waste < 1 stock, no repack check)

Cutting Stock - 5 cuts, stock=100

View Code (2996 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;

public static class Program
{
    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len;
        private int _ptr;

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = Read();
            } while (c <= 32 && c != -1);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }

            int value = 0;
            while (c > 32 && c != -1)
            {
                value = value * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return value * sign;
        }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner();
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int stockLength = fs.NextInt();

        var cuts = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cuts[i] = fs.NextInt();

        var order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) order[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) =>
        {
            int cmp = cuts[b].CompareTo(cuts[a]);
            return cmp != 0 ? cmp : a.CompareTo(b);
        });

        var bins = new List<List<int>>();
        var remaining = new List<int>();

        foreach (int idx in order)
        {
            int len = cuts[idx];
            if (len > stockLength)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("0");
                return;
            }

            int chosen = -1;
            int bestRem = int.MaxValue;

            for (int b = 0; b < bins.Count; b++)
            {
                int rem = remaining[b];
                if (len <= rem && rem - len < bestRem)
                {
                    bestRem = rem - len;
                    chosen = b;
                    if (bestRem == 0) break;
                }
            }

            if (chosen == -1)
            {
                bins.Add(new List<int>());
                remaining.Add(stockLength);
                chosen = bins.Count - 1;
            }

            bins[chosen].Add(idx);
            remaining[chosen] -= len;
        }

        var writer = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = false };
        writer.WriteLine(bins.Count);
        for (int i = 0; i < bins.Count; i++)
        {
            var bin = bins[i];
            for (int j = 0; j < bin.Count; j++)
            {
                if (j > 0) writer.Write(' ');
                writer.Write(bin[j]);
            }
            writer.WriteLine();
        }
        writer.Flush();
    }
}
