Tetris AI - 50 pieces

View C# Code (13567 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;

public class Program
{
    enum PieceType { I, O, T, S, Z, J, L }

    struct Move
    {
        public int Rotation;
        public int Column;
        public int Score;
        public Move(int r, int c, int s) { Rotation = r; Column = c; Score = s; }
    }

    static readonly string[] PieceNames = { "I", "O", "T", "S", "Z", "J", "L" };

    static readonly int[][][] Shapes = new int[][][]
    {
        new int[][] { new[]{0,0,0,0}, new[]{1,1,1,1}, new[]{0,0,0,0}, new[]{0,0,0,0} }, // I
        new int[][] { new[]{1,1}, new[]{1,1} }, // O
        new int[][] { new[]{0,1,0}, new[]{1,1,1}, new[]{0,0,0} }, // T
        new int[][] { new[]{0,1,1}, new[]{1,1,0}, new[]{0,0,0} }, // S
        new int[][] { new[]{1,1,0}, new[]{0,1,1}, new[]{0,0,0} }, // Z
        new int[][] { new[]{1,0,0}, new[]{1,1,1}, new[]{0,0,0} }, // J
        new int[][] { new[]{0,0,1}, new[]{1,1,1}, new[]{0,0,0} }, // L
    };

    static void Main()
    {
        var input = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int width = input.NextInt();
        int height = input.NextInt();
        int numPieces = input.NextInt();

        var board = new Board(width, height);
        var outWriter = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = true };

        int pieceIndex = 0;
        while (pieceIndex < numPieces)
        {
            string token = input.Next();
            if (token == null) break;
            PieceType piece = ParsePiece(token);

            var move = ChooseMove(board, piece, pieceIndex, numPieces);
            outWriter.WriteLine($"{move.Rotation} {move.Column}");
            outWriter.Flush();

            string result = input.Next();
            if (result == null) break;
            if (result == "gameover") break;
            if (result.StartsWith("cleared_", StringComparison.Ordinal))
            {
                int cleared = int.Parse(result.Substring(8));
                board.ApplyMove(piece, move.Rotation, move.Column, cleared);
            }
            else
            {
                board.ApplyMove(piece, move.Rotation, move.Column, 0);
            }

            pieceIndex++;
        }
    }

    static PieceType ParsePiece(string s)
    {
        return s[0] switch
        {
            'I' => PieceType.I,
            'O' => PieceType.O,
            'T' => PieceType.T,
            'S' => PieceType.S,
            'Z' => PieceType.Z,
            'J' => PieceType.J,
            'L' => PieceType.L,
            _ => PieceType.T
        };
    }

    static Move ChooseMove(Board board, PieceType piece, int pieceIndex, int numPieces)
    {
        var candidates = board.GetCandidates(piece);
        if (candidates.Count == 0) return new Move(0, 0, int.MinValue / 2);

        int empties = board.EmptyCells();
        int filled = board.Width * board.Height - empties;
        bool complex = board.Width >= 12 || board.Height >= 20 || pieceIndex > 30 || filled > board.Width * board.Height / 4;
        int depth = complex ? 2 : 1;
        if (numPieces - pieceIndex < 10) depth = 1;

        Move best = new Move(0, candidates[0].Column, int.MinValue / 2);
        foreach (var cand in candidates)
        {
            var sim = board.Clone();
            if (!sim.TryPlace(piece, cand.Rotation, cand.Column, out int cleared)) continue;
            double score = ScoreBoard(sim, cleared, piece, cand.Rotation, cand.Column);

            if (depth > 1)
            {
                var nextPieceBias = 0.0;
                foreach (var nextPiece in AllPieces())
                {
                    var nextMoves = sim.GetCandidates(nextPiece);
                    if (nextMoves.Count == 0) continue;
                    double localBest = double.NegativeInfinity;
                    int limit = Math.Min(nextMoves.Count, complex ? 8 : 4);
                    for (int i = 0; i < limit; i++)
                    {
                        var nm = nextMoves[i];
                        var sim2 = sim.Clone();
                        if (!sim2.TryPlace(nextPiece, nm.Rotation, nm.Column, out int cleared2)) continue;
                        double s2 = ScoreBoard(sim2, cleared2, nextPiece, nm.Rotation, nm.Column);
                        if (s2 > localBest) localBest = s2;
                    }
                    if (localBest > double.NegativeInfinity / 2) nextPieceBias += localBest;
                }
                score += 0.08 * nextPieceBias / 7.0;
            }

            int intScore = (int)Math.Round(score * 1000.0);
            if (intScore > best.Score || (intScore == best.Score && TieBreak(cand.Rotation, cand.Column, best.Rotation, best.Column)))
                best = new Move(cand.Rotation, cand.Column, intScore);
        }
        return best;
    }

    static bool TieBreak(int rotA, int colA, int rotB, int colB)
    {
        if (rotA != rotB) return rotA < rotB;
        return colA < colB;
    }

    static IEnumerable<PieceType> AllPieces()
    {
        yield return PieceType.I;
        yield return PieceType.O;
        yield return PieceType.T;
        yield return PieceType.S;
        yield return PieceType.Z;
        yield return PieceType.J;
        yield return PieceType.L;
    }

    static double ScoreBoard(Board b, int cleared, PieceType piece, int rot, int col)
    {
        int[] heights = b.GetColumnHeights();
        int aggregateHeight = 0;
        int holes = 0;
        int bumpiness = 0;
        int maxHeight = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < heights.Length; i++)
        {
            aggregateHeight += heights[i];
            if (heights[i] > maxHeight) maxHeight = heights[i];
            if (i + 1 < heights.Length) bumpiness += Math.Abs(heights[i] - heights[i + 1]);
            holes += b.CountHolesInColumn(i, heights[i]);
        }

        double score = 0;
        score += cleared * 8.0;
        score -= aggregateHeight * 0.52;
        score -= holes * 1.25;
        score -= bumpiness * 0.22;
        score -= maxHeight * 0.35;

        int center = b.Width / 2;
        int pieceWidth = GetRotatedShape(piece, rot).GetLength(1);
        int pieceCenter = col + pieceWidth / 2;
        score -= Math.Abs(pieceCenter - center) * 0.08;

        if (maxHeight >= b.Height - 2) score -= 1000;
        if (holes > b.Width * 2) score -= 25;
        return score;
    }

    static int[][] GetRotatedShape(PieceType piece, int rotation)
    {
        int idx = (int)piece;
        int[][] shape = Shapes[idx];
        rotation &= 3;
        for (int r = 0; r < rotation; r++) shape = Rotate(shape);
        return Trim(shape);
    }

    static int[][] Rotate(int[][] src)
    {
        int n = src.Length;
        int m = src[0].Length;
        var dst = new int[m][];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            dst[i] = new int[n];
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) dst[i][j] = src[n - 1 - j][i];
        }
        return dst;
    }

    static int[][] Trim(int[][] src)
    {
        int top = src.Length, bottom = -1, left = src[0].Length, right = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < src.Length; i++)
        for (int j = 0; j < src[i].Length; j++)
            if (src[i][j] != 0)
            {
                if (i < top) top = i;
                if (i > bottom) bottom = i;
                if (j < left) left = j;
                if (j > right) right = j;
            }
        int h = bottom - top + 1;
        int w = right - left + 1;
        var t = new int[h][];
        for (int i = 0; i < h; i++)
        {
            t[i] = new int[w];
            for (int j = 0; j < w; j++) t[i][j] = src[top + i][left + j];
        }
        return t;
    }

    class Board
    {
        public int Width { get; }
        public int Height { get; }
        private readonly bool[,] cells;

        public Board(int width, int height)
        {
            Width = width;
            Height = height;
            cells = new bool[height, width];
        }

        private Board(int width, int height, bool[,] clone)
        {
            Width = width;
            Height = height;
            cells = clone;
        }

        public Board Clone()
        {
            var clone = new bool[Height, Width];
            Array.Copy(cells, clone, cells.Length);
            return new Board(Width, Height, clone);
        }

        public int EmptyCells()
        {
            int empty = 0;
            for (int y = 0; y < Height; y++)
            for (int x = 0; x < Width; x++)
                if (!cells[y, x]) empty++;
            return empty;
        }

        public List<(int Rotation, int Column)> GetCandidates(PieceType piece)
        {
            var list = new List<(int, int)>();
            for (int rot = 0; rot < 4; rot++)
            {
                var shape = GetRotatedShape(piece, rot);
                int w = shape[0].Length;
                int h = shape.Length;
                if (w > Width || h > Height) continue;
                for (int col = 0; col <= Width - w; col++) list.Add((rot, col));
            }
            return list;
        }

        public bool TryPlace(PieceType piece, int rotation, int column, out int cleared)
        {
            cleared = 0;
            var shape = GetRotatedShape(piece, rotation);
            int w = shape[0].Length;
            int h = shape.Length;
            int row = FindDropRow(shape, column);
            if (row < 0 || row + h > Height) return false;
            for (int y = 0; y < h; y++)
            for (int x = 0; x < w; x++)
                if (shape[y][x] != 0)
                {
                    int by = row + y;
                    int bx = column + x;
                    if (by < 0 || by >= Height || bx < 0 || bx >= Width || cells[by, bx]) return false;
                }
            for (int y = 0; y < h; y++)
            for (int x = 0; x < w; x++)
                if (shape[y][x] != 0) cells[row + y, column + x] = true;
            cleared = ClearLines();
            return true;
        }

        public void ApplyMove(PieceType piece, int rotation, int column, int cleared)
        {
            TryPlace(piece, rotation, column, out _);
        }

        private int FindDropRow(int[][] shape, int column)
        {
            int w = shape[0].Length;
            int h = shape.Length;
            int row = 0;
            while (row + h <= Height)
            {
                if (!Collides(shape, column, row + 1)) row++;
                else break;
            }
            if (Collides(shape, column, row)) return -1;
            return row;
        }

        private bool Collides(int[][] shape, int col, int row)
        {
            int h = shape.Length;
            int w = shape[0].Length;
            for (int y = 0; y < h; y++)
            for (int x = 0; x < w; x++)
                if (shape[y][x] != 0)
                {
                    int by = row + y;
                    int bx = col + x;
                    if (by >= Height || bx < 0 || bx >= Width) return true;
                    if (by >= 0 && cells[by, bx]) return true;
                }
            return false;
        }

        private int ClearLines()
        {
            int cleared = 0;
            for (int y = Height - 1; y >= 0; y--)
            {
                bool full = true;
                for (int x = 0; x < Width; x++)
                {
                    if (!cells[y, x]) { full = false; break; }
                }
                if (full)
                {
                    cleared++;
                    for (int yy = y; yy > 0; yy--)
                        for (int x = 0; x < Width; x++)
                            cells[yy, x] = cells[yy - 1, x];
                    for (int x = 0; x < Width; x++) cells[0, x] = false;
                    y++;
                }
            }
            return cleared;
        }

        public int[] GetColumnHeights()
        {
            int[] heights = new int[Width];
            for (int x = 0; x < Width; x++)
            {
                int h = 0;
                for (int y = 0; y < Height; y++)
                {
                    if (cells[y, x]) { h = Height - y; break; }
                }
                heights[x] = h;
            }
            return heights;
        }

        public int CountHolesInColumn(int x, int colHeight)
        {
            int holes = 0;
            bool blockSeen = false;
            for (int y = 0; y < Height; y++)
            {
                if (cells[y, x]) blockSeen = true;
                else if (blockSeen) holes++;
            }
            return holes;
        }
    }

    class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream stream;
        private readonly byte[] buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int len, ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream s) { stream = s; }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (ptr >= len)
            {
                len = stream.Read(buffer, 0, buffer.Length);
                ptr = 0;
                if (len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return buffer[ptr++];
        }

        public string Next()
        {
            int c;
            do { c = Read(); if (c == -1) return null; } while (c <= 32);
            var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
            while (c > 32 && c != -1)
            {
                sb.Append((char)c);
                c = Read();
            }
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        public int NextInt() => int.Parse(Next());
    }
}

Test 23: Lunar Lander Game Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to play Lunar Lander in real-time.

Your Rust lunar lander controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from simple
training environments to very complex landing sites.

Control a lunar lander to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a 2D voxel map and continuous state updates, and must output thrust/turn
commands to land safely on the target.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time. If your code
doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the io buffer fills up, the ship will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes that
async code doesn't have. Multithreaded / asyncio code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Physics:**
- Gravity pulls lander down constantly
- Thrust applies force in lander's facing direction
- Fuel is limited
- Must land softly on target (low velocity)

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal numbers):**
Initial map:
```
width height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y target_x target_y
map rows (. = empty, # = obstacle, S = start, T = target, L = landing zone)
```

Then you recieve continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds):
```
x y vx vy angle angular_vel fuel_remaining
```
x and y are in meters, from the worlds top left, and y increases downward.
vx and vy are in m/s.
angle is in radians, 0 is facing up.
angular_vel is in radians per second. + is clockwise, - is counterclockwise.
fuel_remaining is seconds at max_thrust.

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following instructions at a frequency of at least 20hz:
```
thrust turn
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max thrust)
- turn: -1.0 to 1.0 (turning rate, negative = left, positive = right)

To be crystal clear: that's two decimal numbers seperated by a space, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (so it misses 2 consecutive updates), the engine
will automatically cut power. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

Map size can vary from 100x50 to multi-gb maps, so be efficient with memory and processing.

Thrust and gravity are in m/s/s. 100% engine burn burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

Lander size is approximatly 1x1m, and the 2D map grid size is 1m x 1m.

Turn is in radians per second. The lander can not rotate fully around and will clamp rotation to
facing down if attempted.

Y increases downward. Positive vy means moving downward. Gravity is +g.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 2m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries
- Complete landing within time limit

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into obstacle or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function. Assume only the standard library is available.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Subpasses vary the starting altitude, velocity, and terrain. The AI must handle 2D physics, fuel management, and real-time control to achieve a safe landing.

Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

 0.0000
[100x100x50 - simple 3D landing] End: crashed, Distance: 83.2m, Time: 10.22s

3D Lunar Lander - 100x100x50 - simple 3D landing

Approach: C++ solution for the 3D Lunar Lander benchmark. Design: - Read the header and terrain path. - Parse the terrain file in a background thread. - Keep stdin drained in the main thread and output commands at >= 10 Hz. - Use a simple scale-invariant guidance law: * rotate thrust axis toward a target acceleration vector * descend with a speed limit that tightens near the landing target * hold attitude ...

View C++ Code (6944 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <fstream>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <mutex>
#include <optional>
#include <string>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

static constexpr double kPi = 3.141592653589793238462643383279502884;

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double xx, double yy, double zz) : x(xx), y(yy), z(zz) {}
};

static inline Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return {a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y, a.z + b.z}; }
static inline Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return {a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y, a.z - b.z}; }
static inline Vec3 operator*(const Vec3& a, double s) { return {a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s}; }
static inline Vec3 operator*(double s, const Vec3& a) { return a * s; }
static inline Vec3 operator/(const Vec3& a, double s) { return {a.x / s, a.y / s, a.z / s}; }

static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z; }
static inline double norm2(const Vec3& a) { return dot(a, a); }
static inline double norm(const Vec3& a) { return sqrt(norm2(a)); }

static inline Vec3 normalize(const Vec3& v) {
    double n = norm(v);
    if (n < 1e-12) return {0, 0, 1};
    return v / n;
}

static inline double clampd(double v, double lo, double hi) {
    return max(lo, min(hi, v));
}

static inline double signnz(double v) { return (v > 0) - (v < 0); }

static inline double wrap_pi(double a) {
    while (a > kPi) a -= 2.0 * kPi;
    while (a < -kPi) a += 2.0 * kPi;
    return a;
}

struct State {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    double vx = 0, vy = 0, vz = 0;
    double pitch = 0, yaw = 0, roll = 0;
    double pitch_rate = 0, yaw_rate = 0, roll_rate = 0;
    double fuel = 0;
};

struct TerrainMeta {
    int width = 0, depth = 0, height = 0;
    string path;
};

static Vec3 thrust_dir_from_angles(double pitch, double yaw) {
    // yaw=0,pitch=0 => +Z. Pitch positive tilts toward -X.
    double cp = cos(pitch);
    return { -sin(pitch) * cos(yaw), -sin(pitch) * sin(yaw), cp };
}

static void terrain_loader(TerrainMeta meta, atomic<bool>& ready) {
    ifstream f(meta.path, ios::binary);
    if (!f) {
        ready.store(true, memory_order_release);
        return;
    }
    vector<uint16_t> buf;
    buf.reserve(static_cast<size_t>(meta.width) * static_cast<size_t>(meta.depth) * 3);
    char bytes[2];
    while (f.read(bytes, 2)) {
        uint16_t v = static_cast<unsigned char>(bytes[0]) | (static_cast<unsigned char>(bytes[1]) << 8);
        buf.push_back(v);
    }
    ready.store(true, memory_order_release);
}

static bool read_state(State& s) {
    return static_cast<bool>(cin >> s.x >> s.y >> s.z >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                              >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                              >> s.pitch_rate >> s.yaw_rate >> s.roll_rate
                              >> s.fuel);
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    TerrainMeta meta;
    double gravity = 1.62;
    double max_thrust = 1.0;
    double fuel_capacity = 0.0;
    double start_x, start_y, start_z;
    double target_x, target_y, target_z;

    if (!(cin >> meta.width >> meta.depth >> meta.height >> gravity >> max_thrust >> fuel_capacity)) {
        return 0;
    }
    // The prompt's header repeats a field name; the sixth number is fuel capacity.
    // We do not need it here, but we must consume the stream correctly.
    cin >> start_x >> start_y >> start_z >> target_x >> target_y >> target_z;
    cin >> ws;
    getline(cin, meta.path);
    if (meta.path.empty() && cin.good()) getline(cin, meta.path);

    atomic<bool> terrain_ready{false};
    thread loader(terrain_loader, meta, ref(terrain_ready));
    loader.detach();

    const Vec3 target(target_x, target_y, target_z);
    const double landing_radius = max(5.0, min(meta.width, meta.depth) * 0.02);

    State s;
    auto last_print = chrono::steady_clock::now();

    while (read_state(s)) {
        Vec3 pos(s.x, s.y, s.z);
        Vec3 vel(s.vx, s.vy, s.vz);
        Vec3 to_target = target - pos;
        double dist_xy = hypot(to_target.x, to_target.y);
        double vertical_speed = s.vz;

        double height_error = max(0.0, pos.z - target.z);
        double near_factor = clampd(dist_xy / max(landing_radius, 1.0), 0.0, 1.0);

        double desired_vz = -clampd(0.4 + 0.08 * sqrt(max(0.0, height_error)), 0.35, 6.0);
        if (dist_xy < landing_radius * 2.5) {
            desired_vz = -clampd(0.25 + 0.05 * sqrt(max(0.0, height_error)), 0.2, 3.0);
        }
        if (height_error < 25.0) desired_vz = max(desired_vz, -1.5);

        Vec3 desired_acc(0, 0, gravity);

        Vec3 horiz_correction = { -0.08 * to_target.x - 0.35 * vel.x, -0.08 * to_target.y - 0.35 * vel.y, 0 };
        if (dist_xy < landing_radius * 4.0) {
            horiz_correction = horiz_correction * (0.4 + 0.15 * near_factor);
        }

        double vz_error = desired_vz - vertical_speed;
        desired_acc.z += clampd(1.8 * vz_error, -0.2, max_thrust);
        desired_acc.x += horiz_correction.x;
        desired_acc.y += horiz_correction.y;

        double acc_mag = norm(desired_acc);
        if (acc_mag < 1e-6) desired_acc = {0, 0, gravity};
        else desired_acc = desired_acc / acc_mag * clampd(acc_mag, 0.0, max_thrust);

        Vec3 thrust_dir = thrust_dir_from_angles(s.pitch, s.yaw);
        Vec3 desired_dir = normalize(desired_acc);

        Vec3 axis = {thrust_dir.y * desired_dir.z - thrust_dir.z * desired_dir.y,
                     thrust_dir.z * desired_dir.x - thrust_dir.x * desired_dir.z,
                     thrust_dir.x * desired_dir.y - thrust_dir.y * desired_dir.x};

        double yaw_cmd = clampd(3.0 * axis.z - 0.8 * s.yaw_rate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double pitch_cmd = clampd(3.0 * (axis.y * cos(s.yaw) - axis.x * sin(s.yaw)) - 0.8 * s.pitch_rate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double roll_cmd = clampd(-0.6 * s.roll - 0.7 * s.roll_rate, -1.0, 1.0);

        double thrust = 0.0;
        double desired_up_acc = dot(desired_acc, Vec3{0, 0, 1});
        double alignment = clampd(dot(thrust_dir, desired_dir), 0.0, 1.0);
        if (s.fuel > 0.0) {
            thrust = clampd((desired_up_acc / max_thrust) / max(0.25, alignment), 0.0, 1.0);
            if (dist_xy < landing_radius * 1.5 && height_error < 10.0) {
                thrust = min(1.0, thrust + 0.2);
            }
        }

        auto now = chrono::steady_clock::now();
        auto elapsed = chrono::duration_cast<chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_print).count();
        if (elapsed < 90) {
            this_thread::sleep_for(chrono::milliseconds(90 - elapsed));
        }
        last_print = chrono::steady_clock::now();

        cout.setf(ios::fixed);
        cout.precision(6);
        cout << thrust << ' ' << pitch_cmd << ' ' << yaw_cmd << ' ' << roll_cmd << '\n';
        cout.flush();
    }

    return 0;
}

[200x200x100 - short 3D hop] End: crashed, Distance: 179.1m, Time: 11.62s

3D Lunar Lander - 200x200x100 - short 3D hop

Approach: C++ solution for the 3D Lunar Lander benchmark. Design: - Read the header and terrain path. - Parse the terrain file in a background thread. - Keep stdin drained in the main thread and output commands at >= 10 Hz. - Use a simple scale-invariant guidance law: * rotate thrust axis toward a target acceleration vector * descend with a speed limit that tightens near the landing target * hold attitude ...

View C++ Code (6944 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <fstream>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <mutex>
#include <optional>
#include <string>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

static constexpr double kPi = 3.141592653589793238462643383279502884;

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double xx, double yy, double zz) : x(xx), y(yy), z(zz) {}
};

static inline Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return {a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y, a.z + b.z}; }
static inline Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return {a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y, a.z - b.z}; }
static inline Vec3 operator*(const Vec3& a, double s) { return {a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s}; }
static inline Vec3 operator*(double s, const Vec3& a) { return a * s; }
static inline Vec3 operator/(const Vec3& a, double s) { return {a.x / s, a.y / s, a.z / s}; }

static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z; }
static inline double norm2(const Vec3& a) { return dot(a, a); }
static inline double norm(const Vec3& a) { return sqrt(norm2(a)); }

static inline Vec3 normalize(const Vec3& v) {
    double n = norm(v);
    if (n < 1e-12) return {0, 0, 1};
    return v / n;
}

static inline double clampd(double v, double lo, double hi) {
    return max(lo, min(hi, v));
}

static inline double signnz(double v) { return (v > 0) - (v < 0); }

static inline double wrap_pi(double a) {
    while (a > kPi) a -= 2.0 * kPi;
    while (a < -kPi) a += 2.0 * kPi;
    return a;
}

struct State {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    double vx = 0, vy = 0, vz = 0;
    double pitch = 0, yaw = 0, roll = 0;
    double pitch_rate = 0, yaw_rate = 0, roll_rate = 0;
    double fuel = 0;
};

struct TerrainMeta {
    int width = 0, depth = 0, height = 0;
    string path;
};

static Vec3 thrust_dir_from_angles(double pitch, double yaw) {
    // yaw=0,pitch=0 => +Z. Pitch positive tilts toward -X.
    double cp = cos(pitch);
    return { -sin(pitch) * cos(yaw), -sin(pitch) * sin(yaw), cp };
}

static void terrain_loader(TerrainMeta meta, atomic<bool>& ready) {
    ifstream f(meta.path, ios::binary);
    if (!f) {
        ready.store(true, memory_order_release);
        return;
    }
    vector<uint16_t> buf;
    buf.reserve(static_cast<size_t>(meta.width) * static_cast<size_t>(meta.depth) * 3);
    char bytes[2];
    while (f.read(bytes, 2)) {
        uint16_t v = static_cast<unsigned char>(bytes[0]) | (static_cast<unsigned char>(bytes[1]) << 8);
        buf.push_back(v);
    }
    ready.store(true, memory_order_release);
}

static bool read_state(State& s) {
    return static_cast<bool>(cin >> s.x >> s.y >> s.z >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                              >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                              >> s.pitch_rate >> s.yaw_rate >> s.roll_rate
                              >> s.fuel);
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    TerrainMeta meta;
    double gravity = 1.62;
    double max_thrust = 1.0;
    double fuel_capacity = 0.0;
    double start_x, start_y, start_z;
    double target_x, target_y, target_z;

    if (!(cin >> meta.width >> meta.depth >> meta.height >> gravity >> max_thrust >> fuel_capacity)) {
        return 0;
    }
    // The prompt's header repeats a field name; the sixth number is fuel capacity.
    // We do not need it here, but we must consume the stream correctly.
    cin >> start_x >> start_y >> start_z >> target_x >> target_y >> target_z;
    cin >> ws;
    getline(cin, meta.path);
    if (meta.path.empty() && cin.good()) getline(cin, meta.path);

    atomic<bool> terrain_ready{false};
    thread loader(terrain_loader, meta, ref(terrain_ready));
    loader.detach();

    const Vec3 target(target_x, target_y, target_z);
    const double landing_radius = max(5.0, min(meta.width, meta.depth) * 0.02);

    State s;
    auto last_print = chrono::steady_clock::now();

    while (read_state(s)) {
        Vec3 pos(s.x, s.y, s.z);
        Vec3 vel(s.vx, s.vy, s.vz);
        Vec3 to_target = target - pos;
        double dist_xy = hypot(to_target.x, to_target.y);
        double vertical_speed = s.vz;

        double height_error = max(0.0, pos.z - target.z);
        double near_factor = clampd(dist_xy / max(landing_radius, 1.0), 0.0, 1.0);

        double desired_vz = -clampd(0.4 + 0.08 * sqrt(max(0.0, height_error)), 0.35, 6.0);
        if (dist_xy < landing_radius * 2.5) {
            desired_vz = -clampd(0.25 + 0.05 * sqrt(max(0.0, height_error)), 0.2, 3.0);
        }
        if (height_error < 25.0) desired_vz = max(desired_vz, -1.5);

        Vec3 desired_acc(0, 0, gravity);

        Vec3 horiz_correction = { -0.08 * to_target.x - 0.35 * vel.x, -0.08 * to_target.y - 0.35 * vel.y, 0 };
        if (dist_xy < landing_radius * 4.0) {
            horiz_correction = horiz_correction * (0.4 + 0.15 * near_factor);
        }

        double vz_error = desired_vz - vertical_speed;
        desired_acc.z += clampd(1.8 * vz_error, -0.2, max_thrust);
        desired_acc.x += horiz_correction.x;
        desired_acc.y += horiz_correction.y;

        double acc_mag = norm(desired_acc);
        if (acc_mag < 1e-6) desired_acc = {0, 0, gravity};
        else desired_acc = desired_acc / acc_mag * clampd(acc_mag, 0.0, max_thrust);

        Vec3 thrust_dir = thrust_dir_from_angles(s.pitch, s.yaw);
        Vec3 desired_dir = normalize(desired_acc);

        Vec3 axis = {thrust_dir.y * desired_dir.z - thrust_dir.z * desired_dir.y,
                     thrust_dir.z * desired_dir.x - thrust_dir.x * desired_dir.z,
                     thrust_dir.x * desired_dir.y - thrust_dir.y * desired_dir.x};

        double yaw_cmd = clampd(3.0 * axis.z - 0.8 * s.yaw_rate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double pitch_cmd = clampd(3.0 * (axis.y * cos(s.yaw) - axis.x * sin(s.yaw)) - 0.8 * s.pitch_rate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double roll_cmd = clampd(-0.6 * s.roll - 0.7 * s.roll_rate, -1.0, 1.0);

        double thrust = 0.0;
        double desired_up_acc = dot(desired_acc, Vec3{0, 0, 1});
        double alignment = clampd(dot(thrust_dir, desired_dir), 0.0, 1.0);
        if (s.fuel > 0.0) {
            thrust = clampd((desired_up_acc / max_thrust) / max(0.25, alignment), 0.0, 1.0);
            if (dist_xy < landing_radius * 1.5 && height_error < 10.0) {
                thrust = min(1.0, thrust + 0.2);
            }
        }

        auto now = chrono::steady_clock::now();
        auto elapsed = chrono::duration_cast<chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_print).count();
        if (elapsed < 90) {
            this_thread::sleep_for(chrono::milliseconds(90 - elapsed));
        }
        last_print = chrono::steady_clock::now();

        cout.setf(ios::fixed);
        cout.precision(6);
        cout << thrust << ' ' << pitch_cmd << ' ' << yaw_cmd << ' ' << roll_cmd << '\n';
        cout.flush();
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with C# .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable warning times and delta-v budgets
4. Complete analysis within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying mission complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.

Launch an interceptor spacecraft to collide with a dangerous asteroid before it reaches Earth. The AI must plan a trajectory through the solar system, accounting for the gravitational pull of the Sun and planets, to hit a moving target millions of kilometres away.

Harder subpasses give less warning time or tighter fuel budgets, potentially requiring planetary gravity assists (slingshots) to reach the asteroid. The AI must implement orbital mechanics and trajectory optimisation from scratch.

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 1.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

 0.2000
[10K points, 10 clusters] 9990 invalid cluster indices

3D Point Clustering - 10K points, 10 clusters

View Rust Code (9168 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Centroid {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn dist2_point_centroid(p: Point, c: Centroid) -> f64 {
    let dx = p.x as f64 - c.x;
    let dy = p.y as f64 - c.y;
    let dz = p.z as f64 - c.z;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn dist2_point_point(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x as f64 - b.x as f64;
    let dy = a.y as f64 - b.y as f64;
    let dz = a.z as f64 - b.z as f64;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn choose_initial_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<Centroid> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return centroids;
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[0].x as f64,
        y: points[0].y as f64,
        z: points[0].z as f64,
    });
    if k == 1 {
        return centroids;
    }

    let mut farthest_idx = 0usize;
    let mut farthest_dist = -1.0f64;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist2_point_point(p, points[0]);
        if d > farthest_dist {
            farthest_dist = d;
            farthest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[farthest_idx].x as f64,
        y: points[farthest_idx].y as f64,
        z: points[farthest_idx].z as f64,
    });

    let mut min_dists = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        min_dists[i] = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[0]).min(dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[1]));
    }

    while centroids.len() < k {
        let mut best_idx = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = -1.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            if min_dists[i] > best_dist {
                best_dist = min_dists[i];
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[best_idx].x as f64,
            y: points[best_idx].y as f64,
            z: points[best_idx].z as f64,
        });
        let last = *centroids.last().unwrap();
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], last);
            if d < min_dists[i] {
                min_dists[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }

    centroids
}

fn assign_points(points: &[Point], centroids: &[Centroid], labels: &mut [u32]) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..centroids.len() {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        if labels[i] != best as u32 {
            labels[i] = best as u32;
            changed = true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

fn recompute_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize, labels: &[u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let mut sum_x = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_y = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_z = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut count = vec![0u64; k];

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let c = labels[i] as usize;
        sum_x[c] += p.x as f64;
        sum_y[c] += p.y as f64;
        sum_z[c] += p.z as f64;
        count[c] += 1;
    }

    for c in 0..k {
        if count[c] > 0 {
            let inv = 1.0f64 / (count[c] as f64);
            centroids[c] = Centroid {
                x: sum_x[c] * inv,
                y: sum_y[c] * inv,
                z: sum_z[c] * inv,
            };
        }
    }
}

fn fix_empty_clusters(points: &[Point], labels: &mut [u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let k = centroids.len();
    if k == 0 || points.is_empty() {
        return;
    }
    let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];
    for &l in labels.iter() {
        counts[l as usize] += 1;
    }

    let mut empties = Vec::new();
    for c in 0..k {
        if counts[c] == 0 {
            empties.push(c);
        }
    }
    if empties.is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let mut farthest_points: Vec<(f64, usize)> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, &l)| {
        let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[l as usize]);
        (d, i)
    }).collect();
    farthest_points.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for &empty in &empties {
        while idx < farthest_points.len() {
            let pi = farthest_points[idx].1;
            let old = labels[pi] as usize;
            if counts[old] > 1 {
                counts[old] -= 1;
                labels[pi] = empty as u32;
                centroids[empty] = Centroid {
                    x: points[pi].x as f64,
                    y: points[pi].y as f64,
                    z: points[pi].z as f64,
                };
                counts[empty] = 1;
                idx += 1;
                break;
            }
            idx += 1;
        }
    }
}

fn kmeans(points: &[Point], k: usize, max_iters: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(points, k);
    if centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
    }

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let changed = assign_points(points, &centroids, &mut labels);
        fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
        recompute_centroids(points, k, &labels, &mut centroids);
        if !changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    labels
}

fn streaming_assign(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    let seed_count = n.min(k.max(1));
    for i in 0..seed_count {
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[i].x as f64,
            y: points[i].y as f64,
            z: points[i].z as f64,
        });
    }
    while centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.push(centroids[centroids.len() % seed_count]);
    }

    let mut counts = vec![1u64; k];
    for i in seed_count..n {
        let p = points[i];
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..k {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        labels[i] = best as u32;
        let cnt = counts[best] as f64;
        let eta = 1.0 / (cnt + 1.0);
        centroids[best].x = centroids[best].x + (p.x as f64 - centroids[best].x) * eta;
        centroids[best].y = centroids[best].y + (p.y as f64 - centroids[best].y) * eta;
        centroids[best].z = centroids[best].z + (p.z as f64 - centroids[best].z) * eta;
        counts[best] += 1;
    }

    fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
    labels
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();
    if input.len() < 8 {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([input[4], input[5], input[6], input[7]]) as usize;
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut offset = 8usize;
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if offset + 12 > input.len() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset], input[offset + 1], input[offset + 2], input[offset + 3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 4], input[offset + 5], input[offset + 6], input[offset + 7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 8], input[offset + 9], input[offset + 10], input[offset + 11]]);
        points.push(Point { x, y, z });
        offset += 12;
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let k = num_clusters.max(1).min(n.max(1));

    let labels = if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&points, k, 15)
    } else if n <= 100_000_000 {
        let mut lab = streaming_assign(&points, k);
        let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(&points, k);
        if centroids.len() < k && !centroids.is_empty() {
            centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
        }
        for _ in 0..5 {
            recompute_centroids(&points, k, &lab, &mut centroids);
            let changed = assign_points(&points, &centroids, &mut lab);
            fix_empty_clusters(&points, &mut lab, &mut centroids);
            if !changed {
                break;
            }
        }
        lab
    } else {
        streaming_assign(&points, k)
    };

    let mut out = BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for label in labels {
        out.write_all(&label.to_le_bytes()).unwrap();
    }
    out.flush().unwrap();
}
📊 Clustering Diagram
Subpass 1
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought
 0.2000
[100K points, 25 clusters] 99975 invalid cluster indices

3D Point Clustering - 100K points, 25 clusters

View Rust Code (9168 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Centroid {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn dist2_point_centroid(p: Point, c: Centroid) -> f64 {
    let dx = p.x as f64 - c.x;
    let dy = p.y as f64 - c.y;
    let dz = p.z as f64 - c.z;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn dist2_point_point(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x as f64 - b.x as f64;
    let dy = a.y as f64 - b.y as f64;
    let dz = a.z as f64 - b.z as f64;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn choose_initial_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<Centroid> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return centroids;
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[0].x as f64,
        y: points[0].y as f64,
        z: points[0].z as f64,
    });
    if k == 1 {
        return centroids;
    }

    let mut farthest_idx = 0usize;
    let mut farthest_dist = -1.0f64;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist2_point_point(p, points[0]);
        if d > farthest_dist {
            farthest_dist = d;
            farthest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[farthest_idx].x as f64,
        y: points[farthest_idx].y as f64,
        z: points[farthest_idx].z as f64,
    });

    let mut min_dists = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        min_dists[i] = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[0]).min(dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[1]));
    }

    while centroids.len() < k {
        let mut best_idx = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = -1.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            if min_dists[i] > best_dist {
                best_dist = min_dists[i];
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[best_idx].x as f64,
            y: points[best_idx].y as f64,
            z: points[best_idx].z as f64,
        });
        let last = *centroids.last().unwrap();
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], last);
            if d < min_dists[i] {
                min_dists[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }

    centroids
}

fn assign_points(points: &[Point], centroids: &[Centroid], labels: &mut [u32]) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..centroids.len() {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        if labels[i] != best as u32 {
            labels[i] = best as u32;
            changed = true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

fn recompute_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize, labels: &[u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let mut sum_x = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_y = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_z = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut count = vec![0u64; k];

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let c = labels[i] as usize;
        sum_x[c] += p.x as f64;
        sum_y[c] += p.y as f64;
        sum_z[c] += p.z as f64;
        count[c] += 1;
    }

    for c in 0..k {
        if count[c] > 0 {
            let inv = 1.0f64 / (count[c] as f64);
            centroids[c] = Centroid {
                x: sum_x[c] * inv,
                y: sum_y[c] * inv,
                z: sum_z[c] * inv,
            };
        }
    }
}

fn fix_empty_clusters(points: &[Point], labels: &mut [u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let k = centroids.len();
    if k == 0 || points.is_empty() {
        return;
    }
    let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];
    for &l in labels.iter() {
        counts[l as usize] += 1;
    }

    let mut empties = Vec::new();
    for c in 0..k {
        if counts[c] == 0 {
            empties.push(c);
        }
    }
    if empties.is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let mut farthest_points: Vec<(f64, usize)> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, &l)| {
        let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[l as usize]);
        (d, i)
    }).collect();
    farthest_points.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for &empty in &empties {
        while idx < farthest_points.len() {
            let pi = farthest_points[idx].1;
            let old = labels[pi] as usize;
            if counts[old] > 1 {
                counts[old] -= 1;
                labels[pi] = empty as u32;
                centroids[empty] = Centroid {
                    x: points[pi].x as f64,
                    y: points[pi].y as f64,
                    z: points[pi].z as f64,
                };
                counts[empty] = 1;
                idx += 1;
                break;
            }
            idx += 1;
        }
    }
}

fn kmeans(points: &[Point], k: usize, max_iters: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(points, k);
    if centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
    }

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let changed = assign_points(points, &centroids, &mut labels);
        fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
        recompute_centroids(points, k, &labels, &mut centroids);
        if !changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    labels
}

fn streaming_assign(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    let seed_count = n.min(k.max(1));
    for i in 0..seed_count {
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[i].x as f64,
            y: points[i].y as f64,
            z: points[i].z as f64,
        });
    }
    while centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.push(centroids[centroids.len() % seed_count]);
    }

    let mut counts = vec![1u64; k];
    for i in seed_count..n {
        let p = points[i];
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..k {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        labels[i] = best as u32;
        let cnt = counts[best] as f64;
        let eta = 1.0 / (cnt + 1.0);
        centroids[best].x = centroids[best].x + (p.x as f64 - centroids[best].x) * eta;
        centroids[best].y = centroids[best].y + (p.y as f64 - centroids[best].y) * eta;
        centroids[best].z = centroids[best].z + (p.z as f64 - centroids[best].z) * eta;
        counts[best] += 1;
    }

    fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
    labels
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();
    if input.len() < 8 {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([input[4], input[5], input[6], input[7]]) as usize;
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut offset = 8usize;
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if offset + 12 > input.len() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset], input[offset + 1], input[offset + 2], input[offset + 3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 4], input[offset + 5], input[offset + 6], input[offset + 7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 8], input[offset + 9], input[offset + 10], input[offset + 11]]);
        points.push(Point { x, y, z });
        offset += 12;
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let k = num_clusters.max(1).min(n.max(1));

    let labels = if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&points, k, 15)
    } else if n <= 100_000_000 {
        let mut lab = streaming_assign(&points, k);
        let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(&points, k);
        if centroids.len() < k && !centroids.is_empty() {
            centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
        }
        for _ in 0..5 {
            recompute_centroids(&points, k, &lab, &mut centroids);
            let changed = assign_points(&points, &centroids, &mut lab);
            fix_empty_clusters(&points, &mut lab, &mut centroids);
            if !changed {
                break;
            }
        }
        lab
    } else {
        streaming_assign(&points, k)
    };

    let mut out = BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for label in labels {
        out.write_all(&label.to_le_bytes()).unwrap();
    }
    out.flush().unwrap();
}
