3D Point Clustering - 1M points, 50 clusters (~12MB)

View Rust Code (9168 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Centroid {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn dist2_point_centroid(p: Point, c: Centroid) -> f64 {
    let dx = p.x as f64 - c.x;
    let dy = p.y as f64 - c.y;
    let dz = p.z as f64 - c.z;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn dist2_point_point(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x as f64 - b.x as f64;
    let dy = a.y as f64 - b.y as f64;
    let dz = a.z as f64 - b.z as f64;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn choose_initial_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<Centroid> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return centroids;
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[0].x as f64,
        y: points[0].y as f64,
        z: points[0].z as f64,
    });
    if k == 1 {
        return centroids;
    }

    let mut farthest_idx = 0usize;
    let mut farthest_dist = -1.0f64;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist2_point_point(p, points[0]);
        if d > farthest_dist {
            farthest_dist = d;
            farthest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[farthest_idx].x as f64,
        y: points[farthest_idx].y as f64,
        z: points[farthest_idx].z as f64,
    });

    let mut min_dists = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        min_dists[i] = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[0]).min(dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[1]));
    }

    while centroids.len() < k {
        let mut best_idx = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = -1.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            if min_dists[i] > best_dist {
                best_dist = min_dists[i];
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[best_idx].x as f64,
            y: points[best_idx].y as f64,
            z: points[best_idx].z as f64,
        });
        let last = *centroids.last().unwrap();
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], last);
            if d < min_dists[i] {
                min_dists[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }

    centroids
}

fn assign_points(points: &[Point], centroids: &[Centroid], labels: &mut [u32]) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..centroids.len() {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        if labels[i] != best as u32 {
            labels[i] = best as u32;
            changed = true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

fn recompute_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize, labels: &[u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let mut sum_x = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_y = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_z = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut count = vec![0u64; k];

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let c = labels[i] as usize;
        sum_x[c] += p.x as f64;
        sum_y[c] += p.y as f64;
        sum_z[c] += p.z as f64;
        count[c] += 1;
    }

    for c in 0..k {
        if count[c] > 0 {
            let inv = 1.0f64 / (count[c] as f64);
            centroids[c] = Centroid {
                x: sum_x[c] * inv,
                y: sum_y[c] * inv,
                z: sum_z[c] * inv,
            };
        }
    }
}

fn fix_empty_clusters(points: &[Point], labels: &mut [u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let k = centroids.len();
    if k == 0 || points.is_empty() {
        return;
    }
    let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];
    for &l in labels.iter() {
        counts[l as usize] += 1;
    }

    let mut empties = Vec::new();
    for c in 0..k {
        if counts[c] == 0 {
            empties.push(c);
        }
    }
    if empties.is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let mut farthest_points: Vec<(f64, usize)> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, &l)| {
        let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[l as usize]);
        (d, i)
    }).collect();
    farthest_points.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for &empty in &empties {
        while idx < farthest_points.len() {
            let pi = farthest_points[idx].1;
            let old = labels[pi] as usize;
            if counts[old] > 1 {
                counts[old] -= 1;
                labels[pi] = empty as u32;
                centroids[empty] = Centroid {
                    x: points[pi].x as f64,
                    y: points[pi].y as f64,
                    z: points[pi].z as f64,
                };
                counts[empty] = 1;
                idx += 1;
                break;
            }
            idx += 1;
        }
    }
}

fn kmeans(points: &[Point], k: usize, max_iters: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(points, k);
    if centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
    }

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let changed = assign_points(points, &centroids, &mut labels);
        fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
        recompute_centroids(points, k, &labels, &mut centroids);
        if !changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    labels
}

fn streaming_assign(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    let seed_count = n.min(k.max(1));
    for i in 0..seed_count {
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[i].x as f64,
            y: points[i].y as f64,
            z: points[i].z as f64,
        });
    }
    while centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.push(centroids[centroids.len() % seed_count]);
    }

    let mut counts = vec![1u64; k];
    for i in seed_count..n {
        let p = points[i];
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..k {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        labels[i] = best as u32;
        let cnt = counts[best] as f64;
        let eta = 1.0 / (cnt + 1.0);
        centroids[best].x = centroids[best].x + (p.x as f64 - centroids[best].x) * eta;
        centroids[best].y = centroids[best].y + (p.y as f64 - centroids[best].y) * eta;
        centroids[best].z = centroids[best].z + (p.z as f64 - centroids[best].z) * eta;
        counts[best] += 1;
    }

    fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
    labels
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();
    if input.len() < 8 {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([input[4], input[5], input[6], input[7]]) as usize;
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut offset = 8usize;
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if offset + 12 > input.len() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset], input[offset + 1], input[offset + 2], input[offset + 3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 4], input[offset + 5], input[offset + 6], input[offset + 7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 8], input[offset + 9], input[offset + 10], input[offset + 11]]);
        points.push(Point { x, y, z });
        offset += 12;
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let k = num_clusters.max(1).min(n.max(1));

    let labels = if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&points, k, 15)
    } else if n <= 100_000_000 {
        let mut lab = streaming_assign(&points, k);
        let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(&points, k);
        if centroids.len() < k && !centroids.is_empty() {
            centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
        }
        for _ in 0..5 {
            recompute_centroids(&points, k, &lab, &mut centroids);
            let changed = assign_points(&points, &centroids, &mut lab);
            fix_empty_clusters(&points, &mut lab, &mut centroids);
            if !changed {
                break;
            }
        }
        lab
    } else {
        streaming_assign(&points, k)
    };

    let mut out = BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for label in labels {
        out.write_all(&label.to_le_bytes()).unwrap();
    }
    out.flush().unwrap();
}
📊 Clustering Diagram
Subpass 3
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought
 0.2000
[5M points, 100 clusters (~60MB)] 4999900 invalid cluster indices

3D Point Clustering - 5M points, 100 clusters (~60MB)

View Rust Code (9168 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Centroid {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn dist2_point_centroid(p: Point, c: Centroid) -> f64 {
    let dx = p.x as f64 - c.x;
    let dy = p.y as f64 - c.y;
    let dz = p.z as f64 - c.z;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn dist2_point_point(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x as f64 - b.x as f64;
    let dy = a.y as f64 - b.y as f64;
    let dz = a.z as f64 - b.z as f64;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn choose_initial_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<Centroid> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return centroids;
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[0].x as f64,
        y: points[0].y as f64,
        z: points[0].z as f64,
    });
    if k == 1 {
        return centroids;
    }

    let mut farthest_idx = 0usize;
    let mut farthest_dist = -1.0f64;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist2_point_point(p, points[0]);
        if d > farthest_dist {
            farthest_dist = d;
            farthest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[farthest_idx].x as f64,
        y: points[farthest_idx].y as f64,
        z: points[farthest_idx].z as f64,
    });

    let mut min_dists = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        min_dists[i] = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[0]).min(dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[1]));
    }

    while centroids.len() < k {
        let mut best_idx = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = -1.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            if min_dists[i] > best_dist {
                best_dist = min_dists[i];
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[best_idx].x as f64,
            y: points[best_idx].y as f64,
            z: points[best_idx].z as f64,
        });
        let last = *centroids.last().unwrap();
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], last);
            if d < min_dists[i] {
                min_dists[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }

    centroids
}

fn assign_points(points: &[Point], centroids: &[Centroid], labels: &mut [u32]) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..centroids.len() {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        if labels[i] != best as u32 {
            labels[i] = best as u32;
            changed = true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

fn recompute_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize, labels: &[u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let mut sum_x = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_y = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_z = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut count = vec![0u64; k];

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let c = labels[i] as usize;
        sum_x[c] += p.x as f64;
        sum_y[c] += p.y as f64;
        sum_z[c] += p.z as f64;
        count[c] += 1;
    }

    for c in 0..k {
        if count[c] > 0 {
            let inv = 1.0f64 / (count[c] as f64);
            centroids[c] = Centroid {
                x: sum_x[c] * inv,
                y: sum_y[c] * inv,
                z: sum_z[c] * inv,
            };
        }
    }
}

fn fix_empty_clusters(points: &[Point], labels: &mut [u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let k = centroids.len();
    if k == 0 || points.is_empty() {
        return;
    }
    let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];
    for &l in labels.iter() {
        counts[l as usize] += 1;
    }

    let mut empties = Vec::new();
    for c in 0..k {
        if counts[c] == 0 {
            empties.push(c);
        }
    }
    if empties.is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let mut farthest_points: Vec<(f64, usize)> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, &l)| {
        let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[l as usize]);
        (d, i)
    }).collect();
    farthest_points.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for &empty in &empties {
        while idx < farthest_points.len() {
            let pi = farthest_points[idx].1;
            let old = labels[pi] as usize;
            if counts[old] > 1 {
                counts[old] -= 1;
                labels[pi] = empty as u32;
                centroids[empty] = Centroid {
                    x: points[pi].x as f64,
                    y: points[pi].y as f64,
                    z: points[pi].z as f64,
                };
                counts[empty] = 1;
                idx += 1;
                break;
            }
            idx += 1;
        }
    }
}

fn kmeans(points: &[Point], k: usize, max_iters: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(points, k);
    if centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
    }

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let changed = assign_points(points, &centroids, &mut labels);
        fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
        recompute_centroids(points, k, &labels, &mut centroids);
        if !changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    labels
}

fn streaming_assign(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    let seed_count = n.min(k.max(1));
    for i in 0..seed_count {
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[i].x as f64,
            y: points[i].y as f64,
            z: points[i].z as f64,
        });
    }
    while centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.push(centroids[centroids.len() % seed_count]);
    }

    let mut counts = vec![1u64; k];
    for i in seed_count..n {
        let p = points[i];
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..k {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        labels[i] = best as u32;
        let cnt = counts[best] as f64;
        let eta = 1.0 / (cnt + 1.0);
        centroids[best].x = centroids[best].x + (p.x as f64 - centroids[best].x) * eta;
        centroids[best].y = centroids[best].y + (p.y as f64 - centroids[best].y) * eta;
        centroids[best].z = centroids[best].z + (p.z as f64 - centroids[best].z) * eta;
        counts[best] += 1;
    }

    fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
    labels
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();
    if input.len() < 8 {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([input[4], input[5], input[6], input[7]]) as usize;
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut offset = 8usize;
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if offset + 12 > input.len() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset], input[offset + 1], input[offset + 2], input[offset + 3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 4], input[offset + 5], input[offset + 6], input[offset + 7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 8], input[offset + 9], input[offset + 10], input[offset + 11]]);
        points.push(Point { x, y, z });
        offset += 12;
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let k = num_clusters.max(1).min(n.max(1));

    let labels = if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&points, k, 15)
    } else if n <= 100_000_000 {
        let mut lab = streaming_assign(&points, k);
        let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(&points, k);
        if centroids.len() < k && !centroids.is_empty() {
            centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
        }
        for _ in 0..5 {
            recompute_centroids(&points, k, &lab, &mut centroids);
            let changed = assign_points(&points, &centroids, &mut lab);
            fix_empty_clusters(&points, &mut lab, &mut centroids);
            if !changed {
                break;
            }
        }
        lab
    } else {
        streaming_assign(&points, k)
    };

    let mut out = BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for label in labels {
        out.write_all(&label.to_le_bytes()).unwrap();
    }
    out.flush().unwrap();
}

3D Point Clustering - 10M points, 200 clusters (~120MB)

View Rust Code (9168 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Centroid {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn dist2_point_centroid(p: Point, c: Centroid) -> f64 {
    let dx = p.x as f64 - c.x;
    let dy = p.y as f64 - c.y;
    let dz = p.z as f64 - c.z;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn dist2_point_point(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x as f64 - b.x as f64;
    let dy = a.y as f64 - b.y as f64;
    let dz = a.z as f64 - b.z as f64;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn choose_initial_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<Centroid> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return centroids;
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[0].x as f64,
        y: points[0].y as f64,
        z: points[0].z as f64,
    });
    if k == 1 {
        return centroids;
    }

    let mut farthest_idx = 0usize;
    let mut farthest_dist = -1.0f64;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist2_point_point(p, points[0]);
        if d > farthest_dist {
            farthest_dist = d;
            farthest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[farthest_idx].x as f64,
        y: points[farthest_idx].y as f64,
        z: points[farthest_idx].z as f64,
    });

    let mut min_dists = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        min_dists[i] = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[0]).min(dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[1]));
    }

    while centroids.len() < k {
        let mut best_idx = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = -1.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            if min_dists[i] > best_dist {
                best_dist = min_dists[i];
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[best_idx].x as f64,
            y: points[best_idx].y as f64,
            z: points[best_idx].z as f64,
        });
        let last = *centroids.last().unwrap();
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], last);
            if d < min_dists[i] {
                min_dists[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }

    centroids
}

fn assign_points(points: &[Point], centroids: &[Centroid], labels: &mut [u32]) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..centroids.len() {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        if labels[i] != best as u32 {
            labels[i] = best as u32;
            changed = true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

fn recompute_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize, labels: &[u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let mut sum_x = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_y = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_z = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut count = vec![0u64; k];

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let c = labels[i] as usize;
        sum_x[c] += p.x as f64;
        sum_y[c] += p.y as f64;
        sum_z[c] += p.z as f64;
        count[c] += 1;
    }

    for c in 0..k {
        if count[c] > 0 {
            let inv = 1.0f64 / (count[c] as f64);
            centroids[c] = Centroid {
                x: sum_x[c] * inv,
                y: sum_y[c] * inv,
                z: sum_z[c] * inv,
            };
        }
    }
}

fn fix_empty_clusters(points: &[Point], labels: &mut [u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let k = centroids.len();
    if k == 0 || points.is_empty() {
        return;
    }
    let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];
    for &l in labels.iter() {
        counts[l as usize] += 1;
    }

    let mut empties = Vec::new();
    for c in 0..k {
        if counts[c] == 0 {
            empties.push(c);
        }
    }
    if empties.is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let mut farthest_points: Vec<(f64, usize)> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, &l)| {
        let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[l as usize]);
        (d, i)
    }).collect();
    farthest_points.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for &empty in &empties {
        while idx < farthest_points.len() {
            let pi = farthest_points[idx].1;
            let old = labels[pi] as usize;
            if counts[old] > 1 {
                counts[old] -= 1;
                labels[pi] = empty as u32;
                centroids[empty] = Centroid {
                    x: points[pi].x as f64,
                    y: points[pi].y as f64,
                    z: points[pi].z as f64,
                };
                counts[empty] = 1;
                idx += 1;
                break;
            }
            idx += 1;
        }
    }
}

fn kmeans(points: &[Point], k: usize, max_iters: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(points, k);
    if centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
    }

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let changed = assign_points(points, &centroids, &mut labels);
        fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
        recompute_centroids(points, k, &labels, &mut centroids);
        if !changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    labels
}

fn streaming_assign(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    let seed_count = n.min(k.max(1));
    for i in 0..seed_count {
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[i].x as f64,
            y: points[i].y as f64,
            z: points[i].z as f64,
        });
    }
    while centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.push(centroids[centroids.len() % seed_count]);
    }

    let mut counts = vec![1u64; k];
    for i in seed_count..n {
        let p = points[i];
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..k {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        labels[i] = best as u32;
        let cnt = counts[best] as f64;
        let eta = 1.0 / (cnt + 1.0);
        centroids[best].x = centroids[best].x + (p.x as f64 - centroids[best].x) * eta;
        centroids[best].y = centroids[best].y + (p.y as f64 - centroids[best].y) * eta;
        centroids[best].z = centroids[best].z + (p.z as f64 - centroids[best].z) * eta;
        counts[best] += 1;
    }

    fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
    labels
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();
    if input.len() < 8 {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([input[4], input[5], input[6], input[7]]) as usize;
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut offset = 8usize;
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if offset + 12 > input.len() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset], input[offset + 1], input[offset + 2], input[offset + 3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 4], input[offset + 5], input[offset + 6], input[offset + 7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 8], input[offset + 9], input[offset + 10], input[offset + 11]]);
        points.push(Point { x, y, z });
        offset += 12;
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let k = num_clusters.max(1).min(n.max(1));

    let labels = if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&points, k, 15)
    } else if n <= 100_000_000 {
        let mut lab = streaming_assign(&points, k);
        let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(&points, k);
        if centroids.len() < k && !centroids.is_empty() {
            centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
        }
        for _ in 0..5 {
            recompute_centroids(&points, k, &lab, &mut centroids);
            let changed = assign_points(&points, &centroids, &mut lab);
            fix_empty_clusters(&points, &mut lab, &mut centroids);
            if !changed {
                break;
            }
        }
        lab
    } else {
        streaming_assign(&points, k)
    };

    let mut out = BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for label in labels {
        out.write_all(&label.to_le_bytes()).unwrap();
    }
    out.flush().unwrap();
}

 0.0000
[50M points, 500 clusters (~600MB)] Timeout

3D Point Clustering - 50M points, 500 clusters (~600MB)

View Rust Code (9168 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
struct Centroid {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn dist2_point_centroid(p: Point, c: Centroid) -> f64 {
    let dx = p.x as f64 - c.x;
    let dy = p.y as f64 - c.y;
    let dz = p.z as f64 - c.z;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn dist2_point_point(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x as f64 - b.x as f64;
    let dy = a.y as f64 - b.y as f64;
    let dz = a.z as f64 - b.z as f64;
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

fn choose_initial_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<Centroid> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return centroids;
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[0].x as f64,
        y: points[0].y as f64,
        z: points[0].z as f64,
    });
    if k == 1 {
        return centroids;
    }

    let mut farthest_idx = 0usize;
    let mut farthest_dist = -1.0f64;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist2_point_point(p, points[0]);
        if d > farthest_dist {
            farthest_dist = d;
            farthest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    centroids.push(Centroid {
        x: points[farthest_idx].x as f64,
        y: points[farthest_idx].y as f64,
        z: points[farthest_idx].z as f64,
    });

    let mut min_dists = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        min_dists[i] = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[0]).min(dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[1]));
    }

    while centroids.len() < k {
        let mut best_idx = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = -1.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            if min_dists[i] > best_dist {
                best_dist = min_dists[i];
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[best_idx].x as f64,
            y: points[best_idx].y as f64,
            z: points[best_idx].z as f64,
        });
        let last = *centroids.last().unwrap();
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], last);
            if d < min_dists[i] {
                min_dists[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }

    centroids
}

fn assign_points(points: &[Point], centroids: &[Centroid], labels: &mut [u32]) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..centroids.len() {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        if labels[i] != best as u32 {
            labels[i] = best as u32;
            changed = true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

fn recompute_centroids(points: &[Point], k: usize, labels: &[u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let mut sum_x = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_y = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut sum_z = vec![0.0f64; k];
    let mut count = vec![0u64; k];

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        let c = labels[i] as usize;
        sum_x[c] += p.x as f64;
        sum_y[c] += p.y as f64;
        sum_z[c] += p.z as f64;
        count[c] += 1;
    }

    for c in 0..k {
        if count[c] > 0 {
            let inv = 1.0f64 / (count[c] as f64);
            centroids[c] = Centroid {
                x: sum_x[c] * inv,
                y: sum_y[c] * inv,
                z: sum_z[c] * inv,
            };
        }
    }
}

fn fix_empty_clusters(points: &[Point], labels: &mut [u32], centroids: &mut [Centroid]) {
    let k = centroids.len();
    if k == 0 || points.is_empty() {
        return;
    }
    let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];
    for &l in labels.iter() {
        counts[l as usize] += 1;
    }

    let mut empties = Vec::new();
    for c in 0..k {
        if counts[c] == 0 {
            empties.push(c);
        }
    }
    if empties.is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let mut farthest_points: Vec<(f64, usize)> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, &l)| {
        let d = dist2_point_centroid(points[i], centroids[l as usize]);
        (d, i)
    }).collect();
    farthest_points.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for &empty in &empties {
        while idx < farthest_points.len() {
            let pi = farthest_points[idx].1;
            let old = labels[pi] as usize;
            if counts[old] > 1 {
                counts[old] -= 1;
                labels[pi] = empty as u32;
                centroids[empty] = Centroid {
                    x: points[pi].x as f64,
                    y: points[pi].y as f64,
                    z: points[pi].z as f64,
                };
                counts[empty] = 1;
                idx += 1;
                break;
            }
            idx += 1;
        }
    }
}

fn kmeans(points: &[Point], k: usize, max_iters: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(points, k);
    if centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
    }

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let changed = assign_points(points, &centroids, &mut labels);
        fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
        recompute_centroids(points, k, &labels, &mut centroids);
        if !changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    labels
}

fn streaming_assign(points: &[Point], k: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut labels = vec![0u32; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return labels;
    }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n {
            labels[i] = i as u32;
        }
        return labels;
    }

    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    let seed_count = n.min(k.max(1));
    for i in 0..seed_count {
        centroids.push(Centroid {
            x: points[i].x as f64,
            y: points[i].y as f64,
            z: points[i].z as f64,
        });
    }
    while centroids.len() < k {
        centroids.push(centroids[centroids.len() % seed_count]);
    }

    let mut counts = vec![1u64; k];
    for i in seed_count..n {
        let p = points[i];
        let mut best = 0usize;
        let mut best_dist = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[0]);
        for c in 1..k {
            let d = dist2_point_centroid(p, centroids[c]);
            if d < best_dist {
                best_dist = d;
                best = c;
            }
        }
        labels[i] = best as u32;
        let cnt = counts[best] as f64;
        let eta = 1.0 / (cnt + 1.0);
        centroids[best].x = centroids[best].x + (p.x as f64 - centroids[best].x) * eta;
        centroids[best].y = centroids[best].y + (p.y as f64 - centroids[best].y) * eta;
        centroids[best].z = centroids[best].z + (p.z as f64 - centroids[best].z) * eta;
        counts[best] += 1;
    }

    fix_empty_clusters(points, &mut labels, &mut centroids);
    labels
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();
    if input.len() < 8 {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([input[4], input[5], input[6], input[7]]) as usize;
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut offset = 8usize;
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if offset + 12 > input.len() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset], input[offset + 1], input[offset + 2], input[offset + 3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 4], input[offset + 5], input[offset + 6], input[offset + 7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([input[offset + 8], input[offset + 9], input[offset + 10], input[offset + 11]]);
        points.push(Point { x, y, z });
        offset += 12;
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let k = num_clusters.max(1).min(n.max(1));

    let labels = if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&points, k, 15)
    } else if n <= 100_000_000 {
        let mut lab = streaming_assign(&points, k);
        let mut centroids = choose_initial_centroids(&points, k);
        if centroids.len() < k && !centroids.is_empty() {
            centroids.resize(k, centroids[0]);
        }
        for _ in 0..5 {
            recompute_centroids(&points, k, &lab, &mut centroids);
            let changed = assign_points(&points, &centroids, &mut lab);
            fix_empty_clusters(&points, &mut lab, &mut centroids);
            if !changed {
                break;
            }
        }
        lab
    } else {
        streaming_assign(&points, k)
    };

    let mut out = BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for label in labels {
        out.write_all(&label.to_le_bytes()).unwrap();
    }
    out.flush().unwrap();
}

Test 27: Graph Coloring (C++) Score: 1.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the Graph Coloring problem.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ graph colorer will be tested with graphs ranging from simple 50 nodes
to massive million-node graphs requiring 60 colours.

**Problem:**
Color a graph with exactly k colours such that no two adjacent vertices share the same colour.
This is NP-Complete and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges num_colors
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
color_0 color_1 ... color_(n-1) (color assignment for each vertex)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices assigned valid colors (0 to k-1)
- No adjacent vertices share the same color
- All vertices are colored (complete coloring)

**Failure Criteria:**
- Cannot find valid coloring within time limit
- Invalid color assignment (adjacent vertices same color)
- Incomplete coloring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with g++ or MSVC (C++17)
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and color counts
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on graph complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Colour the nodes of a graph using as few colours as possible, with the rule that no two connected nodes may share the same colour. This is the same idea as colouring a map so that no neighbouring countries are the same colour.

Finding the minimum number of colours is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the graph size and connectivity. The AI’s colouring must be valid (no clashes) and use as few colours as possible.

 1.0000
[20 vertices, 4 colors] Valid coloring in 0.03s

Graph Coloring - 20 vertices, 4 colors

View C++ Code (5417 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <algorithm>
#include <numeric>
#include <limits>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace std;

struct Graph {
    int n = 0, m = 0, k = 0;
    vector<vector<int>> adj;
    vector<int> deg;
};

static bool validate_coloring(const Graph& g, const vector<int>& color) {
    if ((int)color.size() != g.n) return false;
    for (int u = 0; u < g.n; ++u) {
        if (color[u] < 0 || color[u] >= g.k) return false;
        for (int v : g.adj[u]) {
            if (u < v && color[u] == color[v]) return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}

static vector<int> greedy_order_coloring(const Graph& g) {
    vector<int> order(g.n);
    iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
    sort(order.begin(), order.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
        if (g.deg[a] != g.deg[b]) return g.deg[a] > g.deg[b];
        return a < b;
    });

    vector<int> color(g.n, -1);
    vector<int> used(g.k, -1);
    int stamp = 0;
    for (int u : order) {
        ++stamp;
        for (int v : g.adj[u]) {
            int c = color[v];
            if (c >= 0) used[c] = stamp;
        }
        int chosen = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < g.k; ++c) {
            if (used[c] != stamp) { chosen = c; break; }
        }
        if (chosen == -1) {
            // Allow temporary conflict-free fallback only if k is zero impossible.
            chosen = 0;
        }
        color[u] = chosen;
    }
    return color;
}

static vector<int> dsatur_coloring(const Graph& g) {
    vector<int> color(g.n, -1);
    vector<int> sat(g.n, 0);
    vector<int> neighbor_mask_count(g.n, 0);
    vector<vector<char>> seen(g.n, vector<char>(g.k, 0));
    vector<int> degree = g.deg;

    for (int iter = 0; iter < g.n; ++iter) {
        int best = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < g.n; ++i) {
            if (color[i] != -1) continue;
            if (best == -1 || sat[i] > sat[best] || (sat[i] == sat[best] && degree[i] > degree[best]) || (sat[i] == sat[best] && degree[i] == degree[best] && i < best)) {
                best = i;
            }
        }
        if (best == -1) break;

        vector<char> forbidden(g.k, 0);
        for (int v : g.adj[best]) {
            if (color[v] != -1) forbidden[color[v]] = 1;
        }
        int chosen = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < g.k; ++c) {
            if (!forbidden[c]) { chosen = c; break; }
        }
        if (chosen == -1) chosen = 0;
        color[best] = chosen;
        for (int v : g.adj[best]) {
            if (color[v] == -1 && !seen[v][chosen]) {
                seen[v][chosen] = 1;
                ++sat[v];
            }
        }
    }
    return color;
}

static bool repair_coloring(const Graph& g, vector<int>& color, int max_rounds) {
    if (g.k <= 0) return false;
    vector<int> conflict(g.n, 0);
    auto rebuild_conflicts = [&]() {
        fill(conflict.begin(), conflict.end(), 0);
        for (int u = 0; u < g.n; ++u) {
            for (int v : g.adj[u]) {
                if (u < v && color[u] == color[v]) {
                    ++conflict[u];
                    ++conflict[v];
                }
            }
        }
    };

    rebuild_conflicts();
    vector<int> count(g.k, 0);
    for (int round = 0; round < max_rounds; ++round) {
        int worst = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < g.n; ++i) {
            if (conflict[i] > 0 && (worst == -1 || conflict[i] > conflict[worst] || (conflict[i] == conflict[worst] && g.deg[i] > g.deg[worst]))) {
                worst = i;
            }
        }
        if (worst == -1) return true;

        fill(count.begin(), count.end(), 0);
        for (int v : g.adj[worst]) {
            if (color[v] >= 0) ++count[color[v]];
        }
        int bestc = -1, bestscore = numeric_limits<int>::max();
        for (int c = 0; c < g.k; ++c) {
            if (count[c] == 0) {
                bestc = c;
                break;
            }
            if (count[c] < bestscore) {
                bestscore = count[c];
                bestc = c;
            }
        }
        if (bestc < 0) bestc = 0;
        color[worst] = bestc;
        rebuild_conflicts();
    }
    return validate_coloring(g, color);
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    Graph g;
    cin >> g.n >> g.m >> g.k;
    g.adj.assign(g.n, {});
    g.deg.assign(g.n, 0);

    for (int i = 0; i < g.m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        if (u < 0 || u >= g.n || v < 0 || v >= g.n || u == v) continue;
        g.adj[u].push_back(v);
        g.adj[v].push_back(u);
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < g.n; ++i) {
        auto& a = g.adj[i];
        sort(a.begin(), a.end());
        a.erase(unique(a.begin(), a.end()), a.end());
        g.deg[i] = (int)a.size();
    }

    vector<int> color;
    if (g.n <= 20000 || (long long)g.m <= 10LL * g.n) {
        color = dsatur_coloring(g);
    } else {
        color = greedy_order_coloring(g);
    }

    if (!validate_coloring(g, color)) {
        if (!repair_coloring(g, color, max(1000, g.n * 2))) {
            color = dsatur_coloring(g);
            repair_coloring(g, color, max(1000, g.n * 3));
        }
    }

    if ((int)color.size() != g.n) color.assign(g.n, 0);
    for (int i = 0; i < g.n; ++i) {
        if (color[i] < 0 || color[i] >= g.k) color[i] = 0;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < g.n; ++i) {
        if (i) cout << ' ';
        cout << color[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 28: Minimum Vertex Cover (Rust) Score: 8.48 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Vertex Cover problem.

Your code may see graphs with up to 2M vertices and 15M edges.

**Problem:**
Find the minimum set of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set.
This is NP-Hard and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
vertex_count
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the cover, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All edges are covered by selected vertices
- Vertex set size is minimized (or near-minimized for large graphs)
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Not all edges are covered
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the smallest set of nodes in a graph such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set. Imagine placing guards at intersections so that every road is watched by at least one guard — use as few guards as possible.

This is a classic NP-hard problem. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal answer, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 0.8750
[10 vertices, 30 edges] Cover size 8 (optimal: 7), 0.03s

Minimum Vertex Cover - 10 vertices, 30 edges

View Rust Code (3515 chars)
use std::cmp::Reverse;
use std::collections::BinaryHeap;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let n: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(x) => x.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    let mut edges: Vec<(usize, usize)> = Vec::with_capacity(m);

    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u == v {
            adj[u].push(v);
        } else {
            adj[u].push(v);
            adj[v].push(u);
        }
        edges.push((u, v));
    }

    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        deg[i] = adj[i].len();
    }

    let mut heap: BinaryHeap<(Reverse<usize>, usize)> = BinaryHeap::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        heap.push((Reverse(deg[v]), v));
    }

    let mut removed = vec![false; n];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];

    while let Some((Reverse(d), v)) = heap.pop() {
        if removed[v] || !active[v] || deg[v] != d {
            continue;
        }
        if deg[v] == 0 {
            removed[v] = true;
            active[v] = false;
            continue;
        }

        let mut best_neighbor = None;
        let mut best_score = 0usize;
        for &to in &adj[v] {
            if active[to] {
                let score = deg[to];
                if score > best_score {
                    best_score = score;
                    best_neighbor = Some(to);
                }
            }
        }

        let take_v = best_neighbor.map_or(true, |u| deg[v] >= deg[u]);
        let chosen = if take_v {
            v
        } else {
            best_neighbor.unwrap()
        };

        if cover[chosen] || removed[chosen] {
            continue;
        }

        cover[chosen] = true;
        active[chosen] = false;
        removed[chosen] = true;

        for &to in &adj[chosen] {
            if active[to] {
                active[to] = false;
                removed[to] = true;
                for &w in &adj[to] {
                    if active[w] && deg[w] > 0 {
                        deg[w] -= 1;
                        heap.push((Reverse(deg[w]), w));
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let mut uncovered = Vec::new();
    for &(u, v) in &edges {
        if !(cover[u] || cover[v]) {
            uncovered.push((u, v));
        }
    }

    if !uncovered.is_empty() {
        let mut chosen = vec![false; n];
        for &(u, v) in &uncovered {
            if !chosen[u] && !chosen[v] {
                let pick = if deg[u] >= deg[v] { u } else { v };
                chosen[pick] = true;
            }
        }
        for i in 0..n {
            if chosen[i] {
                cover[i] = true;
            }
        }
    }

    let mut ans = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if cover[i] {
            ans.push(i);
        }
    }

    let mut out = String::new();
    out.push_str(&format!("{}\n", ans.len()));
    for (i, v) in ans.iter().enumerate() {
        if i > 0 {
            out.push(' ');
        }
        out.push_str(&v.to_string());
    }
    out.push('\n');

    let mut stdout = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout());
    stdout.write_all(out.as_bytes()).unwrap();
}

Test 29: Maximum Clique (C#) Score: 18.00 / 18
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Maximum Clique problem, which is NP Hard.

Your C# maximum clique finder will be tested with graphs ranging from sparse to very dense instances.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL graph complexities, from 20 vertices to 1 million.

**Problem:**
Find the largest complete subgraph (clique) in an undirected graph.
A clique is a set of vertices where every pair of vertices is connected by an edge.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
clique_size
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the maximum clique, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices in output form a complete subgraph
- No larger clique exists in the graph
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Output vertices don't form a clique
- Larger clique exists
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and densities
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Hints:
- You may wish to drop to a hueristic if time is running out for partial score.
- Use threads to process in parallel.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the largest group of nodes in a graph where every pair is directly connected. Think of finding the biggest circle of friends where everyone knows everyone else — no strangers allowed.

This is one of the hardest problems in computer science (NP-hard). The test uses planted instances with a known answer for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size and density.

 1.0000
[20 vertices, dense] Answered Clique size 18 (true: 18), 0.07s

Maximum Clique - 20 vertices, dense

View C# Code (8088 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;

public static class Program
{
    private static ulong[][] Adj;
    private static int[] Best;
    private static int BestSize;
    private static long DeadlineTicks;
    private static readonly object BestLock = new object();

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int m = fs.NextInt();
        var adjLists = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) adjLists[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            if ((uint)u >= (uint)n || (uint)v >= (uint)n || u == v) continue;
            adjLists[u].Add(v);
            adjLists[v].Add(u);
        }

        Adj = BuildBitsets(adjLists, n);
        Best = new int[0];
        BestSize = 0;

        // Keep a hard wall-clock bound so we always emit a valid clique.
        DeadlineTicks = DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks + TimeSpan.FromMinutes(4.75).Ticks;

        // Cheap lower bound from greedy expansion.
        var greedy = GreedyClique(adjLists, n);
        UpdateBest(greedy);

        // Order vertices by descending degree to improve branching.
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();

        // Parallelize the root branching when there is enough work.
        if (n >= 40)
        {
            int rootCount = Math.Min(n, 8);
            var threads = new Thread[rootCount];
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++)
            {
                int idx = i;
                threads[i] = new Thread(() =>
                {
                    if (TimeExpired()) return;
                    int v = order[idx];
                    int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, idx + 1, n, v);
                    var current = new int[1] { v };
                    Search(current, cand, cand.Length);
                });
                threads[i].IsBackground = true;
                threads[i].Start();
            }
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++) threads[i].Join();
        }
        else
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                if (TimeExpired()) break;
                int v = order[i];
                int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, i + 1, n, v);
                Search(new int[1] { v }, cand, cand.Length);
            }
        }

        // Fallback in case search was cut short.
        if (BestSize == 0 && n > 0) Best = new[] { 0 }; // singleton is always a clique if graph non-empty
        BestSize = Best.Length;

        var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = false };
        sw.WriteLine(BestSize);
        if (BestSize > 0)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < BestSize; i++)
            {
                if (i > 0) sw.Write(' ');
                sw.Write(Best[i]);
            }
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        else
        {
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        sw.Flush();
    }

    private static ulong[][] BuildBitsets(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int words = (n + 63) >> 6;
        var bits = new ulong[n][];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) bits[i] = new ulong[words];
        for (int u = 0; u < n; u++)
        {
            foreach (int v in adjLists[u])
            {
                bits[u][v >> 6] |= 1UL << (v & 63);
            }
        }
        return bits;
    }

    private static int[] GreedyClique(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();
        var clique = new List<int>();
        foreach (int v in order)
        {
            bool ok = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < clique.Count; i++)
            {
                int u = clique[i];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) == 0)
                {
                    ok = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (ok) clique.Add(v);
        }
        return clique.ToArray();
    }

    private static void UpdateBest(int[] clique)
    {
        lock (BestLock)
        {
            if (clique.Length > BestSize)
            {
                BestSize = clique.Length;
                Best = (int[])clique.Clone();
            }
        }
    }

    private static bool TimeExpired() => DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks >= DeadlineTicks;

    private static int[] IntersectWithNeighbors(int[] order, int start, int n, int v)
    {
        var tmp = new int[n - start];
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = start; i < n; i++)
        {
            int u = order[i];
            if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0) tmp[count++] = u;
        }
        Array.Resize(ref tmp, count);
        return tmp;
    }

    private static void Search(int[] clique, int[] candidates, int candCount)
    {
        if (TimeExpired()) return;
        if (clique.Length + candCount <= BestSize) return;

        if (candCount == 0)
        {
            UpdateBest(clique);
            return;
        }

        // Color bound: greedy coloring over the candidate set.
        int[] colors = new int[candCount];
        int maxColor = ColorBound(candidates, candCount, colors);
        if (clique.Length + maxColor <= BestSize) return;

        for (int i = candCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            if (TimeExpired()) return;
            if (clique.Length + i + 1 <= BestSize) return;

            int v = candidates[i];
            int[] nextClique = new int[clique.Length + 1];
            Array.Copy(clique, nextClique, clique.Length);
            nextClique[clique.Length] = v;

            int[] nextCand = new int[i];
            int nextCount = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                int u = candidates[j];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    nextCand[nextCount++] = u;
            }
            Array.Resize(ref nextCand, nextCount);
            Search(nextClique, nextCand, nextCount);
        }
    }

    private static int ColorBound(int[] candidates, int count, int[] colors)
    {
        int maxColor = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
        {
            int v = candidates[i];
            int c = 1;
            while (c <= maxColor)
            {
                bool conflict = false;
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (colors[j] != c) continue;
                    int u = candidates[j];
                    if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    {
                        conflict = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!conflict) break;
                c++;
            }
            if (c > maxColor) maxColor = c;
            colors[i] = c;
        }
        return maxColor;
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len, _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream) => _stream = stream;

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = Read();
            } while (c <= 32 && c >= 0);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }

            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return val * sign;
        }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }
    }
}

Maximum Clique - 40 vertices, medium density

View C# Code (8088 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;

public static class Program
{
    private static ulong[][] Adj;
    private static int[] Best;
    private static int BestSize;
    private static long DeadlineTicks;
    private static readonly object BestLock = new object();

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int m = fs.NextInt();
        var adjLists = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) adjLists[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            if ((uint)u >= (uint)n || (uint)v >= (uint)n || u == v) continue;
            adjLists[u].Add(v);
            adjLists[v].Add(u);
        }

        Adj = BuildBitsets(adjLists, n);
        Best = new int[0];
        BestSize = 0;

        // Keep a hard wall-clock bound so we always emit a valid clique.
        DeadlineTicks = DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks + TimeSpan.FromMinutes(4.75).Ticks;

        // Cheap lower bound from greedy expansion.
        var greedy = GreedyClique(adjLists, n);
        UpdateBest(greedy);

        // Order vertices by descending degree to improve branching.
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();

        // Parallelize the root branching when there is enough work.
        if (n >= 40)
        {
            int rootCount = Math.Min(n, 8);
            var threads = new Thread[rootCount];
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++)
            {
                int idx = i;
                threads[i] = new Thread(() =>
                {
                    if (TimeExpired()) return;
                    int v = order[idx];
                    int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, idx + 1, n, v);
                    var current = new int[1] { v };
                    Search(current, cand, cand.Length);
                });
                threads[i].IsBackground = true;
                threads[i].Start();
            }
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++) threads[i].Join();
        }
        else
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                if (TimeExpired()) break;
                int v = order[i];
                int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, i + 1, n, v);
                Search(new int[1] { v }, cand, cand.Length);
            }
        }

        // Fallback in case search was cut short.
        if (BestSize == 0 && n > 0) Best = new[] { 0 }; // singleton is always a clique if graph non-empty
        BestSize = Best.Length;

        var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = false };
        sw.WriteLine(BestSize);
        if (BestSize > 0)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < BestSize; i++)
            {
                if (i > 0) sw.Write(' ');
                sw.Write(Best[i]);
            }
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        else
        {
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        sw.Flush();
    }

    private static ulong[][] BuildBitsets(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int words = (n + 63) >> 6;
        var bits = new ulong[n][];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) bits[i] = new ulong[words];
        for (int u = 0; u < n; u++)
        {
            foreach (int v in adjLists[u])
            {
                bits[u][v >> 6] |= 1UL << (v & 63);
            }
        }
        return bits;
    }

    private static int[] GreedyClique(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();
        var clique = new List<int>();
        foreach (int v in order)
        {
            bool ok = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < clique.Count; i++)
            {
                int u = clique[i];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) == 0)
                {
                    ok = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (ok) clique.Add(v);
        }
        return clique.ToArray();
    }

    private static void UpdateBest(int[] clique)
    {
        lock (BestLock)
        {
            if (clique.Length > BestSize)
            {
                BestSize = clique.Length;
                Best = (int[])clique.Clone();
            }
        }
    }

    private static bool TimeExpired() => DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks >= DeadlineTicks;

    private static int[] IntersectWithNeighbors(int[] order, int start, int n, int v)
    {
        var tmp = new int[n - start];
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = start; i < n; i++)
        {
            int u = order[i];
            if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0) tmp[count++] = u;
        }
        Array.Resize(ref tmp, count);
        return tmp;
    }

    private static void Search(int[] clique, int[] candidates, int candCount)
    {
        if (TimeExpired()) return;
        if (clique.Length + candCount <= BestSize) return;

        if (candCount == 0)
        {
            UpdateBest(clique);
            return;
        }

        // Color bound: greedy coloring over the candidate set.
        int[] colors = new int[candCount];
        int maxColor = ColorBound(candidates, candCount, colors);
        if (clique.Length + maxColor <= BestSize) return;

        for (int i = candCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            if (TimeExpired()) return;
            if (clique.Length + i + 1 <= BestSize) return;

            int v = candidates[i];
            int[] nextClique = new int[clique.Length + 1];
            Array.Copy(clique, nextClique, clique.Length);
            nextClique[clique.Length] = v;

            int[] nextCand = new int[i];
            int nextCount = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                int u = candidates[j];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    nextCand[nextCount++] = u;
            }
            Array.Resize(ref nextCand, nextCount);
            Search(nextClique, nextCand, nextCount);
        }
    }

    private static int ColorBound(int[] candidates, int count, int[] colors)
    {
        int maxColor = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
        {
            int v = candidates[i];
            int c = 1;
            while (c <= maxColor)
            {
                bool conflict = false;
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (colors[j] != c) continue;
                    int u = candidates[j];
                    if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    {
                        conflict = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!conflict) break;
                c++;
            }
            if (c > maxColor) maxColor = c;
            colors[i] = c;
        }
        return maxColor;
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len, _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream) => _stream = stream;

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = Read();
            } while (c <= 32 && c >= 0);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }

            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return val * sign;
        }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }
    }
}

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;

public static class Program
{
    private static ulong[][] Adj;
    private static int[] Best;
    private static int BestSize;
    private static long DeadlineTicks;
    private static readonly object BestLock = new object();

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int m = fs.NextInt();
        var adjLists = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) adjLists[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            if ((uint)u >= (uint)n || (uint)v >= (uint)n || u == v) continue;
            adjLists[u].Add(v);
            adjLists[v].Add(u);
        }

        Adj = BuildBitsets(adjLists, n);
        Best = new int[0];
        BestSize = 0;

        // Keep a hard wall-clock bound so we always emit a valid clique.
        DeadlineTicks = DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks + TimeSpan.FromMinutes(4.75).Ticks;

        // Cheap lower bound from greedy expansion.
        var greedy = GreedyClique(adjLists, n);
        UpdateBest(greedy);

        // Order vertices by descending degree to improve branching.
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();

        // Parallelize the root branching when there is enough work.
        if (n >= 40)
        {
            int rootCount = Math.Min(n, 8);
            var threads = new Thread[rootCount];
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++)
            {
                int idx = i;
                threads[i] = new Thread(() =>
                {
                    if (TimeExpired()) return;
                    int v = order[idx];
                    int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, idx + 1, n, v);
                    var current = new int[1] { v };
                    Search(current, cand, cand.Length);
                });
                threads[i].IsBackground = true;
                threads[i].Start();
            }
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++) threads[i].Join();
        }
        else
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                if (TimeExpired()) break;
                int v = order[i];
                int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, i + 1, n, v);
                Search(new int[1] { v }, cand, cand.Length);
            }
        }

        // Fallback in case search was cut short.
        if (BestSize == 0 && n > 0) Best = new[] { 0 }; // singleton is always a clique if graph non-empty
        BestSize = Best.Length;

        var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = false };
        sw.WriteLine(BestSize);
        if (BestSize > 0)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < BestSize; i++)
            {
                if (i > 0) sw.Write(' ');
                sw.Write(Best[i]);
            }
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        else
        {
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        sw.Flush();
    }

    private static ulong[][] BuildBitsets(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int words = (n + 63) >> 6;
        var bits = new ulong[n][];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) bits[i] = new ulong[words];
        for (int u = 0; u < n; u++)
        {
            foreach (int v in adjLists[u])
            {
                bits[u][v >> 6] |= 1UL << (v & 63);
            }
        }
        return bits;
    }

    private static int[] GreedyClique(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();
        var clique = new List<int>();
        foreach (int v in order)
        {
            bool ok = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < clique.Count; i++)
            {
                int u = clique[i];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) == 0)
                {
                    ok = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (ok) clique.Add(v);
        }
        return clique.ToArray();
    }

    private static void UpdateBest(int[] clique)
    {
        lock (BestLock)
        {
            if (clique.Length > BestSize)
            {
                BestSize = clique.Length;
                Best = (int[])clique.Clone();
            }
        }
    }

    private static bool TimeExpired() => DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks >= DeadlineTicks;

    private static int[] IntersectWithNeighbors(int[] order, int start, int n, int v)
    {
        var tmp = new int[n - start];
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = start; i < n; i++)
        {
            int u = order[i];
            if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0) tmp[count++] = u;
        }
        Array.Resize(ref tmp, count);
        return tmp;
    }

    private static void Search(int[] clique, int[] candidates, int candCount)
    {
        if (TimeExpired()) return;
        if (clique.Length + candCount <= BestSize) return;

        if (candCount == 0)
        {
            UpdateBest(clique);
            return;
        }

        // Color bound: greedy coloring over the candidate set.
        int[] colors = new int[candCount];
        int maxColor = ColorBound(candidates, candCount, colors);
        if (clique.Length + maxColor <= BestSize) return;

        for (int i = candCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            if (TimeExpired()) return;
            if (clique.Length + i + 1 <= BestSize) return;

            int v = candidates[i];
            int[] nextClique = new int[clique.Length + 1];
            Array.Copy(clique, nextClique, clique.Length);
            nextClique[clique.Length] = v;

            int[] nextCand = new int[i];
            int nextCount = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                int u = candidates[j];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    nextCand[nextCount++] = u;
            }
            Array.Resize(ref nextCand, nextCount);
            Search(nextClique, nextCand, nextCount);
        }
    }

    private static int ColorBound(int[] candidates, int count, int[] colors)
    {
        int maxColor = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
        {
            int v = candidates[i];
            int c = 1;
            while (c <= maxColor)
            {
                bool conflict = false;
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (colors[j] != c) continue;
                    int u = candidates[j];
                    if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    {
                        conflict = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!conflict) break;
                c++;
            }
            if (c > maxColor) maxColor = c;
            colors[i] = c;
        }
        return maxColor;
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len, _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream) => _stream = stream;

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = Read();
            } while (c <= 32 && c >= 0);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }

            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return val * sign;
        }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }
    }
}
