using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;

public static class Program
{
    private static ulong[][] Adj;
    private static int[] Best;
    private static int BestSize;
    private static long DeadlineTicks;
    private static readonly object BestLock = new object();

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int m = fs.NextInt();
        var adjLists = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) adjLists[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            if ((uint)u >= (uint)n || (uint)v >= (uint)n || u == v) continue;
            adjLists[u].Add(v);
            adjLists[v].Add(u);
        }

        Adj = BuildBitsets(adjLists, n);
        Best = new int[0];
        BestSize = 0;

        // Keep a hard wall-clock bound so we always emit a valid clique.
        DeadlineTicks = DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks + TimeSpan.FromMinutes(4.75).Ticks;

        // Cheap lower bound from greedy expansion.
        var greedy = GreedyClique(adjLists, n);
        UpdateBest(greedy);

        // Order vertices by descending degree to improve branching.
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();

        // Parallelize the root branching when there is enough work.
        if (n >= 40)
        {
            int rootCount = Math.Min(n, 8);
            var threads = new Thread[rootCount];
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++)
            {
                int idx = i;
                threads[i] = new Thread(() =>
                {
                    if (TimeExpired()) return;
                    int v = order[idx];
                    int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, idx + 1, n, v);
                    var current = new int[1] { v };
                    Search(current, cand, cand.Length);
                });
                threads[i].IsBackground = true;
                threads[i].Start();
            }
            for (int i = 0; i < rootCount; i++) threads[i].Join();
        }
        else
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                if (TimeExpired()) break;
                int v = order[i];
                int[] cand = IntersectWithNeighbors(order, i + 1, n, v);
                Search(new int[1] { v }, cand, cand.Length);
            }
        }

        // Fallback in case search was cut short.
        if (BestSize == 0 && n > 0) Best = new[] { 0 }; // singleton is always a clique if graph non-empty
        BestSize = Best.Length;

        var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = false };
        sw.WriteLine(BestSize);
        if (BestSize > 0)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < BestSize; i++)
            {
                if (i > 0) sw.Write(' ');
                sw.Write(Best[i]);
            }
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        else
        {
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        sw.Flush();
    }

    private static ulong[][] BuildBitsets(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int words = (n + 63) >> 6;
        var bits = new ulong[n][];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) bits[i] = new ulong[words];
        for (int u = 0; u < n; u++)
        {
            foreach (int v in adjLists[u])
            {
                bits[u][v >> 6] |= 1UL << (v & 63);
            }
        }
        return bits;
    }

    private static int[] GreedyClique(List<int>[] adjLists, int n)
    {
        int[] order = Enumerable.Range(0, n).OrderByDescending(x => adjLists[x].Count).ToArray();
        var clique = new List<int>();
        foreach (int v in order)
        {
            bool ok = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < clique.Count; i++)
            {
                int u = clique[i];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) == 0)
                {
                    ok = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (ok) clique.Add(v);
        }
        return clique.ToArray();
    }

    private static void UpdateBest(int[] clique)
    {
        lock (BestLock)
        {
            if (clique.Length > BestSize)
            {
                BestSize = clique.Length;
                Best = (int[])clique.Clone();
            }
        }
    }

    private static bool TimeExpired() => DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks >= DeadlineTicks;

    private static int[] IntersectWithNeighbors(int[] order, int start, int n, int v)
    {
        var tmp = new int[n - start];
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = start; i < n; i++)
        {
            int u = order[i];
            if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0) tmp[count++] = u;
        }
        Array.Resize(ref tmp, count);
        return tmp;
    }

    private static void Search(int[] clique, int[] candidates, int candCount)
    {
        if (TimeExpired()) return;
        if (clique.Length + candCount <= BestSize) return;

        if (candCount == 0)
        {
            UpdateBest(clique);
            return;
        }

        // Color bound: greedy coloring over the candidate set.
        int[] colors = new int[candCount];
        int maxColor = ColorBound(candidates, candCount, colors);
        if (clique.Length + maxColor <= BestSize) return;

        for (int i = candCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            if (TimeExpired()) return;
            if (clique.Length + i + 1 <= BestSize) return;

            int v = candidates[i];
            int[] nextClique = new int[clique.Length + 1];
            Array.Copy(clique, nextClique, clique.Length);
            nextClique[clique.Length] = v;

            int[] nextCand = new int[i];
            int nextCount = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                int u = candidates[j];
                if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    nextCand[nextCount++] = u;
            }
            Array.Resize(ref nextCand, nextCount);
            Search(nextClique, nextCand, nextCount);
        }
    }

    private static int ColorBound(int[] candidates, int count, int[] colors)
    {
        int maxColor = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
        {
            int v = candidates[i];
            int c = 1;
            while (c <= maxColor)
            {
                bool conflict = false;
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (colors[j] != c) continue;
                    int u = candidates[j];
                    if (((Adj[v][u >> 6] >> (u & 63)) & 1UL) != 0)
                    {
                        conflict = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!conflict) break;
                c++;
            }
            if (c > maxColor) maxColor = c;
            colors[i] = c;
        }
        return maxColor;
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len, _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream) => _stream = stream;

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = Read();
            } while (c <= 32 && c >= 0);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }

            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return val * sign;
        }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }
    }
}
Test 30: 3-SAT Solver (Python) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the 3-SAT (Boolean Satisfiability) problem, which is NP-Complete.

**What is 3-SAT?**
You have N boolean variables (x1, x2, ..., xN), each of which is either True or False.
You are given M "clauses". Each clause is a constraint containing exactly 3 "literals".
A literal is either:
- A positive integer (e.g. 5) meaning "variable 5 must be True", or
- A negative integer (e.g. -5) meaning "variable 5 must be False".

A clause is **satisfied** if ANY of its 3 literals evaluates to True.
The formula is **satisfiable** if ALL clauses can be satisfied simultaneously
by some assignment of True/False to the variables.

**Example:**
Variables: x1, x2, x3
Clause "1 -2 3" means: x1=True OR x2=False OR x3=True.
If x1=True, x2=True, x3=False → clause satisfied (x1=True is enough).

Your task: find an assignment of True/False to every variable that satisfies
every clause. All test instances are guaranteed to be satisfiable.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_variables num_clauses
lit1 lit2 lit3
lit1 lit2 lit3
...
```
Variables are 1-indexed. Negative literals mean negation.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
SAT
v1 v2 v3 ... vN
```
Where each vi is 1 (True) or 0 (False) for variable i, space-separated.

**Grading:**
- Output "SAT" on the first line, then the assignment on the second line.
- Every clause must be satisfied by your assignment.
- Timeouts, crashes, wrong format, or unsatisfied clauses all score 0.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Handle up to 20M variables and 85M clauses.
3. Complete within 5 minutes.

Write complete, runnable Python code.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a logic puzzle made of true/false variables — where each rule says “at least one of these three things must be true” and all rules must hold simultaneously — find an assignment that satisfies every rule.

This is the classic 3-SAT problem, one of the first problems proven to be NP-complete. Every test instance has a hidden satisfying assignment the AI must discover. Subpasses increase the number of variables and clauses.

 0.0000
[20 vars, 85 clauses] 7/85 clauses unsatisfied, 0.38s

3-SAT Solver - 20 vars, 85 clauses

View Python Code (1122 chars)
import sys


def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer
    first = data.readline()
    if not first:
        return
    n, m = map(int, first.split())

    assign = bytearray(b"\x01") * n
    clauses = []

    for _ in range(m):
        a, b, c = map(int, data.readline().split())
        clauses.append((a, b, c))

    for _ in range(2):
        changed = False
        for a, b, c in clauses:
            va = assign[abs(a) - 1] if a > 0 else 1 - assign[abs(a) - 1]
            vb = assign[abs(b) - 1] if b > 0 else 1 - assign[abs(b) - 1]
            vc = assign[abs(c) - 1] if c > 0 else 1 - assign[abs(c) - 1]
            if va or vb or vc:
                continue
            for lit in (a, b, c):
                idx = abs(lit) - 1
                desired = 1 if lit > 0 else 0
                if assign[idx] != desired:
                    assign[idx] = desired
                    changed = True
                    break
        if not changed:
            break

    out = sys.stdout.write
    out("SAT\n")
    out(" ".join("1" if x else "0" for x in assign))
    out("\n")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Test 31: Subset Sum (Rust) Score: 6.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Subset Sum problem.

Your Rust subset sum solver will be tested with instances ranging from simple to very
complex sets. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL problem complexities.

Given a set of integers and a target value, determine if there exists a subset
that sums to exactly the target value. Simple sets (<100 numbers) you can probably
brute force, however this code will also need to run on sets with millions of
numbers. This is NP-Complete and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances:
additionally; threading, memory optimization, vectorization and parallelization are all
strongly suggested.

**Input format (stdin, space seperated decimal integers):**
```
num_numbers target
num1 num2 ... numN
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
YES or NO
[index1 index2 ... indexK] (only if YES, 0-indexed indices of subset elements)
```

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect set complexity and choose appropriate algorithm
- Use efficient data structures: bitsets, hash sets for DP
- Implement adaptive quality vs speed tradeoffs
- For very large sets, focus on approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty set, zero target, all numbers add to target.
- Use fast I/O as the input size can approach 1gb.

**Success Criteria:**
- Correctly determine if subset sum exists
- If YES, provide valid subset indices
- Complete within time limit

**Failure Criteria:**
- Incorrect subset sum determination
- Invalid subset indices
- Timeout without conclusion

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable set sizes and target values
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying subset sum complexities efficiently

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of numbers and a target total, find a subset of those numbers that adds up to exactly the target. It sounds simple, but with large lists the number of possible subsets is astronomical.

This is a classic NP-hard problem. The test uses planted instances with a known solution for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the list size and target magnitude.

 1.0000
[20 numbers, small values] Valid subset of 5 elements, 0.03s

Subset Sum - 20 numbers, small values

View Rust Code (6393 chars)
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::io::{self, Read};

fn parse_input() -> Option<(usize, i64, Vec<i64>)> {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).ok()?;
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let n: usize = it.next()?.parse().ok()?;
    let target: i64 = it.next()?.parse().ok()?;

    let mut nums = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for _ in 0..n {
        if let Some(tok) = it.next() {
            if let Ok(v) = tok.parse::<i64>() {
                nums.push(v);
            } else {
                return None;
            }
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }

    Some((n, target, nums))
}

fn reconstruct(parent: &[Option<(u64, usize)>], end_mask: u64) -> Vec<usize> {
    let mut indices = Vec::new();
    let mut mask = end_mask;
    while mask != 0 {
        let bit = mask.trailing_zeros() as usize;
        indices.push(bit);
        if let Some((prev, _idx)) = parent[bit] {
            mask = prev;
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }
    indices.reverse();
    indices
}

fn subset_sum_small(nums: &[i64], target: i64) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = nums.len();
    if n == 0 {
        return if target == 0 { Some(Vec::new()) } else { None };
    }

    let total_masks = if n >= 60 { None } else { Some(1u64 << n) };
    if let Some(limit) = total_masks {
        if limit <= 1_000_000 {
            let mut s = 0u64;
            while s < limit {
                let mut sum = 0i64;
                let mut subset = Vec::new();
                let mut m = s;
                let mut i = 0usize;
                while m != 0 {
                    if (m & 1) != 0 {
                        sum += nums[i];
                        subset.push(i);
                    }
                    i += 1;
                    m >>= 1;
                }
                if sum == target {
                    return Some(subset);
                }
                s += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    None
}

fn subset_sum_dp_nonnegative(nums: &[i64], target: i64) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    if target < 0 {
        return None;
    }
    let t = target as usize;
    let mut parent_sum: Vec<Option<usize>> = vec![None; t + 1];
    let mut parent_idx: Vec<Option<usize>> = vec![None; t + 1];
    parent_sum[0] = Some(0);

    for (i, &x) in nums.iter().enumerate() {
        if x < 0 {
            return None;
        }
        let v = x as usize;
        if v > t {
            continue;
        }
        for s in (v..=t).rev() {
            if parent_sum[s].is_none() && parent_sum[s - v].is_some() {
                parent_sum[s] = Some(s - v);
                parent_idx[s] = Some(i);
            }
        }
    }

    if parent_sum[t].is_none() {
        return None;
    }

    let mut result = Vec::new();
    let mut cur = t;
    while cur != 0 {
        let idx = parent_idx[cur]?;
        result.push(idx);
        cur = parent_sum[cur]?;
    }
    result.reverse();
    Some(result)
}

fn meet_in_middle(nums: &[i64], target: i64) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = nums.len();
    let mid = n / 2;
    let left = &nums[..mid];
    let right = &nums[mid..];

    let mut left_map: HashMap<i64, u64> = HashMap::new();
    let left_limit = 1u64.checked_shl(left.len() as u32).unwrap_or(0);
    if left_limit == 0 {
        return None;
    }

    let mut mask = 0u64;
    while mask < left_limit {
        let mut sum = 0i64;
        let mut m = mask;
        let mut i = 0usize;
        while m != 0 {
            if (m & 1) != 0 {
                sum += left[i];
            }
            i += 1;
            m >>= 1;
        }
        left_map.entry(sum).or_insert(mask);
        mask += 1;
    }

    let right_limit = 1u64.checked_shl(right.len() as u32).unwrap_or(0);
    if right_limit == 0 {
        return None;
    }

    let mut rmask = 0u64;
    while rmask < right_limit {
        let mut sum = 0i64;
        let mut m = rmask;
        let mut i = 0usize;
        while m != 0 {
            if (m & 1) != 0 {
                sum += right[i];
            }
            i += 1;
            m >>= 1;
        }

        let need = target - sum;
        if let Some(&lmask) = left_map.get(&need) {
            let mut ans = Vec::new();
            let mut lm = lmask;
            let mut i = 0usize;
            while lm != 0 {
                if (lm & 1) != 0 {
                    ans.push(i);
                }
                i += 1;
                lm >>= 1;
            }
            let mut rm = rmask;
            let mut j = 0usize;
            while rm != 0 {
                if (rm & 1) != 0 {
                    ans.push(mid + j);
                }
                j += 1;
                rm >>= 1;
            }
            ans.sort_unstable();
            return Some(ans);
        }

        rmask += 1;
    }

    None
}

fn subset_sum_signed(nums: &[i64], target: i64) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    if nums.is_empty() {
        return if target == 0 { Some(Vec::new()) } else { None };
    }

    let all_nonnegative = nums.iter().all(|&x| x >= 0);
    if all_nonnegative && target >= 0 {
        let sum: i128 = nums.iter().map(|&x| x as i128).sum();
        if sum <= 2_000_000_i128 && target <= 2_000_000_i64 {
            if let Some(ans) = subset_sum_dp_nonnegative(nums, target) {
                return Some(ans);
            }
        }
    }

    if nums.len() <= 44 {
        if let Some(ans) = meet_in_middle(nums, target) {
            return Some(ans);
        }
    }

    if nums.len() <= 24 {
        if let Some(ans) = subset_sum_small(nums, target) {
            return Some(ans);
        }
    }

    None
}

fn main() {
    let (n, target, mut nums) = match parse_input() {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => return,
    };

    if nums.len() > n {
        nums.truncate(n);
    }

    if target == 0 {
        println!("YES");
        println!();
        return;
    }

    if nums.is_empty() {
        println!("NO");
        return;
    }

    if let Some(ans) = subset_sum_signed(&nums, target) {
        println!("YES");
        if ans.is_empty() {
            println!();
        } else {
            let out = ans
                .iter()
                .map(|v| v.to_string())
                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
                .join(" ");
            println!("{out}");
        }
    } else {
        println!("NO");
    }
}

Test 32: Steiner Tree (C++) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the Steiner Tree problem (NP-Hard).

**Problem:**
Given a weighted undirected graph and a set of terminal vertices, find a minimum-weight
tree that connects all terminals. The tree may include non-terminal (Steiner) vertices.

**Input (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges num_terminals
u v weight (one edge per line, 0-indexed)
terminal_1 terminal_2 ... terminal_K
```

**Output (stdout):**
```
total_weight edge_count
u1 v1
u2 v2
...
```
Edges listed must form a connected, acyclic subgraph that includes all terminals.


**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

**Hints (not required):**
- Exact DP (Dreyfus-Wagner) for small terminal sets.
- MST / shortest-path heuristics for large graphs.
- Use adjacency lists and fast I/O.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Connect a set of important nodes in a weighted graph using the cheapest possible tree of edges. Unlike a minimum spanning tree, the solution may pass through intermediate nodes that aren’t in the required set if doing so saves cost — like building a road network that connects key cities via junctions.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal tree for strict grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 0.0000
Compilation error: Compilation failed:
tmpuk3jdz5r.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpuk3jdz5r.cpp(123): error C3861: '__builtin_ctz': identifier not found

Steiner Tree - 20V, 5 terminals

View C++ Code (7526 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <cassert>
#include <cstdint>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <queue>
#include <numeric>
#include <tuple>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Edge {
    int to;
    long long w;
    int id;
};

struct FullEdge {
    int u, v;
    long long w;
};

static constexpr long long INF = (1LL << 60);

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m, k;
    if (!(cin >> n >> m >> k)) return 0;

    vector<FullEdge> edges;
    edges.reserve(m);
    vector<vector<Edge>> g(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        long long w;
        cin >> u >> v >> w;
        edges.push_back({u, v, w});
        g[u].push_back({v, w, i});
        g[v].push_back({u, w, i});
    }

    vector<int> terminals(k);
    for (int i = 0; i < k; ++i) cin >> terminals[i];

    if (k == 0) {
        cout << 0 << ' ' << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }
    if (k == 1) {
        cout << 0 << ' ' << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    if (k <= 10 && n <= 80) {
        int S = 1 << k;
        vector<vector<long long>> dp(S, vector<long long>(n, INF));
        vector<vector<int>> parent_mask(S, vector<int>(n, -1));
        vector<vector<int>> parent_node(S, vector<int>(n, -1));

        for (int i = 0; i < k; ++i) dp[1 << i][terminals[i]] = 0;

        for (int mask = 1; mask < S; ++mask) {
            if ((mask & (mask - 1)) != 0) {
                for (int sub = (mask - 1) & mask; sub; sub = (sub - 1) & mask) {
                    int other = mask ^ sub;
                    if (other == 0) continue;
                    for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                        long long cand = dp[sub][v] + dp[other][v];
                        if (cand < dp[mask][v]) {
                            dp[mask][v] = cand;
                            parent_mask[mask][v] = sub;
                            parent_node[mask][v] = v;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            priority_queue<pair<long long, int>, vector<pair<long long, int>>, greater<pair<long long, int>>> pq;
            vector<char> vis(n, 0);
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                if (dp[mask][v] < INF) pq.push({dp[mask][v], v});
            }
            while (!pq.empty()) {
                auto [d, v] = pq.top();
                pq.pop();
                if (vis[v]) continue;
                vis[v] = 1;
                if (d != dp[mask][v]) continue;
                for (const auto &e : g[v]) {
                    long long nd = d + e.w;
                    if (nd < dp[mask][e.to]) {
                        dp[mask][e.to] = nd;
                        parent_mask[mask][e.to] = mask;
                        parent_node[mask][e.to] = v;
                        pq.push({nd, e.to});
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        int full = S - 1;
        long long best = INF;
        int root = -1;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (dp[full][v] < best) {
                best = dp[full][v];
                root = v;
            }
        }

        vector<int> used_edge(m, 0);
        vector<char> seen_state(S * n, 0);
        function<void(int,int)> reconstruct = [&](int mask, int v) {
            int idx = mask * n + v;
            if (seen_state[idx]) return;
            seen_state[idx] = 1;
            if ((mask & (mask - 1)) == 0 && v == terminals[__builtin_ctz(mask)]) return;
            int pm = parent_mask[mask][v];
            int pn = parent_node[mask][v];
            if (pm == -1) return;
            if (pm == mask) {
                if (pn != -1 && pn != v) {
                    for (const auto &e : g[pn]) {
                        if (e.to == v && dp[mask][v] == dp[mask][pn] + e.w) {
                            used_edge[e.id] = 1;
                            reconstruct(mask, pn);
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                }
            } else {
                int sub = pm;
                int other = mask ^ sub;
                reconstruct(sub, v);
                reconstruct(other, v);
            }
        };

        reconstruct(full, root);

        vector<int> ids;
        long long total = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
            if (used_edge[i]) {
                ids.push_back(i);
                total += edges[i].w;
            }
        }

        cout << total << ' ' << ids.size() << '\n';
        for (int id : ids) {
            cout << edges[id].u << ' ' << edges[id].v << '\n';
        }
        return 0;
    }

    vector<vector<long long>> dist(k, vector<long long>(n, INF));
    vector<vector<int>> prev_node(k, vector<int>(n, -1));
    vector<vector<int>> prev_edge(k, vector<int>(n, -1));

    for (int s = 0; s < k; ++s) {
        priority_queue<pair<long long, int>, vector<pair<long long, int>>, greater<pair<long long, int>>> pq;
        dist[s][terminals[s]] = 0;
        pq.push({0, terminals[s]});
        while (!pq.empty()) {
            auto [d, v] = pq.top();
            pq.pop();
            if (d != dist[s][v]) continue;
            for (const auto &e : g[v]) {
                long long nd = d + e.w;
                if (nd < dist[s][e.to]) {
                    dist[s][e.to] = nd;
                    prev_node[s][e.to] = v;
                    prev_edge[s][e.to] = e.id;
                    pq.push({nd, e.to});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    struct Candidate {
        long long w;
        int a, b;
    };
    vector<Candidate> cand;
    for (int i = 0; i < k; ++i) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < k; ++j) {
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                if (dist[i][v] < INF && dist[j][v] < INF) {
                    cand.push_back({dist[i][v] + dist[j][v], i, j});
                }
            }
        }
    }
    sort(cand.begin(), cand.end(), [](const Candidate &x, const Candidate &y) {
        return x.w < y.w;
    });

    vector<int> parent(k);
    iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);
    function<int(int)> findp = [&](int x) {
        return parent[x] == x ? x : parent[x] = findp(parent[x]);
    };

    vector<int> used_edge(m, 0);
    auto mark_path = [&](int s, int v) {
        while (v != terminals[s]) {
            int e = prev_edge[s][v];
            if (e < 0) break;
            used_edge[e] = 1;
            v = prev_node[s][v];
        }
    };

    int comps = k;
    for (const auto &c : cand) {
        int pa = findp(c.a), pb = findp(c.b);
        if (pa == pb) continue;
        int best_v = -1;
        long long best_sum = INF;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (dist[c.a][v] < INF && dist[c.b][v] < INF) {
                long long s = dist[c.a][v] + dist[c.b][v];
                if (s < best_sum) {
                    best_sum = s;
                    best_v = v;
                }
            }
        }
        if (best_v == -1) continue;
        mark_path(c.a, best_v);
        mark_path(c.b, best_v);
        parent[pa] = pb;
        --comps;
        if (comps == 1) break;
    }

    vector<int> ids;
    long long total = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        if (used_edge[i]) {
            ids.push_back(i);
            total += edges[i].w;
        }
    }

    cout << total << ' ' << ids.size() << '\n';
    for (int id : ids) {
        cout << edges[id].u << ' ' << edges[id].v << '\n';
    }
    return 0;
}

#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <cassert>
#include <cstdint>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <queue>
#include <numeric>
#include <tuple>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Edge {
    int to;
    long long w;
    int id;
};

struct FullEdge {
    int u, v;
    long long w;
};

static constexpr long long INF = (1LL << 60);

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m, k;
    if (!(cin >> n >> m >> k)) return 0;

    vector<FullEdge> edges;
    edges.reserve(m);
    vector<vector<Edge>> g(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        long long w;
        cin >> u >> v >> w;
        edges.push_back({u, v, w});
        g[u].push_back({v, w, i});
        g[v].push_back({u, w, i});
    }

    vector<int> terminals(k);
    for (int i = 0; i < k; ++i) cin >> terminals[i];

    if (k == 0) {
        cout << 0 << ' ' << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }
    if (k == 1) {
        cout << 0 << ' ' << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    if (k <= 10 && n <= 80) {
        int S = 1 << k;
        vector<vector<long long>> dp(S, vector<long long>(n, INF));
        vector<vector<int>> parent_mask(S, vector<int>(n, -1));
        vector<vector<int>> parent_node(S, vector<int>(n, -1));

        for (int i = 0; i < k; ++i) dp[1 << i][terminals[i]] = 0;

        for (int mask = 1; mask < S; ++mask) {
            if ((mask & (mask - 1)) != 0) {
                for (int sub = (mask - 1) & mask; sub; sub = (sub - 1) & mask) {
                    int other = mask ^ sub;
                    if (other == 0) continue;
                    for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                        long long cand = dp[sub][v] + dp[other][v];
                        if (cand < dp[mask][v]) {
                            dp[mask][v] = cand;
                            parent_mask[mask][v] = sub;
                            parent_node[mask][v] = v;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            priority_queue<pair<long long, int>, vector<pair<long long, int>>, greater<pair<long long, int>>> pq;
            vector<char> vis(n, 0);
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                if (dp[mask][v] < INF) pq.push({dp[mask][v], v});
            }
            while (!pq.empty()) {
                auto [d, v] = pq.top();
                pq.pop();
                if (vis[v]) continue;
                vis[v] = 1;
                if (d != dp[mask][v]) continue;
                for (const auto &e : g[v]) {
                    long long nd = d + e.w;
                    if (nd < dp[mask][e.to]) {
                        dp[mask][e.to] = nd;
                        parent_mask[mask][e.to] = mask;
                        parent_node[mask][e.to] = v;
                        pq.push({nd, e.to});
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        int full = S - 1;
        long long best = INF;
        int root = -1;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (dp[full][v] < best) {
                best = dp[full][v];
                root = v;
            }
        }

        vector<int> used_edge(m, 0);
        vector<char> seen_state(S * n, 0);
        function<void(int,int)> reconstruct = [&](int mask, int v) {
            int idx = mask * n + v;
            if (seen_state[idx]) return;
            seen_state[idx] = 1;
            if ((mask & (mask - 1)) == 0 && v == terminals[__builtin_ctz(mask)]) return;
            int pm = parent_mask[mask][v];
            int pn = parent_node[mask][v];
            if (pm == -1) return;
            if (pm == mask) {
                if (pn != -1 && pn != v) {
                    for (const auto &e : g[pn]) {
                        if (e.to == v && dp[mask][v] == dp[mask][pn] + e.w) {
                            used_edge[e.id] = 1;
                            reconstruct(mask, pn);
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                }
            } else {
                int sub = pm;
                int other = mask ^ sub;
                reconstruct(sub, v);
                reconstruct(other, v);
            }
        };

        reconstruct(full, root);

        vector<int> ids;
        long long total = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
            if (used_edge[i]) {
                ids.push_back(i);
                total += edges[i].w;
            }
        }

        cout << total << ' ' << ids.size() << '\n';
        for (int id : ids) {
            cout << edges[id].u << ' ' << edges[id].v << '\n';
        }
        return 0;
    }

    vector<vector<long long>> dist(k, vector<long long>(n, INF));
    vector<vector<int>> prev_node(k, vector<int>(n, -1));
    vector<vector<int>> prev_edge(k, vector<int>(n, -1));

    for (int s = 0; s < k; ++s) {
        priority_queue<pair<long long, int>, vector<pair<long long, int>>, greater<pair<long long, int>>> pq;
        dist[s][terminals[s]] = 0;
        pq.push({0, terminals[s]});
        while (!pq.empty()) {
            auto [d, v] = pq.top();
            pq.pop();
            if (d != dist[s][v]) continue;
            for (const auto &e : g[v]) {
                long long nd = d + e.w;
                if (nd < dist[s][e.to]) {
                    dist[s][e.to] = nd;
                    prev_node[s][e.to] = v;
                    prev_edge[s][e.to] = e.id;
                    pq.push({nd, e.to});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    struct Candidate {
        long long w;
        int a, b;
    };
    vector<Candidate> cand;
    for (int i = 0; i < k; ++i) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < k; ++j) {
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                if (dist[i][v] < INF && dist[j][v] < INF) {
                    cand.push_back({dist[i][v] + dist[j][v], i, j});
                }
            }
        }
    }
    sort(cand.begin(), cand.end(), [](const Candidate &x, const Candidate &y) {
        return x.w < y.w;
    });

    vector<int> parent(k);
    iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);
    function<int(int)> findp = [&](int x) {
        return parent[x] == x ? x : parent[x] = findp(parent[x]);
    };

    vector<int> used_edge(m, 0);
    auto mark_path = [&](int s, int v) {
        while (v != terminals[s]) {
            int e = prev_edge[s][v];
            if (e < 0) break;
            used_edge[e] = 1;
            v = prev_node[s][v];
        }
    };

    int comps = k;
    for (const auto &c : cand) {
        int pa = findp(c.a), pb = findp(c.b);
        if (pa == pb) continue;
        int best_v = -1;
        long long best_sum = INF;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (dist[c.a][v] < INF && dist[c.b][v] < INF) {
                long long s = dist[c.a][v] + dist[c.b][v];
                if (s < best_sum) {
                    best_sum = s;
                    best_v = v;
                }
            }
        }
        if (best_v == -1) continue;
        mark_path(c.a, best_v);
        mark_path(c.b, best_v);
        parent[pa] = pb;
        --comps;
        if (comps == 1) break;
    }

    vector<int> ids;
    long long total = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        if (used_edge[i]) {
            ids.push_back(i);
            total += edges[i].w;
        }
    }

    cout << total << ' ' << ids.size() << '\n';
    for (int id : ids) {
        cout << edges[id].u << ' ' << edges[id].v << '\n';
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 33: Quadratic Assignment Problem (C#) Score: 16.40 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Quadratic Assignment Problem (QAP), which is NP-hard.

**Problem:**
You have N facilities and N locations. You must assign each facility to exactly one
location (a permutation). The cost of assigning facility i to location p[i] is:

sum over all pairs (i, j): flow[i][j] * distance[p[i]][p[j]]

Your goal is to minimize this total cost.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
N
flow[0][0] flow[0][1] ... flow[0][N-1]
...
flow[N-1][0] ... flow[N-1][N-1]
dist[0][0] dist[0][1] ... dist[0][N-1]
...
dist[N-1][0] ... dist[N-1][N-1]
```
All values are integers. Matrices are N×N. Indices are 0-based in the output.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
total_cost
p0 p1 p2 ... p(N-1)
```
Where p is a permutation of 0..N-1 and p[i] is the location assigned to facility i.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output a valid permutation.
3. Handle N from very small up to ~2000 within 5 minutes.
4. Heuristics are acceptable for large N; lower cost scores higher.

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method. Assume only the standard library.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Assign N facilities to N locations so that the total cost is minimised, where cost depends on how much material flows between each pair of facilities multiplied by the distance between their assigned locations. Think of arranging departments in an office to minimise how far people walk between them.

This is the Quadratic Assignment Problem, one of the hardest combinatorial optimisation problems. The test uses planted instances with a known optimum for strict grading. Subpasses increase the problem size.

 1.0000
[8 facilities] Cost 104854 (greedy: 122211), 0.06s

Quadratic Assignment - 8 facilities

View C# Code (4873 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public class Program
{
    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len;
        private int _ptr;

        private byte ReadByte()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return 0;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            byte c;
            do c = ReadByte(); while (c <= 32 && c != 0);
            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-') { sign = -1; c = ReadByte(); }
            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = ReadByte();
            }
            return val * sign;
        }
    }

    private static long CalcCost(int[] perm, int[][] flow, int[][] dist)
    {
        int n = perm.Length;
        long cost = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int pi = perm[i];
            int[] fi = flow[i];
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) cost += (long)fi[j] * dist[pi][perm[j]];
        }
        return cost;
    }

    private static long DeltaSwap(int a, int b, int[] perm, int[][] flow, int[][] dist)
    {
        if (a == b) return 0;
        int n = perm.Length;
        int pa = perm[a];
        int pb = perm[b];
        long delta = 0;
        for (int k = 0; k < n; k++)
        {
            if (k == a || k == b) continue;
            int pk = perm[k];
            long fik = flow[a][k] + flow[k][a];
            long fjk = flow[b][k] + flow[k][b];
            delta += fik * (dist[pb][pk] - dist[pa][pk]);
            delta += fjk * (dist[pa][pk] - dist[pb][pk]);
        }
        long fab = flow[a][b] + flow[b][a];
        delta += fab * (dist[pb][pa] - dist[pa][pb]);
        delta += (long)flow[a][a] * (dist[pb][pb] - dist[pa][pa]);
        delta += (long)flow[b][b] * (dist[pa][pa] - dist[pb][pb]);
        return delta;
    }

    private static void ImproveLocal(int[] perm, int[][] flow, int[][] dist, int maxPasses)
    {
        int n = perm.Length;
        for (int pass = 0; pass < maxPasses; pass++)
        {
            bool improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++)
                {
                    long d = DeltaSwap(i, j, perm, flow, dist);
                    if (d < 0)
                    {
                        int t = perm[i]; perm[i] = perm[j]; perm[j] = t;
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
            if (!improved) break;
        }
    }

    private static int[] GreedyConstruct(int[][] flow, int[][] dist)
    {
        int n = flow.Length;
        int[] orderF = new int[n];
        int[] orderL = new int[n];
        long[] scoreF = new long[n];
        long[] scoreL = new long[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            long sf = 0, sl = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
            {
                sf += Math.Abs((long)flow[i][j]) + Math.Abs((long)flow[j][i]);
                sl += Math.Abs((long)dist[i][j]) + Math.Abs((long)dist[j][i]);
            }
            scoreF[i] = sf;
            scoreL[i] = sl;
            orderF[i] = i;
            orderL[i] = i;
        }
        Array.Sort(scoreF, orderF);
        Array.Sort(scoreL, orderL);
        Array.Reverse(orderF);
        Array.Reverse(orderL);

        int[] perm = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) perm[orderF[i]] = orderL[i];
        return perm;
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner();
        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int[][] flow = new int[n][];
        int[][] dist = new int[n][];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            flow[i] = new int[n];
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) flow[i][j] = fs.NextInt();
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            dist[i] = new int[n];
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) dist[i][j] = fs.NextInt();
        }

        int[] perm = GreedyConstruct(flow, dist);

        if (n <= 250)
        {
            ImproveLocal(perm, flow, dist, 4);
        }
        else if (n <= 800)
        {
            ImproveLocal(perm, flow, dist, 1);
        }

        long cost = CalcCost(perm, flow, dist);
        var sb = new StringBuilder();
        sb.AppendLine(cost.ToString());
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            if (i > 0) sb.Append(' ');
            sb.Append(perm[i]);
        }
        sb.AppendLine();
        Console.Write(sb.ToString());
    }
}

Test 34: Vehicle Routing Problem (Python) Score: 0.94 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem (CVRP).

**Problem:**
You have a depot and N customers with coordinates and demands. You must create up to K vehicle
routes starting and ending at the depot. Each customer must be visited exactly once and the sum
of demands on any route must not exceed the vehicle capacity. Minimize total travel distance.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
N K CAPACITY
depot_x depot_y
x1 y1 demand1
x2 y2 demand2
...
xN yN demandN
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
total_distance
num_routes
route_1
route_2
...
```
Each route line should list customer indices (0-based) in visit order.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output valid routes that cover all customers exactly once.
3. Respect vehicle capacity on every route.
4. Heuristics are acceptable for large instances; shorter distance scores higher.

Write complete, runnable Python code.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Plan delivery routes for a fleet of vehicles so that every customer is visited and the total distance travelled is minimised. Each vehicle has a capacity limit, so the AI must decide which customers go on which truck and in what order.

This is the Vehicle Routing Problem, a generalisation of the Travelling Salesman Problem. Subpasses increase the number of customers and vehicles. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic.

 0.9370
[15 customers, 3 vehicles] Distance 764.1 (NN: 678.6), 0.36s

Vehicle Routing - 15 customers, 3 vehicles

View Python Code (3451 chars)
import sys
import math


def dist(a, b):
    return math.hypot(a[0] - b[0], a[1] - b[1])


def route_length(route, pts):
    if not route:
        return 0.0
    total = dist(pts[0], pts[route[0]])
    for i in range(len(route) - 1):
        total += dist(pts[route[i]], pts[route[i + 1]])
    total += dist(pts[route[-1]], pts[0])
    return total


def insertion_cost(route, pos, customer, pts):
    prev_pt = pts[0] if pos == 0 else pts[route[pos - 1]]
    next_pt = pts[0] if pos == len(route) else pts[route[pos]]
    return dist(prev_pt, pts[customer]) + dist(pts[customer], next_pt) - dist(prev_pt, next_pt)


def best_insertion_pos(route, customer, pts):
    best_pos = 0
    best_cost = float("inf")
    for pos in range(len(route) + 1):
        c = insertion_cost(route, pos, customer, pts)
        if c < best_cost:
            best_cost = c
            best_pos = pos
    return best_pos, best_cost


def two_opt(route, pts):
    if len(route) < 4:
        return route
    improved = True
    while improved:
        improved = False
        for i in range(len(route) - 2):
            a = pts[0] if i == 0 else pts[route[i - 1]]
            b = pts[route[i]]
            for j in range(i + 2, len(route)):
                c = pts[route[j - 1]]
                d = pts[0] if j == len(route) else pts[route[j]]
                old = dist(a, b) + dist(c, d)
                new = dist(a, c) + dist(b, d)
                if new + 1e-12 < old:
                    route[i:j] = reversed(route[i:j])
                    improved = True
                    break
            if improved:
                break
    return route


def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return

    it = iter(data)
    n = int(next(it))
    k = int(next(it))
    cap = float(next(it))

    depot = (float(next(it)), float(next(it)))
    pts = [depot]
    demand = [0.0]
    for _ in range(n):
        x = float(next(it))
        y = float(next(it))
        d = float(next(it))
        pts.append((x, y))
        demand.append(d)

    customers = list(range(1, n + 1))
    customers.sort(key=lambda i: (-demand[i], dist(depot, pts[i])))

    routes = []
    loads = []

    for cust in customers:
        best_route = -1
        best_pos = 0
        best_score = float("inf")

        for r_idx, route in enumerate(routes):
            if loads[r_idx] + demand[cust] > cap + 1e-12:
                continue
            pos, cost = best_insertion_pos(route, cust, pts)
            score = cost + 0.05 * len(route)
            if score < best_score:
                best_score = score
                best_route = r_idx
                best_pos = pos

        if best_route == -1:
            routes.append([cust])
            loads.append(demand[cust])
        else:
            routes[best_route].insert(best_pos, cust)
            loads[best_route] += demand[cust]

    for idx in range(len(routes)):
        routes[idx] = two_opt(routes[idx], pts)

    route_infos = []
    for route in routes:
        route_infos.append((route_length(route, pts), route))

    route_infos.sort(key=lambda x: (len(x[1]), x[0]))
    routes = [r for _, r in route_infos]

    total = sum(route_length(route, pts) for route in routes)

    out = [f"{total:.6f}", str(len(routes))]
    for route in routes:
        out.append(" ".join(str(i - 1) for i in route))
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()

Vehicle Routing - 25 customers, 4 vehicles

View Python Code (3451 chars)
import sys
import math


def dist(a, b):
    return math.hypot(a[0] - b[0], a[1] - b[1])


def route_length(route, pts):
    if not route:
        return 0.0
    total = dist(pts[0], pts[route[0]])
    for i in range(len(route) - 1):
        total += dist(pts[route[i]], pts[route[i + 1]])
    total += dist(pts[route[-1]], pts[0])
    return total


def insertion_cost(route, pos, customer, pts):
    prev_pt = pts[0] if pos == 0 else pts[route[pos - 1]]
    next_pt = pts[0] if pos == len(route) else pts[route[pos]]
    return dist(prev_pt, pts[customer]) + dist(pts[customer], next_pt) - dist(prev_pt, next_pt)


def best_insertion_pos(route, customer, pts):
    best_pos = 0
    best_cost = float("inf")
    for pos in range(len(route) + 1):
        c = insertion_cost(route, pos, customer, pts)
        if c < best_cost:
            best_cost = c
            best_pos = pos
    return best_pos, best_cost


def two_opt(route, pts):
    if len(route) < 4:
        return route
    improved = True
    while improved:
        improved = False
        for i in range(len(route) - 2):
            a = pts[0] if i == 0 else pts[route[i - 1]]
            b = pts[route[i]]
            for j in range(i + 2, len(route)):
                c = pts[route[j - 1]]
                d = pts[0] if j == len(route) else pts[route[j]]
                old = dist(a, b) + dist(c, d)
                new = dist(a, c) + dist(b, d)
                if new + 1e-12 < old:
                    route[i:j] = reversed(route[i:j])
                    improved = True
                    break
            if improved:
                break
    return route


def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return

    it = iter(data)
    n = int(next(it))
    k = int(next(it))
    cap = float(next(it))

    depot = (float(next(it)), float(next(it)))
    pts = [depot]
    demand = [0.0]
    for _ in range(n):
        x = float(next(it))
        y = float(next(it))
        d = float(next(it))
        pts.append((x, y))
        demand.append(d)

    customers = list(range(1, n + 1))
    customers.sort(key=lambda i: (-demand[i], dist(depot, pts[i])))

    routes = []
    loads = []

    for cust in customers:
        best_route = -1
        best_pos = 0
        best_score = float("inf")

        for r_idx, route in enumerate(routes):
            if loads[r_idx] + demand[cust] > cap + 1e-12:
                continue
            pos, cost = best_insertion_pos(route, cust, pts)
            score = cost + 0.05 * len(route)
            if score < best_score:
                best_score = score
                best_route = r_idx
                best_pos = pos

        if best_route == -1:
            routes.append([cust])
            loads.append(demand[cust])
        else:
            routes[best_route].insert(best_pos, cust)
            loads[best_route] += demand[cust]

    for idx in range(len(routes)):
        routes[idx] = two_opt(routes[idx], pts)

    route_infos = []
    for route in routes:
        route_infos.append((route_length(route, pts), route))

    route_infos.sort(key=lambda x: (len(x[1]), x[0]))
    routes = [r for _, r in route_infos]

    total = sum(route_length(route, pts) for route in routes)

    out = [f"{total:.6f}", str(len(routes))]
    for route in routes:
        out.append(" ".join(str(i - 1) for i in route))
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()

Test 35: Minimum Dominating Set (Rust) Score: 4.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Dominating Set problem (NP-hard).

**Problem:**
Given an undirected graph, find a small set of vertices S such that every vertex is
either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Smaller sets score better.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u1 v1
u2 v2
...
```
Vertices are 0-indexed.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
set_size
v1 v2 v3 ...
```
Where the second line lists the vertices in the dominating set (0-indexed).

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output a valid dominating set.
3. Heuristics are acceptable for large graphs; smaller sets score higher.
4. Must run within 5 minutes.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc


Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the smallest set of nodes such that every node in the graph is either in the set or directly connected to a node in the set. Think of placing Wi-Fi routers so that every room is within range of at least one router.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal set for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 0.5000
[20 vertices] Size 16 (greedy: 3), 0.04s

Minimum Dominating Set - 20 vertices

View Rust Code (5018 chars)
use std::cmp::Reverse;
use std::collections::{BinaryHeap, HashSet};
use std::io::{self, Read};

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let n: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(x) => x.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut adj = vec![Vec::<usize>::new(); n];
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u == v {
            adj[u].push(v);
        } else {
            adj[u].push(v);
            adj[v].push(u);
        }
    }

    for neigh in &mut adj {
        neigh.sort_unstable();
        neigh.dedup();
    }

    let mut dominated = vec![false; n];
    let mut chosen = vec![false; n];
    let mut solution: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();

    let mut heap: BinaryHeap<(Reverse<usize>, usize)> = BinaryHeap::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        heap.push((Reverse(adj[v].len() + 1), v));
    }

    let mut uncovered = n;
    while uncovered > 0 {
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_gain = 0usize;

        while let Some((Reverse(_prio), v)) = heap.pop() {
            let mut gain = if !dominated[v] { 1 } else { 0 };
            for &to in &adj[v] {
                if !dominated[to] {
                    gain += 1;
                }
            }
            if gain == 0 {
                continue;
            }
            best_v = Some(v);
            best_gain = gain;
            break;
        }

        if let Some(v) = best_v {
            if chosen[v] {
                continue;
            }
            chosen[v] = true;
            solution.push(v);
            if !dominated[v] {
                dominated[v] = true;
                uncovered -= 1;
            }
            for &to in &adj[v] {
                if !dominated[to] {
                    dominated[to] = true;
                    uncovered -= 1;
                }
            }
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }

    let mut dom_count = vec![0usize; n];
    let mut witness = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    for &v in &solution {
        if witness[v] == usize::MAX {
            witness[v] = v;
            dom_count[v] += 1;
        }
        for &to in &adj[v] {
            if witness[to] == usize::MAX {
                witness[to] = v;
                dom_count[v] += 1;
            } else {
                dom_count[witness[to]] += 0;
            }
        }
    }

    let mut in_solution = HashSet::with_capacity(solution.len() * 2 + 1);
    for &v in &solution {
        in_solution.insert(v);
    }

    let mut order = solution.clone();
    order.sort_by_key(|&v| adj[v].len());
    for &v in &order {
        if !in_solution.contains(&v) {
            continue;
        }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        let mut affected: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
        if witness[v] == v {
            affected.push(v);
        }
        for &to in &adj[v] {
            if witness[to] == v {
                affected.push(to);
            }
        }
        affected.sort_unstable();
        affected.dedup();

        for &x in &affected {
            let mut covered_elsewhere = false;
            if x == v {
                for &y in &adj[x] {
                    if y != v && in_solution.contains(&y) {
                        covered_elsewhere = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if !covered_elsewhere {
                    can_remove = false;
                    break;
                }
            } else {
                let mut ok = false;
                for &y in &adj[x] {
                    if y != v && in_solution.contains(&y) {
                        ok = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if !ok {
                    can_remove = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if can_remove {
            in_solution.remove(&v);
        }
    }

    let mut sol: Vec<usize> = in_solution.into_iter().collect();
    sol.sort_unstable();

    let mut final_dom = vec![false; n];
    for &v in &sol {
        final_dom[v] = true;
        for &to in &adj[v] {
            final_dom[to] = true;
        }
    }
    if final_dom.iter().any(|&b| !b) {
        for v in 0..n {
            if !final_dom[v] {
                sol.push(v);
                final_dom[v] = true;
                for &to in &adj[v] {
                    final_dom[to] = true;
                }
            }
        }
        sol.sort_unstable();
        sol.dedup();
    }

    println!("{}", sol.len());
    if sol.is_empty() {
        println!();
    } else {
        for (i, v) in sol.iter().enumerate() {
            if i > 0 {
                print!(" ");
            }
            print!("{}", v);
        }
        println!();
    }
}

Test 36: Maximum Independent Set (C++) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the **Maximum Independent Set** problem.

Given an undirected graph, find the largest set of vertices such that no two chosen vertices share an edge.

The evaluator is strict: only an exact maximum independent set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of vertices (0-indexed: 0..n-1)
- `m` = number of undirected edges
- each edge appears as two integers `u v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k` = size of your independent set
- second line lists exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (space-separated)
- vertices may be printed in any order

**What will be checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Every printed vertex is in range.
3. No edge exists between any two printed vertices.
4. `k` is the true maximum for the input graph.

**Requirements**
1. Use standard C++ (C++17-compatible).
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with `main()`.
 Prompt Changes:

View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the largest group of nodes in a graph such that no two nodes in the group are connected by an edge. Think of seating people at a dinner party where certain pairs don’t get along — seat as many guests as possible with no feuding neighbours at the same table.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal answer for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 0.0000
Compilation error: Compilation failed:
tmpbenkfbs2.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpbenkfbs2.cpp(1): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'bits/stdc++.h': No such file or directory

Maximum Independent Set - tiny (60 vertices), planted MIS=20

Graph: 60 vertices, ~200 edges, planted optimum size = 20

View C++ Code (5251 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

struct Solver {
    int n;
    vector<vector<int>> g;
    vector<int> order;
    vector<int> best, cur;
    vector<char> alive;

    int remaining_vertices() const {
        int c = 0;
        for (char x : alive) c += x;
        return c;
    }

    int degree_alive(int v) const {
        int d = 0;
        for (int to : g[v]) if (alive[to]) ++d;
        return d;
    }

    int greedy_coloring_bound() const {
        vector<int> verts;
        verts.reserve(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (alive[i]) verts.push_back(i);
        if (verts.empty()) return 0;

        vector<int> color(verts.size(), 0);
        int colors = 0;
        vector<int> idx(n, -1);
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)verts.size(); ++i) idx[verts[i]] = i;

        vector<vector<int>> buckets;
        for (int v : verts) {
            int c = 0;
            for (;;) {
                bool ok = true;
                for (int to : g[v]) {
                    int p = idx[to];
                    if (p != -1 && color[p] == c) {
                        ok = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (ok) break;
                ++c;
            }
            color[idx[v]] = c;
            colors = max(colors, c + 1);
        }
        return colors;
    }

    int choose_vertex() const {
        int bestv = -1, bestd = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (alive[i]) {
            int d = degree_alive(i);
            if (d > bestd) bestd = d, bestv = i;
        }
        return bestv;
    }

    void include_vertex(int v, vector<int>& removed) {
        if (alive[v]) {
            alive[v] = 0;
            removed.push_back(v);
        }
        for (int to : g[v]) if (alive[to]) {
            alive[to] = 0;
            removed.push_back(to);
        }
    }

    void restore(const vector<int>& removed) {
        for (int v : removed) alive[v] = 1;
    }

    void propagate_forced(vector<int>& picked, vector<int>& removed) {
        bool changed = true;
        while (changed) {
            changed = false;
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) if (alive[v]) {
                int d = 0, last = -1;
                for (int to : g[v]) if (alive[to]) {
                    ++d;
                    last = to;
                    if (d > 1) break;
                }
                if (d == 0) {
                    alive[v] = 0;
                    removed.push_back(v);
                    picked.push_back(v);
                    changed = true;
                    break;
                }
                if (d == 1) {
                    include_vertex(last, removed);
                    picked.push_back(last);
                    changed = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    void dfs() {
        vector<int> local_picked;
        vector<int> local_removed;
        propagate_forced(local_picked, local_removed);
        for (int v : local_picked) cur.push_back(v);

        int rem = remaining_vertices();
        if ((int)cur.size() + rem <= (int)best.size()) {
            for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        if (rem == 0) {
            if (cur.size() > best.size()) best = cur;
            for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        int bound = greedy_coloring_bound();
        if ((int)cur.size() + bound <= (int)best.size()) {
            for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        int v = choose_vertex();
        if (v == -1) {
            if (cur.size() > best.size()) best = cur;
            for (int x : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        {
            vector<int> removed;
            include_vertex(v, removed);
            cur.push_back(v);
            dfs();
            cur.pop_back();
            restore(removed);
        }

        {
            vector<int> removed;
            alive[v] = 0;
            removed.push_back(v);
            dfs();
            restore(removed);
        }

        for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
        restore(local_removed);
    }

    vector<int> solve() {
        alive.assign(n, 1);
        dfs();
        sort(best.begin(), best.end());
        return best;
    }
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    if (!(cin >> n >> m)) return 0;

    Solver s;
    s.n = n;
    s.g.assign(n, {});
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        if (u < 0 || u >= n || v < 0 || v >= n || u == v) continue;
        s.g[u].push_back(v);
        s.g[v].push_back(u);
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        sort(s.g[i].begin(), s.g[i].end());
        s.g[i].erase(unique(s.g[i].begin(), s.g[i].end()), s.g[i].end());
    }

    vector<int> ans = s.solve();
    cout << ans.size() << '\n';
    for (size_t i = 0; i < ans.size(); ++i) {
        if (i) cout << ' ';
        cout << ans[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';
    return 0;
}

#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

struct Solver {
    int n;
    vector<vector<int>> g;
    vector<int> order;
    vector<int> best, cur;
    vector<char> alive;

    int remaining_vertices() const {
        int c = 0;
        for (char x : alive) c += x;
        return c;
    }

    int degree_alive(int v) const {
        int d = 0;
        for (int to : g[v]) if (alive[to]) ++d;
        return d;
    }

    int greedy_coloring_bound() const {
        vector<int> verts;
        verts.reserve(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (alive[i]) verts.push_back(i);
        if (verts.empty()) return 0;

        vector<int> color(verts.size(), 0);
        int colors = 0;
        vector<int> idx(n, -1);
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)verts.size(); ++i) idx[verts[i]] = i;

        vector<vector<int>> buckets;
        for (int v : verts) {
            int c = 0;
            for (;;) {
                bool ok = true;
                for (int to : g[v]) {
                    int p = idx[to];
                    if (p != -1 && color[p] == c) {
                        ok = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (ok) break;
                ++c;
            }
            color[idx[v]] = c;
            colors = max(colors, c + 1);
        }
        return colors;
    }

    int choose_vertex() const {
        int bestv = -1, bestd = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (alive[i]) {
            int d = degree_alive(i);
            if (d > bestd) bestd = d, bestv = i;
        }
        return bestv;
    }

    void include_vertex(int v, vector<int>& removed) {
        if (alive[v]) {
            alive[v] = 0;
            removed.push_back(v);
        }
        for (int to : g[v]) if (alive[to]) {
            alive[to] = 0;
            removed.push_back(to);
        }
    }

    void restore(const vector<int>& removed) {
        for (int v : removed) alive[v] = 1;
    }

    void propagate_forced(vector<int>& picked, vector<int>& removed) {
        bool changed = true;
        while (changed) {
            changed = false;
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) if (alive[v]) {
                int d = 0, last = -1;
                for (int to : g[v]) if (alive[to]) {
                    ++d;
                    last = to;
                    if (d > 1) break;
                }
                if (d == 0) {
                    alive[v] = 0;
                    removed.push_back(v);
                    picked.push_back(v);
                    changed = true;
                    break;
                }
                if (d == 1) {
                    include_vertex(last, removed);
                    picked.push_back(last);
                    changed = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    void dfs() {
        vector<int> local_picked;
        vector<int> local_removed;
        propagate_forced(local_picked, local_removed);
        for (int v : local_picked) cur.push_back(v);

        int rem = remaining_vertices();
        if ((int)cur.size() + rem <= (int)best.size()) {
            for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        if (rem == 0) {
            if (cur.size() > best.size()) best = cur;
            for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        int bound = greedy_coloring_bound();
        if ((int)cur.size() + bound <= (int)best.size()) {
            for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        int v = choose_vertex();
        if (v == -1) {
            if (cur.size() > best.size()) best = cur;
            for (int x : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
            restore(local_removed);
            return;
        }

        {
            vector<int> removed;
            include_vertex(v, removed);
            cur.push_back(v);
            dfs();
            cur.pop_back();
            restore(removed);
        }

        {
            vector<int> removed;
            alive[v] = 0;
            removed.push_back(v);
            dfs();
            restore(removed);
        }

        for (int v : local_picked) cur.pop_back();
        restore(local_removed);
    }

    vector<int> solve() {
        alive.assign(n, 1);
        dfs();
        sort(best.begin(), best.end());
        return best;
    }
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    if (!(cin >> n >> m)) return 0;

    Solver s;
    s.n = n;
    s.g.assign(n, {});
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        if (u < 0 || u >= n || v < 0 || v >= n || u == v) continue;
        s.g[u].push_back(v);
        s.g[v].push_back(u);
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        sort(s.g[i].begin(), s.g[i].end());
        s.g[i].erase(unique(s.g[i].begin(), s.g[i].end()), s.g[i].end());
    }

    vector<int> ans = s.solve();
    cout << ans.size() << '\n';
    for (size_t i = 0; i < ans.size(); ++i) {
        if (i) cout << ' ';
        cout << ans[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';
    return 0;
}
