Route search drones over a probability heatmap with obstacles and battery limits.		 0.0000
drone 0 makes a non-adjacent move (run 0.08s)

Drone Search Coverage Planner - 2 drones, 55 steps

View JavaScript code (5289 chars)
const fs = require('fs');
const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

const grid = data.probability || [];
const drones = data.drones || [];
const maxSteps = Math.max(0, data.maxStepsPerDrone | 0);
const h = grid.length;
const w = h ? grid[0].length : 0;

function inBounds(x, y) {
  return x >= 0 && y >= 0 && x < w && y < h;
}

function cellValue(x, y) {
  return grid[y][x];
}

function key(x, y) { return x + ',' + y; }

const starts = drones.map(d => ({ x: d.x | 0, y: d.y | 0 }));
const n = starts.length;

// Precompute connected components of non-obstacle cells for reachability.
const comp = Array.from({ length: h }, () => Array(w).fill(-1));
const comps = [];
let compId = 0;
const dirs = [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]];
for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
  for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
    if (grid[y][x] < 0 || comp[y][x] !== -1) continue;
    const qx = [x], qy = [y];
    comp[y][x] = compId;
    const cells = [];
    for (let qi = 0; qi < qx.length; qi++) {
      const cx = qx[qi], cy = qy[qi];
      cells.push([cx, cy]);
      for (const [dx, dy] of dirs) {
        const nx = cx + dx, ny = cy + dy;
        if (!inBounds(nx, ny) || grid[ny][nx] < 0 || comp[ny][nx] !== -1) continue;
        comp[ny][nx] = compId;
        qx.push(nx); qy.push(ny);
      }
    }
    comps.push(cells);
    compId++;
  }
}

// BFS from each drone start within its component.
function bfsFrom(sx, sy) {
  const dist = Array.from({ length: h }, () => Array(w).fill(Infinity));
  const prev = Array.from({ length: h }, () => Array(w).fill(null));
  const qx = [sx], qy = [sy];
  dist[sy][sx] = 0;
  for (let qi = 0; qi < qx.length; qi++) {
    const x = qx[qi], y = qy[qi];
    for (const [dx, dy] of dirs) {
      const nx = x + dx, ny = y + dy;
      if (!inBounds(nx, ny) || grid[ny][nx] < 0) continue;
      if (dist[ny][nx] !== Infinity) continue;
      dist[ny][nx] = dist[y][x] + 1;
      prev[ny][nx] = [x, y];
      qx.push(nx); qy.push(ny);
    }
  }
  return { dist, prev };
}

const reach = starts.map(s => bfsFrom(s.x, s.y));

// Build cell list with a simple value heuristic favoring high value and accessibility.
const cells = [];
for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
  for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
    const v = grid[y][x];
    if (v <= 0) continue;
    cells.push({ x, y, v, degree: dirs.reduce((a, [dx, dy]) => a + (inBounds(x + dx, y + dy) && grid[y + dy][x + dx] >= 0 ? 1 : 0), 0) });
  }
}

cells.sort((a, b) => b.v - a.v || a.degree - b.degree);

// Assign each valuable cell to the drone that can reach it cheapest; break ties by current load.
const assigned = Array.from({ length: n }, () => []);
const load = Array(n).fill(0);
for (const c of cells) {
  let best = -1, bestScore = Infinity;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
    const d = reach[i].dist[c.y][c.x];
    if (!Number.isFinite(d)) continue;
    const score = d + load[i] * 0.15 - c.v * 0.001;
    if (score < bestScore) {
      bestScore = score;
      best = i;
    }
  }
  if (best !== -1) {
    assigned[best].push(c);
    load[best] += c.v;
  }
}

function reconstructPath(prev, sx, sy, tx, ty) {
  const path = [];
  let x = tx, y = ty;
  while (!(x === sx && y === sy)) {
    path.push([x, y]);
    const p = prev[y][x];
    if (!p) break;
    [x, y] = p;
  }
  path.push([sx, sy]);
  path.reverse();
  return path;
}

// Create a compact walk for each drone by chaining shortest paths to assigned targets.
const paths = [];
const usedGlobal = new Set();
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  const s = starts[i];
  const order = assigned[i].sort((a, b) => reach[i].dist[a.y][a.x] - reach[i].dist[b.y][b.x] || b.v - a.v);
  let cur = [s.x, s.y];
  let path = [[cur[0], cur[1]]];
  let time = 0;
  const localVisited = new Set([key(cur[0], cur[1])]);
  for (const t of order) {
    if (time >= maxSteps) break;
    const targetPath = reconstructPath(reach[i].prev, cur[0], cur[1], t.x, t.y);
    for (let j = 1; j < targetPath.length && time < maxSteps; j++) {
      const [nx, ny] = targetPath[j];
      path.push([nx, ny]);
      localVisited.add(key(nx, ny));
      cur = [nx, ny];
      time++;
    }
    usedGlobal.add(key(t.x, t.y));
  }

  // If time remains, do a short local sweep around current position to pick up nearby value cells.
  if (time < maxSteps) {
    const q = [[cur[0], cur[1]]];
    const seen = new Set([key(cur[0], cur[1])]);
    const parent = new Map();
    let pick = null;
    while (q.length) {
      const [x, y] = q.shift();
      if (grid[y][x] > 0 && !localVisited.has(key(x, y))) { pick = [x, y]; break; }
      for (const [dx, dy] of dirs) {
        const nx = x + dx, ny = y + dy;
        const k = key(nx, ny);
        if (!inBounds(nx, ny) || grid[ny][nx] < 0 || seen.has(k)) continue;
        seen.add(k);
        parent.set(k, [x, y]);
        q.push([nx, ny]);
      }
    }
    if (pick) {
      const seg = [];
      let x = pick[0], y = pick[1];
      while (!(x === cur[0] && y === cur[1])) {
        seg.push([x, y]);
        [x, y] = parent.get(key(x, y));
      }
      seg.reverse();
      for (const p of seg) {
        if (time >= maxSteps) break;
        path.push(p);
        time++;
      }
    }
  }

  while (path.length < maxSteps + 1) path.push([cur[0], cur[1]]);
  paths.push(path);
}

console.log(JSON.stringify({ paths }));

Test 60: Space Mission Work Schedule (JavaScript) Score: 0.72 / 6
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js JavaScript program for this benchmark task.

Schedule mission-control work across parallel workstations.

The input JSON contains:
- `machineCount`
- `tasks`: objects `{id, duration, deps, deadline, weight}`

A task may start only after every dependency has finished. Return either:
```
{"schedule": [{"id": taskId, "start": integerTime, "machine": machineIndex}, ...]}
```
or a simpler topological order:
```
{"order": [taskId, taskId, ...]}
```
If you return only an order, the grader will place tasks greedily in that order.
The score compares weighted lateness plus makespan against a deterministic list
scheduler reference.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
const fs = require('fs'); const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes
Node.js: v24.14.1

The grader will run the same program on increasingly large scenarios. Use algorithms
that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.
Return the complete runnable JavaScript source code.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought
Schedule dependent mission tasks across parallel workstations with deadlines.		 0.7169
[24 tasks, 3 workstations] cost 510.9, makespan 79; reference 366.3, makespan 86; score 0.717 (run 0.07s)

Space Mission Work Schedule - 24 tasks, 3 workstations

View JavaScript code (5847 chars)
const fs = require('fs');

const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

const machineCount = Math.max(1, data.machineCount | 0);
const tasksInput = Array.isArray(data.tasks) ? data.tasks : [];
const n = tasksInput.length;

if (n === 0) {
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ schedule: [] }));
  process.exit(0);
}

const idToIndex = new Map();
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) idToIndex.set(tasksInput[i].id, i);

const duration = new Array(n);
const deadline = new Array(n);
const weight = new Array(n);
const deps = new Array(n);
const dependents = Array.from({ length: n }, () => []);
const indeg = new Array(n).fill(0);

for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  const t = tasksInput[i];
  duration[i] = Math.max(0, t.duration | 0);
  deadline[i] = Number.isFinite(t.deadline) ? t.deadline : 0;
  weight[i] = Number.isFinite(t.weight) ? t.weight : 0;
  const arr = Array.isArray(t.deps) ? t.deps : [];
  const depIdx = new Array(arr.length);
  for (let j = 0; j < arr.length; j++) {
    const d = idToIndex.get(arr[j]);
    if (d !== undefined) {
      depIdx[j] = d;
      dependents[d].push(i);
      indeg[i]++;
    }
  }
  deps[i] = depIdx;
}

function makeHeap(compare) {
  const a = [];
  return {
    size: () => a.length,
    peek: () => a[0],
    push(v) {
      let i = a.length;
      a.push(v);
      while (i > 0) {
        const p = (i - 1) >> 1;
        if (compare(a[p], v) <= 0) break;
        a[i] = a[p];
        i = p;
      }
      a[i] = v;
    },
    pop() {
      if (a.length === 0) return undefined;
      const top = a[0];
      const v = a.pop();
      if (a.length > 0) {
        let i = 0;
        while (true) {
          let l = i * 2 + 1;
          if (l >= a.length) break;
          let r = l + 1;
          let c = l;
          if (r < a.length && compare(a[r], a[l]) < 0) c = r;
          if (compare(a[c], v) >= 0) break;
          a[i] = a[c];
          i = c;
        }
        a[i] = v;
      }
      return top;
    },
    _raw: a,
  };
}

// Priority heuristic:
// 1) critical-path length remaining
// 2) higher weight
// 3) earlier deadline
// 4) shorter duration
const critical = new Array(n).fill(0);
const topo = [];
const q = [];
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) if (indeg[i] === 0) q.push(i);
for (let qi = 0; qi < q.length; qi++) {
  const u = q[qi];
  topo.push(u);
  for (const v of dependents[u]) {
    if (--indeg[v] === 0) q.push(v);
  }
}

// If there are cycles, fall back to a deterministic order over all tasks.
if (topo.length !== n) {
  const order = tasksInput.map(t => t.id);
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ order }));
  process.exit(0);
}

for (let i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
  const u = topo[i];
  let best = duration[u];
  for (const v of dependents[u]) {
    const cand = duration[u] + critical[v];
    if (cand > best) best = cand;
  }
  critical[u] = best;
}

const indeg2 = new Array(n);
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) indeg2[i] = deps[i].length;

const readyCompare = (a, b) => {
  if (critical[b] !== critical[a]) return critical[b] - critical[a];
  if (weight[b] !== weight[a]) return weight[b] - weight[a];
  if (deadline[a] !== deadline[b]) return deadline[a] - deadline[b];
  if (duration[a] !== duration[b]) return duration[a] - duration[b];
  return a - b;
};

const runningCompare = (a, b) => a.end - b.end || a.machine - b.machine || a.task - b.task;

const ready = makeHeap(readyCompare);
const running = makeHeap(runningCompare);

for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  if (indeg2[i] === 0) ready.push(i);
}

const machineFreeAt = new Array(machineCount).fill(0);
const schedule = new Array(n);
let scheduled = 0;
let now = 0;

function unlockFinished(upToTime) {
  while (running.size() > 0 && running.peek().end <= upToTime) {
    const item = running.pop();
    const u = item.task;
    for (const v of dependents[u]) {
      if (--indeg2[v] === 0) ready.push(v);
    }
  }
}

while (scheduled < n) {
  unlockFinished(now);

  let chosenMachine = -1;
  let earliest = Infinity;
  for (let m = 0; m < machineCount; m++) {
    if (machineFreeAt[m] <= now && machineFreeAt[m] < earliest) {
      earliest = machineFreeAt[m];
      chosenMachine = m;
    }
  }

  if (chosenMachine !== -1 && ready.size() > 0) {
    const task = ready.pop();
    const depFinish = deps[task].reduce((mx, d) => Math.max(mx, schedule[d].start + duration[d]), 0);
    const start = Math.max(now, machineFreeAt[chosenMachine], depFinish);
    const end = start + duration[task];
    schedule[task] = { id: tasksInput[task].id, start, machine: chosenMachine };
    machineFreeAt[chosenMachine] = end;
    running.push({ end, machine: chosenMachine, task });
    scheduled++;
    continue;
  }

  if (ready.size() > 0) {
    let bestMachine = 0;
    let bestStart = Infinity;
    const task = ready.peek();
    for (let m = 0; m < machineCount; m++) {
      const depFinish = deps[task].reduce((mx, d) => Math.max(mx, schedule[d].start + duration[d]), 0);
      const start = Math.max(now, machineFreeAt[m], depFinish);
      if (start < bestStart || (start === bestStart && m < bestMachine)) {
        bestStart = start;
        bestMachine = m;
      }
    }
    const taskId = ready.pop();
    const depFinish = deps[taskId].reduce((mx, d) => Math.max(mx, schedule[d].start + duration[d]), 0);
    const start = Math.max(now, machineFreeAt[bestMachine], depFinish);
    const end = start + duration[taskId];
    schedule[taskId] = { id: tasksInput[taskId].id, start, machine: bestMachine };
    machineFreeAt[bestMachine] = end;
    running.push({ end, machine: bestMachine, task: taskId });
    scheduled++;
    continue;
  }

  const nextRun = running.size() > 0 ? running.peek().end : Infinity;
  const nextFree = Math.min(...machineFreeAt);
  now = Math.min(nextRun, nextFree);
  if (!Number.isFinite(now)) break;
}

process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ schedule }));

Test 61: Delivery Route Planner (TypeScript) Score: 5.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js TypeScript program for this benchmark task.

Plan delivery routes for a fleet of vans.

The input JSON contains:
- `depot`: [x, y]
- `truckCount`: maximum number of vans/routes
- `packages`: objects `{id, x, y}`

Return JSON:
```
{"routes": [[packageId, packageId, ...], ...]}
```
Every package must appear exactly once. Each route starts and ends at the depot.
The score compares your total distance to a deterministic reference planner.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
declare const require: any; const fs = require('fs'); const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

TypeScript compiler: Version 6.0.3

The grader will run the same program on increasingly large scenarios. Use algorithms
that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt

Plan multi-vehicle delivery routes over clustered city coordinates.		 1.0000
[30 packages, 2 vans] distance 2402.1, reference 3027.0, score 1.000 (exact nearest-neighbor grading) (run 0.08s)

Delivery Route Planner - 30 packages, 2 vans

View TypeScript code (3563 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');
const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

type Point = { x: number; y: number; id?: string | number };

const depot: Point = { x: data.depot[0], y: data.depot[1] };
const truckCount: number = Math.max(1, data.truckCount | 0);
const packages: Point[] = Array.isArray(data.packages) ? data.packages.map((p: any) => ({ x: +p.x, y: +p.y, id: p.id })) : [];

function dist2(ax: number, ay: number, bx: number, by: number): number {
  const dx = ax - bx;
  const dy = ay - by;
  return dx * dx + dy * dy;
}

function dist(a: Point, b: Point): number {
  return Math.hypot(a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y);
}

if (packages.length === 0) {
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ routes: [] }));
  process.exit(0);
}

packages.sort((p, q) => {
  const ap = Math.atan2(p.y - depot.y, p.x - depot.x);
  const aq = Math.atan2(q.y - depot.y, q.x - depot.x);
  if (ap !== aq) return ap - aq;
  const rp = dist2(p.x, p.y, depot.x, depot.y);
  const rq = dist2(q.x, q.y, depot.x, depot.y);
  if (rp !== rq) return rp - rq;
  const pid = String(p.id);
  const qid = String(q.id);
  return pid < qid ? -1 : pid > qid ? 1 : 0;
});

const n = packages.length;
const routeCount = Math.min(truckCount, n);
const routes: Point[][] = Array.from({ length: routeCount }, () => []);

// Greedy angular sweep: assign packages in order to the route that can accept
// them with the smallest estimated incremental cost.
const routeLast: Point[] = Array.from({ length: routeCount }, () => depot);
const routeSize = new Array<number>(routeCount).fill(0);

for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  const p = packages[i];
  let bestRoute = 0;
  let bestScore = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;

  for (let r = 0; r < routeCount; r++) {
    const last = routeLast[r];
    const score =
      dist(last, p) +
      dist(p, depot) -
      dist(last, depot) +
      routeSize[r] * 1e-6;
    if (score < bestScore) {
      bestScore = score;
      bestRoute = r;
    }
  }

  routes[bestRoute].push(p);
  routeLast[bestRoute] = p;
  routeSize[bestRoute]++;
}

// Improve each route with a simple 2-opt pass over the sequence generated by
// the sweep assignment. This stays linear-ish in practice because routes are
// typically small compared to the full package set.
function routeLength(seq: Point[]): number {
  if (seq.length === 0) return 0;
  let total = dist(depot, seq[0]);
  for (let i = 1; i < seq.length; i++) total += dist(seq[i - 1], seq[i]);
  total += dist(seq[seq.length - 1], depot);
  return total;
}

function twoOpt(seq: Point[]): Point[] {
  if (seq.length < 4) return seq;
  let improved = true;
  let best = seq.slice();
  let bestLen = routeLength(best);

  while (improved) {
    improved = false;
    for (let i = 0; i < best.length - 2; i++) {
      for (let k = i + 2; k < best.length; k++) {
        const a = i === 0 ? depot : best[i - 1];
        const b = best[i];
        const c = best[k - 1];
        const d = k === best.length ? depot : best[k];
        const delta =
          dist(a, c) + dist(b, d) - dist(a, b) - dist(c, d);
        if (delta < -1e-9) {
          const next = best.slice(0, i).concat(best.slice(i, k).reverse(), best.slice(k));
          best = next;
          bestLen += delta;
          improved = true;
          break;
        }
      }
      if (improved) break;
    }
  }

  return best;
}

const finalRoutes = routes
  .filter(r => r.length > 0)
  .map(r => twoOpt(r).map(p => p.id as string | number));

process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ routes: finalRoutes }));

Test 62: Wildfire Firebreak Planner (TypeScript) Score: 0.00 / 7
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js TypeScript program for this benchmark task.

Choose where to cut firebreaks before a wildfire spreads.

The input JSON contains:
- `width`, `height`
- `grid`: strings where `.` is burnable forest and `~` is water/rock
- `ignitions`: starting fire cells `[x, y]`
- `towns`: objects `{id, x, y, value}`
- `cutBudget`: maximum number of forest cells you may cut

Fire spreads in four directions through burnable cells. Water/rock and your cut
cells block fire. Return JSON:
```
{"firebreaks": [[x, y], [x, y], ...]}
```
The score is the fraction of otherwise-burned town value saved.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
declare const require: any; const fs = require('fs'); const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

TypeScript compiler: Version 6.0.3

The grader will run the same program on increasingly large scenarios. Use algorithms
that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.
Return the complete runnable TypeScript source code.

Choose firebreak cells on a forest map to protect towns from spreading fire.		 0.0000
[32x22 forest, 3 towns] town value burned 27/27, burned cells 657/663, valid cuts 6 (run 0.08s)

Wildfire Firebreak Planner - 32x22 forest, 3 towns

View TypeScript code (3278 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');
const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

type Town = { id: string; x: number; y: number; value: number };

const width: number = data.width | 0;
const height: number = data.height | 0;
const grid: string[] = data.grid;
const ignitions: number[][] = data.ignitions || [];
const towns: Town[] = data.towns || [];
const cutBudget: number = data.cutBudget | 0;

const n = width * height;
const passable = new Uint8Array(n);
for (let y = 0; y < height; y++) {
  const row = grid[y];
  for (let x = 0; x < width; x++) {
    passable[y * width + x] = row.charCodeAt(x) === 46 ? 1 : 0;
  }
}

const INF = 1e9;
const dist = new Int32Array(n);
dist.fill(INF);
const prev = new Int32Array(n);
prev.fill(-1);
const q = new Int32Array(n);
let qh = 0, qt = 0;

function idx(x: number, y: number): number {
  return y * width + x;
}

function push(v: number, d: number, p: number) {
  if (d < dist[v]) {
    dist[v] = d;
    prev[v] = p;
    q[qt++] = v;
  }
}

for (const s of ignitions) {
  const x = s[0] | 0;
  const y = s[1] | 0;
  if (x < 0 || x >= width || y < 0 || y >= height) continue;
  const v = idx(x, y);
  if (!passable[v]) continue;
  push(v, 0, -1);
}

while (qh < qt) {
  const v = q[qh++];
  const d = dist[v];
  const x = v % width;
  const y = (v / width) | 0;
  const nd = d + 1;
  if (x > 0) {
    const u = v - 1;
    if (passable[u]) push(u, nd, v);
  }
  if (x + 1 < width) {
    const u = v + 1;
    if (passable[u]) push(u, nd, v);
  }
  if (y > 0) {
    const u = v - width;
    if (passable[u]) push(u, nd, v);
  }
  if (y + 1 < height) {
    const u = v + width;
    if (passable[u]) push(u, nd, v);
  }
}

const townValue = new Float64Array(n);
for (const t of towns) {
  if (t.x < 0 || t.x >= width || t.y < 0 || t.y >= height) continue;
  const v = idx(t.x, t.y);
  townValue[v] += t.value;
}

const score = new Float64Array(n);
for (let v = 0; v < n; v++) {
  if (!passable[v] || dist[v] >= INF) continue;
  const d = dist[v];
  let s = 0;
  const x = v % width;
  const y = (v / width) | 0;
  if (x > 0) {
    const u = v - 1;
    if (passable[u] && dist[u] === d + 1) s += townValue[u];
  }
  if (x + 1 < width) {
    const u = v + 1;
    if (passable[u] && dist[u] === d + 1) s += townValue[u];
  }
  if (y > 0) {
    const u = v - width;
    if (passable[u] && dist[u] === d + 1) s += townValue[u];
  }
  if (y + 1 < height) {
    const u = v + width;
    if (passable[u] && dist[u] === d + 1) s += townValue[u];
  }
  score[v] = s;
}

const candidates: number[] = [];
for (let v = 0; v < n; v++) {
  if (passable[v] && dist[v] < INF && score[v] > 0) candidates.push(v);
}

candidates.sort((a, b) => score[b] - score[a] || dist[a] - dist[b] || a - b);

const chosen: number[] = [];
const blocked = new Uint8Array(n);
for (const v of candidates) {
  if (chosen.length >= cutBudget) break;
  if (blocked[v]) continue;
  chosen.push(v);
  blocked[v] = 1;
  let u = prev[v];
  while (u !== -1 && !blocked[u]) {
    blocked[u] = 1;
    u = prev[u];
  }
  if (chosen.length >= cutBudget) break;
}

const firebreaks: number[][] = [];
for (const v of chosen) {
  firebreaks.push([v % width, (v / width) | 0]);
}

process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ firebreaks }));

Test 63: Warehouse Robot Job Planner (TypeScript) Score: 1.44 / 6
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js TypeScript program for this benchmark task.

Assign warehouse pick jobs to robots.

The input JSON contains:
- `grid`: strings where `.` is open floor and `#` is blocked shelving
- `robots`: objects `{id, x, y}`
- `jobs`: objects `{id, x, y, priority}`

Return JSON:
```
{"assignments": [[jobId, jobId, ...], ...]}
```
The array index is the robot id. Every job must appear exactly once. The grader
uses shortest grid walking distance through open cells and compares total travel
to a deterministic greedy reference.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
declare const require: any; const fs = require('fs'); const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

Assign warehouse pick jobs to robots moving through aisles and blocked shelves.		 0.3434
[28 jobs, 2 robots] travel 399, reference 137, score 0.343 (exact grid grading) (run 0.09s)

Warehouse Robot Job Planner - 28 jobs, 2 robots

View TypeScript code (4671 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');
const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

type Robot = { id: number; x: number; y: number };
type Job = { id: number; x: number; y: number; priority: number };

const grid: string[] = data.grid ?? [];
const robotsInput: Robot[] = data.robots ?? [];
const jobsInput: Job[] = data.jobs ?? [];

const height = grid.length;
const width = height > 0 ? grid[0].length : 0;
const passable = (x: number, y: number): boolean =>
  y >= 0 && y < height && x >= 0 && x < width && grid[y][x] === '.';

const robotCount = robotsInput.length;
const assignments: number[][] = Array.from({ length: robotCount }, () => []);

if (jobsInput.length === 0 || robotCount === 0) {
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ assignments }));
  process.exit(0);
}

const robots = robotsInput.map((r, index) => ({ ...r, index }));
const jobs = jobsInput.map((j, index) => ({ ...j, index }));

function bfsFrom(startX: number, startY: number): Int32Array {
  const dist = new Int32Array(width * height);
  dist.fill(-1);
  if (!passable(startX, startY)) return dist;
  const queueX = new Int32Array(width * height);
  const queueY = new Int32Array(width * height);
  let head = 0;
  let tail = 0;
  const startIdx = startY * width + startX;
  dist[startIdx] = 0;
  queueX[tail] = startX;
  queueY[tail] = startY;
  tail++;
  while (head < tail) {
    const x = queueX[head];
    const y = queueY[head];
    head++;
    const base = dist[y * width + x] + 1;
    if (x > 0) {
      const nx = x - 1;
      const ny = y;
      const idx = ny * width + nx;
      if (grid[ny][nx] === '.' && dist[idx] === -1) {
        dist[idx] = base;
        queueX[tail] = nx;
        queueY[tail] = ny;
        tail++;
      }
    }
    if (x + 1 < width) {
      const nx = x + 1;
      const ny = y;
      const idx = ny * width + nx;
      if (grid[ny][nx] === '.' && dist[idx] === -1) {
        dist[idx] = base;
        queueX[tail] = nx;
        queueY[tail] = ny;
        tail++;
      }
    }
    if (y > 0) {
      const nx = x;
      const ny = y - 1;
      const idx = ny * width + nx;
      if (grid[ny][nx] === '.' && dist[idx] === -1) {
        dist[idx] = base;
        queueX[tail] = nx;
        queueY[tail] = ny;
        tail++;
      }
    }
    if (y + 1 < height) {
      const nx = x;
      const ny = y + 1;
      const idx = ny * width + nx;
      if (grid[ny][nx] === '.' && dist[idx] === -1) {
        dist[idx] = base;
        queueX[tail] = nx;
        queueY[tail] = ny;
        tail++;
      }
    }
  }
  return dist;
}

const robotDistances: Int32Array[] = robots.map(r => bfsFrom(r.x, r.y));

type Candidate = {
  robotIndex: number;
  jobIndex: number;
  dist: number;
  score: number;
};

const candidates: Candidate[] = [];
for (let ri = 0; ri < robots.length; ri++) {
  const dist = robotDistances[ri];
  for (let ji = 0; ji < jobs.length; ji++) {
    const job = jobs[ji];
    if (!passable(job.x, job.y)) continue;
    const d = dist[job.y * width + job.x];
    if (d >= 0) {
      candidates.push({
        robotIndex: ri,
        jobIndex: ji,
        dist: d,
        score: d - job.priority * 1000000,
      });
    }
  }
}

candidates.sort((a, b) =>
  a.score !== b.score ? a.score - b.score :
  a.dist !== b.dist ? a.dist - b.dist :
  jobs[a.jobIndex].priority !== jobs[b.jobIndex].priority ? jobs[b.jobIndex].priority - jobs[a.jobIndex].priority :
  robots[a.robotIndex].id - robots[b.robotIndex].id ||
  jobs[a.jobIndex].id - jobs[b.jobIndex].id
);

const assignedJob = new Array<boolean>(jobs.length).fill(false);
const robotLoad = new Int32Array(robots.length);

for (const cand of candidates) {
  if (!assignedJob[cand.jobIndex]) {
    assignedJob[cand.jobIndex] = true;
    assignments[cand.robotIndex].push(jobs[cand.jobIndex].id);
    robotLoad[cand.robotIndex]++;
  }
}

const unassigned: number[] = [];
for (let ji = 0; ji < jobs.length; ji++) {
  if (!assignedJob[ji]) unassigned.push(ji);
}

if (unassigned.length > 0) {
  const robotsByLoad = robots.map((_, i) => i).sort((a, b) => robotLoad[a] - robotLoad[b] || robots[a].id - robots[b].id);
  for (const ji of unassigned) {
    let bestRobot = robotsByLoad[0];
    let bestDist = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
    const job = jobs[ji];
    for (const ri of robotsByLoad) {
      const d = robotDistances[ri][job.y * width + job.x];
      if (d >= 0 && d < bestDist) {
        bestDist = d;
        bestRobot = ri;
      }
    }
    assignments[bestRobot].push(job.id);
    robotLoad[bestRobot]++;
  }
}

for (const arr of assignments) arr.sort((a, b) => a - b);

process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ assignments }));

Test 64: Drone Search Coverage Planner (TypeScript) Score: 4.67 / 6
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js TypeScript program for this benchmark task.

Plan grid paths for search drones.

The input JSON contains:
- `probability`: 2D array; -1 is an obstacle, positive values are search value
- `drones`: starting cells `{id, x, y}`
- `maxStepsPerDrone`

Return JSON:
```
{"paths": [[[x,y], [x,y], ...], ...]}
```
Each path belongs to the drone with the same index. Moves must be up/down/left/right
or stay in place, must stay inside the grid, and must avoid obstacles. The score is
unique probability covered compared with a greedy reference search.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
declare const require: any; const fs = require('fs'); const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

TypeScript compiler: Version 6.0.3

that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.
Return the complete runnable TypeScript source code.

Route search drones over a probability heatmap with obstacles and battery limits.		 0.5737
[2 drones, 55 steps] coverage 8303, reference 14473, score 0.574 (run 0.10s)

Drone Search Coverage Planner - 2 drones, 55 steps

View TypeScript code (5899 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');

type Cell = { x: number; y: number };
type Drone = { id: string | number; x: number; y: number };

const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

const grid: number[][] = data.probability || [];
const drones: Drone[] = data.drones || [];
const maxSteps: number = Math.max(0, data.maxStepsPerDrone | 0);

const h = grid.length;
const w = h > 0 ? grid[0].length : 0;

function inBounds(x: number, y: number): boolean {
  return x >= 0 && y >= 0 && x < w && y < h;
}

function isOpen(x: number, y: number): boolean {
  return inBounds(x, y) && grid[y][x] !== -1;
}

const dirs = [
  [0, 0],
  [1, 0],
  [-1, 0],
  [0, 1],
  [0, -1],
];

const targets: Cell[] = [];
for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
  for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
    if (grid[y][x] > 0) targets.push({ x, y });
  }
}

targets.sort((a, b) => {
  const va = grid[a.y][a.x];
  const vb = grid[b.y][b.x];
  if (vb !== va) return vb - va;
  const da = a.x + a.y;
  const db = b.x + b.y;
  if (da !== db) return da - db;
  if (a.y !== b.y) return a.y - b.y;
  return a.x - b.x;
});

function bfsFrom(startX: number, startY: number): number[][] {
  const dist: number[][] = Array.from({ length: h }, () => Array(w).fill(-1));
  if (!isOpen(startX, startY)) return dist;
  const qx = new Int32Array(w * h);
  const qy = new Int32Array(w * h);
  let head = 0;
  let tail = 0;
  dist[startY][startX] = 0;
  qx[tail] = startX;
  qy[tail] = startY;
  tail++;
  while (head < tail) {
    const x = qx[head];
    const y = qy[head];
    head++;
    const nd = dist[y][x] + 1;
    for (let k = 1; k < 5; k++) {
      const nx = x + dirs[k][0];
      const ny = y + dirs[k][1];
      if (!isOpen(nx, ny) || dist[ny][nx] !== -1) continue;
      dist[ny][nx] = nd;
      qx[tail] = nx;
      qy[tail] = ny;
      tail++;
    }
  }
  return dist;
}

const droneCount = drones.length;
const droneDist: number[][][] = [];
for (let i = 0; i < droneCount; i++) {
  droneDist.push(bfsFrom(drones[i].x, drones[i].y));
}

const owner: number[][] = Array.from({ length: h }, () => Array(w).fill(-1));
const bestScore: number[][] = Array.from({ length: h }, () => Array(w).fill(Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY));

for (let ti = 0; ti < targets.length; ti++) {
  const t = targets[ti];
  let bestDrone = -1;
  let bestMetric = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
  for (let i = 0; i < droneCount; i++) {
    const d = droneDist[i][t.y][t.x];
    if (d < 0) continue;
    const metric = d * 1000 - grid[t.y][t.x] * 10 + i;
    if (metric < bestMetric) {
      bestMetric = metric;
      bestDrone = i;
    }
  }
  if (bestDrone >= 0) {
    owner[t.y][t.x] = bestDrone;
    bestScore[t.y][t.x] = bestMetric;
  }
}

function reconstructPath(dist: number[][], startX: number, startY: number, goalX: number, goalY: number): Cell[] | null {
  if (!isOpen(goalX, goalY) || dist[goalY][goalX] < 0) return null;
  const path: Cell[] = [];
  let x = goalX;
  let y = goalY;
  while (!(x === startX && y === startY)) {
    path.push({ x, y });
    const d = dist[y][x];
    let moved = false;
    for (let k = 1; k < 5; k++) {
      const px = x + dirs[k][0];
      const py = y + dirs[k][1];
      if (inBounds(px, py) && dist[py][px] === d - 1) {
        x = px;
        y = py;
        moved = true;
        break;
      }
    }
    if (!moved) return null;
  }
  path.push({ x: startX, y: startY });
  path.reverse();
  return path;
}

function scoreCell(x: number, y: number, visited: Set<number>, droneIndex: number, curX: number, curY: number): number {
  if (!isOpen(x, y) || visited.has(y * w + x)) return -1e18;
  const value = grid[y][x];
  const dist = Math.abs(curX - x) + Math.abs(curY - y);
  const ownership = owner[y][x] === droneIndex ? 25 : 0;
  return value * 1000 + ownership - dist * 8 - (bestScore[y][x] === Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY ? 0 : bestScore[y][x] * 0.01);
}

const paths: number[][][] = [];
const cellIndices: number[] = [];
for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
  for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
    if (grid[y][x] > 0) cellIndices.push(y * w + x);
  }
}

for (let i = 0; i < droneCount; i++) {
  const start = drones[i];
  const visited = new Set<number>();
  const path: number[][] = [[start.x, start.y]];
  visited.add(start.y * w + start.x);
  let curX = start.x;
  let curY = start.y;
  let steps = 0;

  while (steps < maxSteps) {
    let bestTarget: Cell | null = null;
    let bestTargetScore = -1e18;

    const localCandidates: Cell[] = [];
    for (let c = 0; c < cellIndices.length; c++) {
      const idx = cellIndices[c];
      if (visited.has(idx)) continue;
      const x = idx % w;
      const y = (idx / w) | 0;
      if (owner[y][x] !== -1 && owner[y][x] !== i) continue;
      const d = Math.abs(curX - x) + Math.abs(curY - y);
      if (d > maxSteps - steps + 2) continue;
      localCandidates.push({ x, y });
    }

    if (localCandidates.length === 0) break;

    for (let c = 0; c < localCandidates.length; c++) {
      const t = localCandidates[c];
      const s = scoreCell(t.x, t.y, visited, i, curX, curY);
      if (s > bestTargetScore) {
        bestTargetScore = s;
        bestTarget = t;
      }
    }

    if (!bestTarget) break;

    const dist = bfsFrom(curX, curY);
    const route = reconstructPath(dist, curX, curY, bestTarget.x, bestTarget.y);
    if (!route || route.length <= 1) {
      visited.add(bestTarget.y * w + bestTarget.x);
      continue;
    }

    for (let r = 1; r < route.length && steps < maxSteps; r++) {
      const p = route[r];
      if (!isOpen(p.x, p.y)) break;
      path.push([p.x, p.y]);
      steps++;
      curX = p.x;
      curY = p.y;
      visited.add(curY * w + curX);
    }

    if (steps >= maxSteps) break;
    visited.add(bestTarget.y * w + bestTarget.x);
  }

  while (path.length < maxSteps + 1) {
    path.push([curX, curY]);
  }
  paths.push(path);
}

process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ paths }));

Test 65: Space Mission Work Schedule (TypeScript) Score: 4.70 / 6
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js TypeScript program for this benchmark task.

Schedule mission-control work across parallel workstations.

The input JSON contains:
- `machineCount`
- `tasks`: objects `{id, duration, deps, deadline, weight}`

A task may start only after every dependency has finished. Return either:
```
{"schedule": [{"id": taskId, "start": integerTime, "machine": machineIndex}, ...]}
```
or a simpler topological order:
```
{"order": [taskId, taskId, ...]}
```
If you return only an order, the grader will place tasks greedily in that order.
The score compares weighted lateness plus makespan against a deterministic list
scheduler reference.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
declare const require: any; const fs = require('fs'); const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes
Node.js: v24.14.1
TypeScript compiler: Version 6.0.3

The grader will run the same program on increasingly large scenarios. Use algorithms
that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.
Return the complete runnable TypeScript source code.
 Prompt Changes:

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought
Schedule dependent mission tasks across parallel workstations with deadlines.		 0.5590
[24 tasks, 3 workstations] cost 655.3, makespan 86; reference 366.3, makespan 86; score 0.559 (run 0.07s)

Space Mission Work Schedule - 24 tasks, 3 workstations

View TypeScript code (4662 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');
const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

type Task = {
  id: string | number;
  duration: number;
  deps: Array<string | number>;
  deadline?: number;
  weight?: number;
};

type ParsedTask = {
  id: string;
  idx: number;
  duration: number;
  deps: number[];
  deadline: number;
  weight: number;
  out: number[];
  indeg: number;
  rank: number;
};

const machineCount = Math.max(1, Number(data.machineCount) | 0);
const tasks: Task[] = Array.isArray(data.tasks) ? data.tasks : [];
const n = tasks.length;

const idToIndex = new Map<string, number>();
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) idToIndex.set(String(tasks[i].id), i);

const parsed: ParsedTask[] = tasks.map((t, idx) => ({
  id: String(t.id),
  idx,
  duration: Math.max(0, Number(t.duration) || 0),
  deps: [],
  deadline: Number.isFinite(Number(t.deadline)) ? Number(t.deadline) : 0,
  weight: Number.isFinite(Number(t.weight)) ? Number(t.weight) : 1,
  out: [],
  indeg: 0,
  rank: 0,
}));

for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  const deps = Array.isArray(tasks[i].deps) ? tasks[i].deps : [];
  for (const dep of deps) {
    const j = idToIndex.get(String(dep));
    if (j !== undefined && j !== i) parsed[i].deps.push(j);
  }
}

for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  for (const d of parsed[i].deps) {
    parsed[d].out.push(i);
    parsed[i].indeg++;
  }
}

const indeg0 = parsed.map(t => t.indeg);
const q: number[] = [];
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) if (indeg0[i] === 0) q.push(i);
const topo: number[] = [];
for (let head = 0; head < q.length; head++) {
  const u = q[head];
  topo.push(u);
  for (const v of parsed[u].out) {
    indeg0[v]--;
    if (indeg0[v] === 0) q.push(v);
  }
}

if (topo.length !== n) {
  const order = parsed.map(t => t.id);
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ order }));
  process.exit(0);
}

for (let i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
  const u = topo[i];
  let best = 0;
  for (const v of parsed[u].out) {
    const cand = parsed[v].rank + parsed[v].duration;
    if (cand > best) best = cand;
  }
  const slack = Math.max(0, parsed[u].deadline);
  const urgency = parsed[u].weight / (1 + Math.max(0, parsed[u].deadline - best - parsed[u].duration));
  parsed[u].rank = best + parsed[u].duration + urgency * 0.001 + parsed[u].weight * 1e-9 - slack * 1e-12;
}

const ready: number[] = [];
const remainingDeps = parsed.map(t => t.indeg);
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) if (remainingDeps[i] === 0) ready.push(i);

const machineFree: number[] = new Array(machineCount).fill(0);
const finishTime: number[] = new Array(n).fill(0);
const startTime: number[] = new Array(n).fill(0);
const assignedMachine: number[] = new Array(n).fill(0);
let completed = 0;

function pickReady(): number {
  let bestIdx = 0;
  for (let i = 1; i < ready.length; i++) {
    const a = parsed[ready[i]];
    const b = parsed[ready[bestIdx]];
    if (
      a.rank > b.rank ||
      (a.rank === b.rank && a.weight > b.weight) ||
      (a.rank === b.rank && a.weight === b.weight && a.deadline < b.deadline) ||
      (a.rank === b.rank && a.weight === b.weight && a.deadline === b.deadline && a.duration > b.duration) ||
      (a.rank === b.rank && a.weight === b.weight && a.deadline === b.deadline && a.duration === b.duration && a.idx < b.idx)
    ) bestIdx = i;
  }
  const task = ready[bestIdx];
  ready[bestIdx] = ready[ready.length - 1];
  ready.pop();
  return task;
}

while (completed < n) {
  if (ready.length === 0) {
    const order = topo.map(i => parsed[i].id);
    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ order }));
    process.exit(0);
  }

  const taskIdx = pickReady();
  const task = parsed[taskIdx];
  let bestMachine = 0;
  let bestStart = Math.max(machineFree[0], ...task.deps.map(d => finishTime[d]));
  let bestFinish = bestStart + task.duration;

  for (let m = 1; m < machineCount; m++) {
    const s = Math.max(machineFree[m], ...task.deps.map(d => finishTime[d]));
    const f = s + task.duration;
    if (
      f < bestFinish ||
      (f === bestFinish && s < bestStart) ||
      (f === bestFinish && s === bestStart && machineFree[m] < machineFree[bestMachine])
    ) {
      bestMachine = m;
      bestStart = s;
      bestFinish = f;
    }
  }

  startTime[taskIdx] = bestStart;
  finishTime[taskIdx] = bestFinish;
  assignedMachine[taskIdx] = bestMachine;
  machineFree[bestMachine] = bestFinish;
  completed++;

  for (const v of task.out) {
    remainingDeps[v]--;
    if (remainingDeps[v] === 0) ready.push(v);
  }
}

const schedule = topo.map(i => ({
  id: parsed[i].id,
  start: startTime[i] | 0,
  machine: assignedMachine[i] | 0,
}));

process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ schedule }));
